runners.owner: A Pazman, F Alam Et Al

quotes.race: Ranvet Stakes (Group 1) (3yo+) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Al Mubhir just did too much on the way around. He just never let go - Tom Marquand, jockey.

quotes.race: Visit racingtv.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Everything went really smoothly and the soft ground was always going to help coming back to a mile. Al Mubhir\ relaxed well and surprised me how well he did it in the end. He's clearly a talented horse and if we keep getting things right, he's got a big future - Tom Marquand, jockey.

quotes.race: MansionBet Beaten By A Head Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Al Mubhir did that nicely. The jockey liked him but said he knew nothing so we might have to run him again but he was a bit spicy after the race so we'll have to see - William Haggas, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

quotes.race: Spreadex All-Weather Mile Championships Conditions Stakes
quotes.quote: Berkshire Shadow was brilliant at two and then lost his way. He bounced back in a Listed race last time and today I felt over a stiff mile we were going to learn for the future. He definitely stayed. He travelled super well - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

quotes.race: Coventry Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: Berkshire Shadow is a special horse. He's a star. That's an incredible performance for a second start. He's got to have a big future. He's big and immature to look at but he has a very big future ahead of him after what he's just done at Royal Ascot. One step at a time but his dam stayed a mile - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

quotes.race: Highclere Thoroughbred Racing EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Berkshire Shadow has given them a head start, but he's come into the race nicely and I'm really pleased with the way he's picked up. He should stay further and looks a decent type. It'd be lovely to think about Royal Ascot, but he'd have another run before that. I think Oisin was quite impressed with him, but you never know what you're taking on. I would have thought we'd go to six furlongs next time, but we'll discuss all that - Andrew Balding, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He is a lovely prospect who made a fine start when successful on his debut in a 5f Newbury maiden. He is certainly one of the more exciting youngsters at this time and is on course for the Coventry Stakes. He learned a lot from his first run and is quite smart, not just an early two-year-old with a good pedigree, but one with plenty of scope. He will not be just an Ascot juvenile. 19-05-21

racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Hardy's Hero, 4/1 French Affair, 13/2 Shaman Champion, 15/2 Sovereign View, 10/1 Gorey Gold,  Victorious One,  Senorita Vega, 12/1 Amazing Journey, 14/1 Eyes Front, 16/1 John Barleycorn, 20/1 Robert Anstruther

racecards.race_name: Ladbrokes "Free Bet At Ayr" Fillies' Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Impressive on debut; underwhelming since but retains potential now tackling a nursery
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns breeze-up buy who impressed on debut at Yarmouth (7f, good) in July; underwhelming favourite in Salisbury novice (1m, good to firm) since but may have been feeling the ground (hung left); her very useful half-brother Nautical Force won on soft ground; has Group-race entries and retains potential for trainer who won this last year; possible player on nursery debut.

quotes.race: AKS Skips Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Radiance was quite green early doors and we wanted to get a lead, but it was over to Ryan [Moore] really and she's a filly that's shown plenty. It's nice to get the job done. She's still very raw and Highclere have got another nice filly I hope. She looks likely to be a black-type filly - George Boughey, trainer.

runners.owner: D J Mackay & West Shaw Farm
runners.comment: Carlisle maiden winner; begins handicap life on a fair mark and could continue to improve
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of her debut second (5f, good) to win at Carlisle in July (7f, good to soft), and she was third of five in a valuable novice at Goodwood (1m, good to firm) last time; begins handicap life on a fair mark and could continue to improve for her top northern trainer.

runners.comment: Two wins from her three nursery starts and could take latest 7lb rise in her stride
runners.spotlight: Two wins from her three nursery starts, the latest at Catterick (7f, soft) in July when coming clear with one who has franked the form since; did it cosily last time and could take a 7lb rise in her stride.

runners.comment: Kept on well for 6f nursery win at Carlisle recently and respected under 6lb penalty
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to make all by half a length at Carlisle (6f, soft) ten days ago on her second nursery start, beating one who has posted wins either side; kept on well on that occasion and her dam was a 7f winner, both of which bode well for this step up in trip; respected under 6lb penalty.

runners.comment: Kept on well for 3rd in 7f AW novice; today's ground will provide stiffer test of stamina
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in novice/maiden races on her last three starts, most recently keeping on well for third when upped to 7f at Lingfield (AW); today's slow ground will provide a stiffer test of stamina and that may well be to her liking; not ruled out on nursery debut.

runners.owner: Mps Racing Ltd, Roger Thompson & Partner

prev_owners.owner: Mps Racing Ltd, Roger Thompson & Partner
Mps Racing Ltd, Roger Thompson & Partner

runners.comment: Went close in Kempton nursery in July but three lesser runs have followed
runners.spotlight: Won Lingfield maiden (7f, good) in May and went close in Kempton nursery (7f, AW) in July; however, three lesser runs have followed.

runners.comment: Third in two maidens last month but down the field at 18-1 on last week's nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Third at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last month on second start then filled the same position at Ripon (6f, good); however, she was down the field on her nursery debut at Doncaster (6.5f, good to soft; 18-1) last Thursday.

racecards.verdict: The William Haggas-trained MAGIC BOX is progressing nicely and is taken to make it three out of four in nurseries.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Magic Box, 11/4 Radiance, 5/1 Aspiral, 11/2 Magistery, 7/1 Pretty Danielle, 20/1 Bearin Up, 25/1 Paroda Diva

racecards.race_name: Get Home Safe With Thistle Cabs EBF Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Well-beaten sixth in Listed/Group 2 races but impressive in soft-ground novice previously
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Nottingham (6f, good) in June then easily made all in Carlisle novice (6f, soft); well-beaten sixth in Listed/Group 2 races on next two starts (7f/6f, good/good to firm) and needs to get back on track, but the slow ground could be a positive in view of his impressive Carlisle win.

quotes.race: Tattersalls £40,000 EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Reciprocated is turning out to be a really nice horse. We'll definitely look at stakes races now. He had a penalty and he did it really easily. He's got plenty of size and scope about him, he'll improve again for that run and the further he goes, the better he'll get - James Cowley, assistant to Karl Burke.

runners.comment: Has made the frame in Listed races and could make an impact on nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Won by 6l at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in June on second start and has made the frame in 6f Listed races at Newbury and Ripon on his last two starts; could make an impact on nursery debut if coping with the slow ground.

runners.comment: Disappointing favourite on nursery debut at York but trainer has excellent record in this
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite on nursery debut at York but he'd shown considerable promise previously and is well worth a second look given that his trainer has won three of the last five runnings; unraced on slow ground.

runners.comment: Won Glorious Goodwood nursery then third in sales race at York; he's one to consider
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-7 when suited by the step up to 6f and winning on nursery debut at Glorious Goodwood (good to soft; unraced on softer), and another big run when third of 21 in a sales race at York (6f, good to firm) last month; there could still be mileage in his mark and he's one to consider.

runners.comment: Has won in good style on last two starts and he's firmly in calculations on nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of his debut fourth (6f, good) to win over this C&D (good) last month, and he followed up in a novice at Redcar (6f, good to firm); he's done it in good style on those last two starts and his pedigree suggests slow ground will suit; firmly in calculations on nursery debut.

runners.comment: Off since finishing last at Royal Ascot but may nevertheless have potential off this mark
runners.spotlight: Won at Ripon (5f, good) in June on third run then finished last when 66-1 at Royal Ascot; absent since and unraced on slow ground and beyond 5f, but has shown enough to hint he may have potential off this opening mark.

stable_tour.quote: He probably had a run too many going into Royal Ascot after that and we backed off him as he just weakened on me. He won at Ripon and we went for the Norfolk and had a good day out. The form is in the book and I'm sure he'll bounce back. He keeps growing and is quite a tall horse and we won't overrun him between now and the end of the season. There's a Listed race back at Ripon at the end of August that he might go for and there are nurseries at places like Ayr and Doncaster. We'll try to get him to win again and put him away. He'll be a nice horse for next year. 07-08-25

runners.comment: Won Hamilton maiden this month and he could continue to progress; up 6lb
runners.spotlight: Won Hamilton maiden (6f, good to soft) last month on fourth start and returned to form when back on slow ground in a nursery at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) this month, prevailing by just over 1l; could continue to progress and he's a possible player up 6lb for last time.

runners.comment: Won Ffos Las nursery last month but failed to make an impact at Doncaster last week
runners.spotlight: Newmarket trainer won this last year; short-headed at Windsor (6f, good) in July on second nursery start then went one better at Ffos Las (6f, good to soft); however, he failed to make an impact at Doncaster (6f, good) only last Friday and has to leave that run behind.

runners.comment: Some good nursery form at Chester but exposed and probably vulnerable now back up in grade
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt on nursery debut at Chester (7f, good to firm) last month, and after finishing down the field at York he was a close third of six at Chester (7f, good) this month; however, this exposed colt is probably vulnerable now back up in grade; unraced on slow ground.

racecards.verdict: Having won with something to spare over C&D and at Redcar, BYE LAW is taken to complete a hat-trick on this nursery debut.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Reciprocated, 9/2 Ardisia, 11/2 Bye Law,  Go Vince Go, 6/1 Amorim,  Utmost Respect, 12/1 Star Material, 14/1 Born Slippy, 16/1 Ubetterseethis

racecards.race_name: British EBF Stallions Harry Rosebery Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.comment: 66-1 runner-up in sales race at York two starts ago and he could be involved
runners.spotlight: Displayed improved form when runner-up at 66-1 in sales race at York (6f, good to firm) last month then easily got off the mark at the sixth attempt in a recent maiden at Hamilton (5f, good to soft); could be involved.

runners.comment: Has gone close in his two nurseries but has something to find now up in grade
runners.spotlight: Pontefract maiden winner who went close last month in his first two nurseries (6f/5f), on the first occasion in a competitive race at York's Ebor meeting; however, he has something to find now up in grade.

quotes.race: British EBF Stallions Harry Rosebery Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Chairmanfourtimes was good. He's a proper five-furlong horse, even though he was fairly flat out early on. He stayed the five really well. I don't know how many more runs I'll give him but he'll be a proper horse for next year. He's a big, scopey horse and takes everything you give to him - Adrian Keatley, trainer.

quotes.race: British EBF Stallion Studs Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We were probably running Chairmanfourtimes on the wrong ground and over the wrong trip the last couple of times. He appreciated the quicker surface and the drop back to five furlongs. He did it well. I have the Julia Graves Roses Stakes at York in mind for him now, that's where he'll go next - Adrian Keatley, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He looks to be a proper 5f horse and he's been a bit unlucky as we probably ran him once too many over 6f. He won a 5f maiden at Pontefract last week and that's the perfect trip for him. The Listed race at York is a possibility now - as are nurseries. 07-08-25

runners.comment: Thirsk maiden winner; had an excuse at York but others have more substance to their claims
runners.spotlight: The form of his maiden win at Thirsk last month has worked out well (5f, good to firm) and he raced away from the main action when mid-division in sales race at York (6f, good to firm) last time; however, others have more substance to their claims; below best on good to soft ground in July.

prev_owners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.comment: 5f Listed winner who was outstayed when fifth in 6f Group 3 at the Curragh last time
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-4 with Listed win at the Curragh (5f, good) last month, for which she carries a 3lb penalty today; outstayed when fifth in 6f Group 3 at the Curragh (good to yielding) last time; key player now back at 5f.

quotes.race: Mitsubishi Electric Curragh Irish EBF Curragh Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: I knew Ipanema Queen gets 6f so I was happy to make it a test. I felt like she might empty but when I really got stuck into her she was good and tough. She got tired late on but got away with it. She's very fast and feels like a filly who will appreciate easier ground. She has a good attitude - Dylan Browne McMonagle, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: She's a nice filly. Ipanema Queen was ready to run early on but then she got a little bit weak and we gave her a break. She was well coming here but I'd say she was only 75% today and there should be more to come. We have her entered for the Group 2 here on Derby weekend. We wanted to run today because if she did well we would try and run her in the Albany at Ascot. That's the plan - assistant trainer Robson Aguiar.

runners.comment: Won Musselburgh nursery last Saturday but very much up against it upped markedly in grade
runners.spotlight: Won Beverley maiden (5f, good to firm) last month on seventh start and added a nursery win at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) last Saturday; however, she looks very much up against it now upped markedly in grade.

runners.comment: Close Listed fifth at Newbury last month and might not be far away for bang-in-form yard
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 when winning Thirsk novice (5f, good to firm) last month before close Listed fifth at Newbury (5f, good) seven days later, when she made the running; unraced on slow ground; her trainer is in tremendous form and she might not be far away.

quotes.race: Bob Vickery Memorial Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Daneh Of Dandy is a nice filly. Things just didn't go her way in the Group 3 at Ascot last time out. Maybe it was a little bit soon for her and maybe five furlongs is more her bag based on that, she looked quite quick didn't she? The St Hugh's Stakes at Newbury is a possibility and failing that we could go even further north for the five-furlong Listed race at Ayr - James Tate, trainers.

runners.comment: Something to find today but she's been progressive in nurseries and is not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Has improved for this tongue-tie, making it two wins in a row when making all on nursery debut at Ripon (5f, good to firm) before close second to Temple Of Athena in Chester nursery (5f, good to soft) last Saturday; something to find today but she had to work hard to lead from a wide stall at Chester and is now 2lb better off with the winner; progressive and not ruled out.

runners.comment: Made it 2-4 when winning nursery at Sandown; not discounted now back up in grade
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in June and was back on song when winning a nursery at Sandown (5f, soft) three weeks ago; further improvement needed now back up in grade but that's possible; not discounted.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Newcomers' Maiden Stakes (Horses Which Have Never Run) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Lebron Power found the ground a little bit quick but she's sharp, she's genuine and she's straightforward. She picked up nicely and hopefully she keeps on improving - Sean Levey, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.owner: Mrs J Woods R P Gallagher & Partner

prev_owners.owner: Mrs J Woods R P Gallagher & Partner

runners.comment: Fifth at York last time in a much hotter Listed race than this; could be thereabouts
runners.spotlight: Close Listed fourth at Newbury last month (5f, good); beaten further when Listed fifth at York (5f, good to firm) eight days later but that form was strongly boosted in the Flying Childers; this will take less winning and she could be thereabouts for last year's winning yard; unraced on slow ground.

runners.comment: Only seventh in the Lowther but the drop back in grade can help, as might the return to 5f
runners.spotlight: Impressive on debut at Beverley (5f, good to firm) in July and runner-up in Ripon novice (6f, good) next time, before finishing seventh in the Group 2 Lowther at York (6f, good to firm); improvement is needed but the drop back in grade can help, as might the return to 5f; not discounted.

quotes.race: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: We'll see how Pearl Fortune comes out of this and I'll have a word with her owners too. We made the Lowther entry a little while ago, so we thought highly enough of her to do that and hopefully her work will tell us. It's a little bit tight to get another run in before then, but it's not impossible - Karl Burke, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Yet to win from 5 attempts but she's been running well in classy company; leading claims
runners.spotlight: Yet to win from five attempts but she's been running well in some classy company, most recently third in a Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, very soft); leading claims in first-time cheekpieces.

runners.comment: Last in the Nunthorpe but strong form previously and could play a major role
runners.spotlight: Beat subsequent Listed/Group 2 winner Revival Power to win valuable fillies' conditions race at Goodwood (5f, good) in July, then faced tough task against hardened sprinters when last in the Group 1 Nunthorpe; could bounce back and play a major role now back against 2yos; unraced on slow ground.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Alice Keppel Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: I've been a little bit frustrated with Spicy Marg but, with hindsight, she's had legitimate excuses. She was impressive first time out and her homework has always been exceptional so I really fancied her going into the Queen Mary. She came into season the morning of [the race] so we had to put a line through that and when I rode her at Newmarket over six [furlongs] last time she pinged the gates, did too much early and didn't stay. The set up for her today was ideal and we saw the filly I hoped she was - Kieran Shoemark, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Tattersalls £40,000 EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Spicy Marg did a nice piece of work ten days ago when she quickened up very nicely so we were hopeful coming here today. I trained her dam and when I saw this filly at the sales I was never going to be beaten in buying her. She'll go for the Marygate next - Michael Bell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's one of the top-rated two-year-old fillies in Britain. After she won at Newmarket in early May everything was working backwards from Royal Ascot and the Queen Mary. We ducked a couple of options at York and Carlisle with a view that we're saving our powder. We think she's very smart. With the ground, I wouldn't mind it fast but if it was very soft that would be an unknown. We really like her and she's my trainer's pick. 09-06-25

runners.comment: Others have stronger form but she's 2-2 on slow ground; could be in with an each-way shout
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Beverley (5f, soft) in June and returned to winning ways in nursery at Chester (5f, good to soft) last Saturday, which was her fifth start; others have stronger form but she's 2-2 on slow ground and could be in with an each-way shout.

racecards.verdict: The maiden SHINE ON ME has been handed some tough tasks among her five starts and this is a good chance for her to get off the mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Shine On Me, 7/2 Ipanema Queen, 6/1 Boston Dan, 8/1 Daneh Of Dandy, 9/1 Lebron Power,  Our Cody, 14/1 Pearl Fortune, 20/1 Chairmanfourtimes,  Rikki Tiki Tavi,  Hanney Girl,  Temple Of Athena, 40/1 Arduis Invicta

racecards.race_name: Arran Scottish Sprint EBF Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.comment: Well beaten in May on sole run this year but 2-2 here (both Listed); key player if at best
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Newmarket in May on sole run this year but she's 2-2 here, both in 5f Listed races (good to firm/good to soft) and the latest last June; key player if back to her best following another break.

quotes.race: Arran Scottish Sprint EBF Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: The ground at Newmarket jarred Beautiful Diamond up very badly and we've had to take our time with her a little bit and start again. Every bit of the soft ground will have done her a world of good today. She's a classy filly - James Cowley, assistant to Karl Burke.

quotes.race: British EBF Stallions Harry Rosebery Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Beautiful Diamond deserved that and clearly didn't see the six out last time. She's all about speed, Clifford just let her roll along and it all came to her. I'm not sure if she'll run again this year, but she's probably done enough for the season and could go away now. She might get six furlongs next year on better ground but we'll probably stay over five. We'll definitely be looking at Group level now - Philip Robinson, owner's racing manager.

quotes.race: Queen Mary Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: Beautiful Diamond is still a bit of a weak filly to be honest. She's had a hard race today and we'll give her a nice easy month and then take it on from there. She's a good filly for the future. Our other two ran blinders too - Karl Burke, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Beautiful Diamond was a bit edgy when she came but we've not really pushed any buttons with her at home. I said whatever she did today she'll improve on, and we had to train her with the handbrake on leading into the race - Karl Burke, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's done really well physically from three to four and will head to the Palace House at the Guineas meeting to start her season. She was on the tails of some good sprinters last year and I think she's got a chance of being bang there in those Group races. She looks all speed and beyond Newmarket we can look at races like the Temple. 11-04-25

runners.comment: Below best in light campaign this term but in the mix on last autumn's soft-ground form
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of her game in a light campaign this season but she's back on a slow surface today and is in the mix judged on the form of her two soft-ground handicap wins last autumn; interesting contender.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: Grant Tuer said Sophia's Starlight was in form coming here today and she loves that bit of juice in the ground. I don't think she's the easiest filly to keep right at home but she's had a bit of a freshen up and was tough today - Sam James, jockey.

quotes.race: William Hill Great St Wilfrid Handicap
quotes.quote: It's great for Grant (Tuer) and Nick (Bradley) as it was a big call to make with a three-year-old. Sophia's Starlight is improving and I felt like I was going to win easier. Danny (Tudhope) nearly got there, which quite annoyed me, but she was doing everything on her own. I always had the far side beat and she got everything out of her comfort zone a long way out - Sam James, jockey.

quotes.race: Winners Wear House Of Cavani Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: We could look at something like the Sky Bet Sunday Series with Sophia's Starlight but first she is entered at Carlisle on Wednesday  - Grant Tuer, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's had a frustrating campaign, mainly because of the weather as she's proved 6f on soft ground is her thing. She needs to get going now and her main target is probably going to be the Ayr Gold Cup. She could run at Salisbury on Thursday if she gets her ground and there might be a Listed race for her somewhere. She's in good form and just needs the right set-up - a straight track and soft ground. Hopefully, there'll be a nice autumn campaign for her. 04-09-25

runners.comment: Made all in this last year and encouraging Listed third recently on belated reappearance
runners.spotlight: Made all in this last year (good) and encouraging Listed third at Beverley (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago on her belated reappearance; won on soft ground at the start of her career; her usual visor returns (left off last time) and she has leading claims if building on her comeback run.

quotes.race: Arran Scottish Sprint EBF Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: When they put up a monster performance like Star Of Lady M did at York, it can take more out of them than you think. The handicapper put her up 8lb so our hand was forced a little to run at Southwell, and it was worth a lot of money - David O'Meara, trainer.

quotes.race: Visit Irish Yearling Sale With ITM Fillies' Handicap (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: Star Of Lady M enjoyed the fast ground and was on the right part of the track, but it was a surprise because she was running so much and didn't have loads in hand. The fast ground and the tailwind might've exaggerated it. A lot of those good fillies at two can lose their way and fight back with a bang. She's back to her best and a winner on the first day is great - David O'Meara, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She had a phenomenal time last year, running 17 times and going from a rating of 74 to 110. She might start back in the Palace House at Newmarket or there's the Prix de Saint-Georges at Longchamp the week after. She wants a fast five furlongs. 14-04-25

runners.comment: Irish raider who has been progressive in handicaps but improvement is needed today
runners.spotlight: Progressive this year with two handicap wins, the latest at Chester (5f, good to soft) last month; has travelled from Ireland but she was well beaten in a Listed race at Tipperary last time and plenty of improvement is needed in first-time blinkers (replacing a visor).

quotes.race: Future Ticketing x Kappture Handicap
quotes.quote: I thought I'd rather be done for being unlucky than for coming too wide, so I bided my time and waited for gaps. When they came, Billie Be Quick went straight through them and that's just her style of running. She loves it around here - Keithen Kennedy, jockey.

runners.comment: Running well in defeat in handicaps this term but soundly beaten in Listed race last month
runners.spotlight: French soft-ground 2yo winner who has been running well in defeat in handicaps this season; however, she needs something extra on that form to come out on top today and was soundly beaten in a Listed race last month.

runners.comment: 3yo with 6 wins this year; today's company demands another step forward, but it's possible
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive 3yo front-runner who made it six wins for the year (including one here) when scoring at York (5.4f, good) in July; has continued in good form on her next two starts; unproven on slow ground and today's company demands another step forward, but that's possible.

quotes.race: Bet Boost At bet365 Handicap
quotes.quote: Luna A Inbhir Nis is just fantastic. Her owners were patient with her at the backend and they've reaped the rewards now. Her knees were a little bit weak, so we put her away. She has a few options and I'd ideally like to keep her to three-year-old races - Katie Scott, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She came to us just over a year ago unbroken and was our first horse for Summerstorm Bloodstock. She was a bit green - she's a big filly and was just learning at the backend of last year - her knees were a bit immature and she had a bit of time off, but she has just blossomed, winning five of her seven races this year, going from strength to strength. The sky is the limit at the minute - the dream is alive so we'll see how we go. At the moment we've got the Sunday Series race at Haydock pencilled in at the start of August. That would be her first run against older horses so it would be quite a step up for her, but that's what we've got half an eye on. If she kept going the way she's going we'd have to look for some black type for her. 17-07-25

runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo who showed improvement when Listed runner-up at 16-1 at Pontefract
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo who showed improved form when a Listed runner-up at 16-1 at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) last month, and the winner followed up in a French Group 3; the return to slow ground poses a question but she's of interest following that latest performance; cheekpieces go on.

quotes.race: Racing TV EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race) (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: It wasn't too much of a shock. Kevin was pretty hopeful. He said to just take a lead on Magic Basma and see how we get on, and just make sure she enjoys her first day at school ? and she seems to have done that. There was a lot of unraced horses in there, but the Roger Varian horse was coming in with a nice bit of form and I was very happy with my filly - Jockey Oisin McSweeney, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Solid 5f handicap form this term; something to find but the extra yardage could be a plus
runners.spotlight: 3yo who has shown some solid 5f handicap form this season and kept on strongly for sixth of 19 behind Star Of Mehmas in a handicap at York (5f, good to firm) last month; has something to find now up in grade but the extra yardage could be a plus; effective on soft ground; each-way possible.

quotes.race: DragonBet Proud Sponsors Of Bath Racing Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Nad Alshiba Green found six furlongs on slow ground a little bit tiring but she was in the right race there - Oisin Murphy, rider.

runners.comment: Listed fourth at Pontefract last month but she's down the pecking order today
runners.spotlight: Won Salisbury novice (6f, good to firm) last month before Listed fourth at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) four days later, wearing a hood each time; similar form when sixth in handicap at Yarmouth (6f, good to soft) on Tuesday; however, she's down the pecking order today; the hood returns.

runners.comment: 2yo Listed win here 12 months ago; defied top weight in York h'cap latest; strong claims
runners.spotlight: Won the Listed 2yo event on this card 12 months ago (5f, good) and posted a last-gasp win off top weight in a competitive handicap at York (5f, good to firm) last month; today's trip could be a good fit and she's a strong candidate if handling the ground (unraced on a slow surface).

quotes.race: IRE-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish Fillies' Handicap (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: I don't want this season to end, I've had enough of rubbish all my life. I was watching on the TV and Star Of Mehmas went out of the picture, but then she came whooshing by. Ryan said they were going frantic up front and it finally collapsed - Richard Hughes, trainer.

quotes.race: British EBF Stallions Harry Rosebery Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Star Of Mehmas broke nicely and went through the gears well. She's very straightforward and she was tough when I asked her to pick up. Mr Hughes said to have her in the first couple and see how she goes, she has a good cruising speed - Billy Loughnane, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She's a filly on the up and will make a nice three-year-old as she's got a bit of size. 18-09-24

runners.comment: Irish raider but only sixth in Listed race at Tipperary last time; improvement is needed
runners.spotlight: Won Tipperary maiden (5f, good) last month on eighth start and has travelled from Ireland for today's run; however, she was only sixth in a Listed race at Tipperary (5f, yielding) last time and improvement is needed.

runners.comment: Only fifth in Pontefract Listed last time but she's better than that and not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Behind two of these when fifth in Listed race at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) latest but she's better than that, as evidenced by her handicap wins in the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) in June and at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in July; has run well on good to soft (unraced on softer); not ruled out.

quotes.race: Betfred 3YO "Dash" Handicap
quotes.quote: At halfway I think we were last, or second-last; they must have run very quick because Stormy Impact is not slow, and she was struggling to lay up. I did think she could run well here. I was struggling to find a race for her -I had to run her over six at Newmarket and I think five is her trip. She's all speed. I think she will go for the Palace of Holyrood [on the Friday of Royal Ascot over five furlongs] - Richard Fahey, trainer.

runners.breeder: G H S Bloodstock & J C Bloodstock
runners.comment: Arrives on hat-trick; this is a tougher test but she's very much on the up
runners.spotlight: 3yo who cost 100,000euros in 2023 and things have really clicked on her last two runs, with a convincing win at Ripon (5f, good) then a last-gasp one at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) where she defied trouble in running and came clear with a progressive runner-up; this is a tougher test of her credentials but she's very much on the up; effective on good to soft, unraced on softer.

racecards.verdict: A chance is taken on TIVA, who has impressed when winning handicaps on her last two starts. Sophia's Starlight is feared most.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Star Of Mehmas, 7/2 Beautiful Diamond, 4/1 Star Of Lady M, 8/1 Magic Basma, 9/1 Stormy Impact, 11/1 Luna A Inbhir Nis, 16/1 Sophia's Starlight,  Nad Alshiba Green,  Tiva, 25/1 Lady Of Andros,  Rhyme Dust,  Stella Alpina, 40/1 Billie Be Quick

racecards.race_name: Ladbrokes Ayr Bronze Cup Handicap

runners.comment: Won at Kempton 16 days ago for which she carries 5lb penalty; last 3 wins have come on AW
runners.spotlight: Won at Kempton (6f, AW) recently, for which she carries a 5lb penalty; has won four times on turf (good-soft) but her last three wins have come on AW; needs to improve upon what she's shown on grass over the last 12 months.

runners.owner: Sporting Life Racing Club - R Fahey
runners.comment: Got no run when favourite at Thirsk recently; each-way shout on this year's best form
runners.spotlight: Got no run when favourite at Thirsk a fortnight ago and a line can be put through that race; effective in a big field and has an each-way shout on this year's best form (on various ground); wears first-time cheekpieces.

runners.comment: 3yo who went close at Carlisle this month and is 3lb well in today; chance
runners.spotlight: 3yo who was a close third in a big field at York (6f, good) in June then won at Doncaster (6f, good to soft); beaten just a head by Top Juggler at Carlisle (6f, good) this month and is 3lb well in today; chance.

runners.owner: J Fyffe, A Edmondson & L McGurty
runners.comment: A strongly run 6f in a big field could prove ideal for this progressive filly; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Has come from the rear for her two wins this season (6f/7f, good to firm; versatile ground-wise); looked as good as ever when keeping on for third at Haydock (7f, good) a fortnight ago and a strongly run 6f in a big field could prove ideal for this progressive filly; on the shortlist.

stable_tour.quote: I think she's very interesting. She's shown the kind of form she did the other day before and that was quite a good race and she's been showing that kind of ability at home. I'm not sure about trip for her. She's been working well. It's whether we keep her at 6f or go back to five. She's progressive. She possibly needs a bit of cut in the ground. We'll be trying to find similar races for her. She's a nice filly and well-made and good-bodied. I'd imagine there will be improvement in her. Mentally, we were very happy with her. We got her switched off the other day, which probably wasn't an advantage to her. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Stayed on well into mid-division over 5f last Sunday; the return to 6f is worth exploring
runners.spotlight: Hasn't threatened since winning in good style at Carlisle (5f, good to soft) in May and he's 2lb higher than when mid-division at Doncaster (5f, soft) last Sunday; however, he stayed on well and wasn't beaten far, and the return to 6f is worth exploring; first-time cheekpieces replace blinkers.

quotes.race: Boost Your Acca With BetUK Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was very straightforward. Moonstone Boy has been knocking on the door and deserved to win. Iain [Jardine, trainer] and Andrew [Mullen, regular jockey] said to me that he'd come on leaps and bounds from his first run here. He's got a lot of speed - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: A lovely sprinter who wants dig in the ground and with the dry weather his season has been on hold. I think it'll pay dividends in the back end now we're getting rain. He's one to go to Ayr with. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Positive performances on last 2 runs; each-way chance if handling the forecast slow ground
runners.spotlight: Second of 15 at Haydock (6f, good) in July and kept on strongly when close fifth of 14 at Newcastle (5f, AW) last month; each-way claims now back up to 6f, provided the ground suits (unproven on a slow surface).

quotes.race: BetUK. It's Where The UK Bets Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Bright is a lovely genuine horse. This was his first run for Mark (Loughnane) and they thought he might want six furlongs, but he wouldn't be screaming for them to step him up. He's very quick - Finley Marsh, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.owner: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd

prev_owners.owner: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd

runners.comment: Eyecatching 6th in the Gold Cup Trial over C&D in July; interesting if the ground is slow
runners.spotlight: 9yo who has regressed and isn't easy to win with; well beaten at Thirsk (6f, good) recently but slow ground is his thing and he was denied a clear run off his reduced mark when sixth of 18 in the Gold Cup Trial over C&D (good to soft) in July; cheekpieces return (no positive impact previously); interesting contender if conditions are in his favour.

stable_tour.quote: He won first time out this season when the ground was soft at Doncaster and he will win again but he needs cut underfoot. He got that last week at Ayr but he is not the biggest of horses and having to shoulder 10st 2lb around there was a big ask. The handicapper never really relents with horses like him and his recent mark of 88 proved what we expected. He won off 83 in May and needs to be around that to be seriously competitive. 26-07-23

runners.comment: Won at Thirsk in July and has continued in good heart but needs something extra today
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who won at Thirsk (6f, good) in July and is versatile ground-wise; has run pretty well on his last two starts and can give another good account, but needs something extra to win this competitive race.

quotes.race: tote On The Go At tote.co.uk Racing League 18 Handicap (Trainers Reg' To Racing League)
quotes.quote: Manila Scouse probably just likes the rain and the uphill finish, because he stays six [furlongs] so he travels really strong as well and gets through the line strong - David Allan, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Sunday Series Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: Manila Scouse ran a blinder last week and the drop back to five really suited him. He stays it really well, especially in that ground. He's very game and stuck on well in the last furlong - Oliver Stammers, jockey.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
quotes.quote: Manila Scouse loved this ground and the tongue-tie helped as well - Paul Mulrennan, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He had time off as a three-year-old, we couldn't train him, but he came back really well last season. He wants five furlongs with some cut. He was entered at Newmarket, but the ground was advertised as fast so I didn't declare him, although it actually would have been all right. 23-04-24

runners.comment: Completed hat-trick in the Portland on Saturday and able to run off 2lb lower today
runners.spotlight: Thriving 5yo who has won her last three (6f/5.5f, good), all under Lauren Young, most recently the Portland at Doncaster last Saturday; has won here three times and is effective on good to soft; this front-runner is able to run off 2lb lower than at Doncaster and could be tough to catch.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Ayr Bronze Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: Eternal Sunshine just took a false step coming out of the stalls. She probably did a bit too much through the first furlong because I only had one iron, other than that she's run a blinder - Lauren Young, rider.

quotes.race: Betfred Portland (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: Eternal Sunshine has been running really well, she was full of confidence and she was carrying no weight at all. She picked up and quickened away nicely - Lauren Young, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She's had a really good season, winning five times. It was frustrating to get beat last time when she got hampered in the Ayr Bronze Cup. She's a homebred and we purposely let her mature. I still think there's improvement in her but we've decided that was enough for the season. Hopefully she'll come back off a nice mark next year and we'll look to follow in the footsteps of American Affair. 02-10-24

runners.comment: Won the Silver Cup in 2023 and runner-up on his last two starts; he's one to consider
runners.spotlight: His losing run is up to 12 but he went close over C&D (good to soft) in July before 3l second at Chepstow (7f, good to soft) behind one who raced alone; won the Silver Cup in 2023 and he's one to consider.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Ayr Silver Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: It means a lot because there was a lot of money on Wobwobwob. I don't know who they were but I'm glad they backed a winner. It's a great race to win and the Gold Cup is on my bucket list, so hopefully this lad is heading in that direction. He loves this type of ground and a fast pace to aim at, and he's won over seven so stays the six furlongs really well. Hopefully he's one for the Gold next year. They went very hard but that suited him and he had something to aim at - Adrian Keatley, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Handicap
quotes.quote: Off a light weight you're always conscious that you can probably get away with using that bit more gas early on than you normally could. Wobwobwob is very straightforward and he's a genuine little horse. I was expecting them to come chasing at the two pole but all he did was dig deep and get away from them. A top performance off the back of a nice run at Newmarket last time - Tom Marquand, jockey.

quotes.race: Dubai Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Wobwobwob is a tough horse and did it well from his draw on the rail - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He was unlucky when second at Ayr last time; he just got going a few strides too late. He's running into form and wants ease in the ground, so he could go back to Ayr for one of the Cups. Those fast-run 6f races on soft ground - where he's a little outpaced early but flies home late - are the races for him. He's nearly ready to start winning again. 07-08-25

runners.comment: In good form up to June but his progress has stalled on his last two outings
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a win at Musselburgh (7f, good) and runner-up on next two starts (6f, good to firm); has won here twice and is probably fine on slow ground; however, his progress has stalled on his last two outings and he needs a boost from the first-time blinkers (replacing cheekpieces).

stable_tour.quote: He's done well this year and we're very pleased with him. He could have probably run another couple of times but we've kept him back for Ayr next week. We'll take him there fresh and he'll probably get in the Bronze Cup, where he could run a really big race. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Mark has fallen 12lb this season, but he hasn't been shaping as though about to exploit it
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner whose mark has fallen 12lb this season, but he hasn't been shaping as though he's about to exploit it; a visor goes on.

stable_tour.quote: He's a nice horse who has done well this year, winning a lot of prize-money. He's been busy and won a couple of times before finishing third at Newbury. He's a Saturday horse and will run in a lot of the big sprint handicaps. 29-04-24

runners.comment: Four wins this year and fared best of those drawn in single-figure stalls at Thirsk latest
runners.spotlight: 5yo who made it four wins for the season when scoring at Ripon (6f, good) last month; has also won on soft/good to soft; fared best of those drawn in single-figure stalls when close fourth of 16 to Eternal Sunshine at Thirsk (6f, good) a fortnight ago and he's not ruled out.

runners.comment: C&D winner; five wins this year and third at Ripon on last two starts; each-way contender
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (soft) who has won five times this year, the latest when making all in a big field at York (6f, good to firm) in June, and he's been third at Ripon on his last two outings; each-way contender.

stable_tour.quote: He arrived here in February in good nick from Iain Jardine's stable looking for a change of scenery having been in the same yard since he was a yearling. He proceeded to rattle off three on the spin at Newcastle twice and then at Southwell by two lengths. His trip is 6f and he likes some give in theound. I'm looking at Thirsk in May next, but things will be more difficult for him off an all-time high mark. Also entered at Newcastle and Thirsk this week. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Won at Hamilton in June but down the field on last two outings; has to get back on track
runners.spotlight: Close second at Hamilton (5f, good to soft) in June and went one better there (5f, good) 11 days later; effective over 6f in the past; however, down the field on his last two outings and needs to get back on track.

quotes.race: Top Odds Every Day With BetUK Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I think Brooklyn Nine Nine just got a bit tired the last half furlong but he got the job done. I started on the outside rail but he just drifted a bit left and I think he got a bit lonely. His two runs last year were very good and I think he needed that today so he's going to come on a bit for it - Oisin Orr, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Progressive since these cheekpieces went on and he could play a leading role
runners.spotlight: Progressive since these cheekpieces went on, winning at Doncaster (5.5f, good) and Epsom (6f, good) and finishing a close third of 16 to Eternal Sunshine at Thirsk (6f, good) recently despite having to wait for a clear run; he's right at home on slow ground and could play a leading role.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Ayr Bronze Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: It was Plan B but that doesn't matter does it? We were going to sit handier but Winged Messenger didn't jump as well as we hoped he would. They went quick and he was on the right side. This time last year we were thinking of getting rid of him but he's just changed completely this year. He used to worry all the time coming racing. Last year he'd be lathered and he'd be shaking and worried, and he wasn't a great eater. I don't know what's changed in his life but it's changed for the better. That may be it for this year for him - Julie Camacho, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We've been really pleased with him this season because last year he was very hit and miss. This year he seems to have thrived and has been improving all the time physically, which has been reflected on the racecourse. He probably just wants 6f as well, which will bring out a bit more improvement. I think 5f up a hill with some ease in the ground is fine, but on quick ground he probably needs another furlong, so it's possible he can improve again. He's done well since he won at Doncaster last week, so we'll look for another run. We'll possibly run him at York on Friday - there's a 0-80 handicap over 6f and I'm sure his owners would love a day out. He can win again. 23-07-25

runners.comment: Consistent in defeat here this season and runner-up over C&D latest; each-way claims
runners.spotlight: Two-time 7f course winner (heavy/good) who has been consistent in defeat here this season and was runner-up over today's C&D (good to firm) last month on a rare crack at sprinting; each-way claims once more.

stable_tour.quote: He doesn't really look after himself as he goes flat out. He managed to give himself a stress fracture, so I've stuck a screw in it and he's on the way back. He seems well. He likes cut in the ground. He's the opposite from Tafsir, who is very laid back. He's an improving, athletic horse. With cut in the ground, he'll be tasty. He's hard to catch, but he is vulnerable on faster ground. I'd think he's six weeks away from a run. We'll look forward to him. 07-05-25

runners.owner: Michele Craig And David D Smith
runners.comment: Four-time course winner over 7f/1m; strong pace will set things up nicely for him
runners.spotlight: He's done all of his winning over 7f/1m (four of them here) but he kept on well for third of 18 in the Gold Cup Trial over C&D (good to soft) in July, when denied a clear run; a strong pace in this big field will set things up nicely for him, provided he gets the breaks; possible player.

quotes.race: Musselburgh Windsor FC Celebrating 70 Years Handicap
quotes.quote: The race didn't set up the way we thought as Joe (Fanning) got to the front and slowed up a bit but Yaaser did it well, he ran as straight as a gun-barrel up the straight. The last time here we probably played too early and we've learnt from that, the more you take him back the more he comes back on the bit. I think you could even play him later as he has a good turn of foot - Jim Goldie, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: He looks to be back to his best and his best is very good. He had a good year last year but not quite where we'd had him before. He looked really well in the paddock before he won and showed his wellbeing in the race. He's won some nice races. He likes a strong pace to run at over 7f and I think that's possibly his optimum, but he ran well in the Carlisle Bell over a stiff mile. We've won it before but that race is on our bucket list, but it may be a bit sharp for him at the moment. We'll have a look at York for him because he ran well there last year and a big field over 7f would suit him. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Six wins in the first half of the year but his more recent form has been mixed
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins early this year (5f/6f) and off the mark on turf at Haydock (7f) in May; his subsequent form has been mixed, however, and he's yet to shine on slow ground; others are more compelling.

quotes.race: Pertemps Handicap
quotes.quote: I thought Oriental Prince had just held on. The race set up nicely, he relaxed and I was able to get a breather into him. He hasn't stopped improving yet - Paul Mulrennan, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He's had a brilliant all-weather season. We're at a crossroads with him because we're not sure if he's done enough or not, or if we need to change tactics. He's hard to catch on the all-weather but the turf is slightly different to the all-weather and we're not sure if we're going too hard or not hard enough. He's still quite an exciting horse. If you look at the sectionals at Musselburgh in a very strong race last month it was very good. We probably used up a lot of petrol with Gweedore and I think if we'd have been left alone then we'd have been first or second. That's Ayr Gold Cup form and we're taking on Class 2 handicappers, so he's interesting. He's obviously very fast. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Somewhat wayward in blinkers at Doncaster last week but nevertheless posted a close fourth
runners.spotlight: Somewhat wayward in first-time blinkers (retained) at Doncaster (6f, good) last Friday but nevertheless returned to form with a close fourth of 15; his wins have come on slow ground and he might not be far away.

stable_tour.quote: It was incredible that it took us so long to win with him last season, but he was just doing too much too early in his races. I told Jamie Spencer to get him settled when he ran at Nottingham, which he did, and he picked up great and won nicely. He did the same thing next time at York and should be one who progresses again this season in those big sprint handicaps. 04-04-24

runners.comment: Running well (including here) before form dipped at Chester; e-w possible if bouncing back
runners.spotlight: 3yo who won two in a row in June (6f, good) before close thirds on his next two starts (including over C&D on good to soft); his form dipped at Chester three weeks ago but he's an each-way possible if bouncing back.

quotes.race: TMT Group Handicap
quotes.quote: We ruined Arabian Cobra last year by running him in the Windsor Castle. It's taken a long time to get his confidence back. But this year working with the juveniles has got him back to himself. That was his second win in a week and if things went right I wouldn't rule out his winning again - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

runners.breeder: D Ryan, D S Ryan & R A Williams

prev_owners.owner: C Barclay, Racing Knights & C Cleevely

runners.comment: In good form at favourite course Ripon earlier in term but this 9yo is probably vulnerable
runners.spotlight: Placed on first three starts of the season and has a lower mark today; however, those were at his favourite course Ripon and subsequent efforts haven't been up to scratch (at York/Ripon); this 9yo is probably vulnerable.

quotes.race: Wilmot-Smith Memorial Handicap
quotes.quote: Fortamour broke well and I got a good position; everything just went so smoothly. He was pulling up as soon as he hit the front, but just seems to love this track - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Easily made all in small field at Yarmouth in June but no better than eighth subsequently
runners.spotlight: Easily made all in a small field at Yarmouth (6f, good) in June and has also won on good to soft; however, he's finished no better than eighth across his last three outings; wears a first-time hood.

runners.comment: Won on recent stable debut at Carlisle; could have more to offer for yard; not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground 2yo winner for Quinn yard last August and beat Territorial Knight by a head to win on recent stable debut at Carlisle (6f, good); could have more to offer for this yard and not ruled out under 5lb penalty.

runners.comment: 6f AW novice winner but has to improve upon what he's shown in three handicaps this summer
runners.spotlight: 3yo who easily won 6f AW novice in June on third start but he needs to improve upon what he's shown in his three handicaps this summer.

racecards.verdict: It is hard to get away from the thriving ETERNAL SUNSHINE (nap), who can run off 2lb lower than when winning the Portland on Saturday.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Eternal Sunshine, 15/2 Winged Messenger, 8/1 Territorial Knight, 9/1 Mostar Dreams, 12/1 Mister Sox,  Kelpie Grey,  Wobwobwob,  Kats Bob, 14/1 Top Juggler, 16/1 Bright,  Musical Touch, 20/1 Arabian Cobra, 25/1 Woven,  Jonny Concrete,  Irish Nectar, 33/1 Yaaser,  Powdering,  Brooklyn Nine Nine,  Bosh,  Manila Scouse, 40/1 Moonstone Boy, 50/1 Oriental Prince,  Fortamour, 66/1 Beyond Borders,  Azure Zain

prev_owners.owner: Haras D'Etreham, Riviera Equine Sarl & Ecurie De Montlahuc

runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner; only his second run in a visor when third at Ripon (1m, good to firm) but took an age to get going and may have developed his own ideas; fine on soft; non-runner.

stable_tour.quote: He won at Beverley on his comeback and then ran a nice race in the Thirsk Hunt Cup. A nice, big horse with some reasonable form in France, he could have a good enough summer and might run at York this week. 09-05-22

runners.comment: A well-held third here on Thursday and the previous Haydock run was nothing special
runners.spotlight: Effective at this track but was a well-held third here on Thursday and her previous Haydock run was nothing special.

quotes.race: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
quotes.quote: It's probably the place to be running today from the front. We were dropping Penelope's Sister back in trip and we know she gets this one well. Jim's [Goldie, trainer] horses are all healthy and in great form - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Given a bit to do before staying on into third on soft ground at Sandown
runners.spotlight: Solid record in handicaps and was given a bit to do before staying on into third on soft ground at Sandown (1m); lower mileage than many in here.

runners.comment: Out of the money here on Thursday when behind Little Ted
runners.spotlight: Dual Irish winner who has been well held for this yard bar one run; out of the money here on Thursday when behind Little Ted.

runners.comment: First run on ground slower than good when a clearcut winner at Musselburgh
runners.spotlight: Consistent and having first run on ground slower than good when a clearcut winner at Musselburgh (1m); carries a penalty in a higher grade here but considered.

runners.comment: Well beaten in her last four races and perhaps better at 7f than 1m
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in her last four races and perhaps better at 7f than 1m.

runners.comment: Lightly raced and best form yet in handicaps when third at Carlisle (1m, soft)
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and best form yet in handicaps when third at Carlisle (1m, soft); could improve some more.

stable_tour.quote: Quite a nice filly who won at Ripon in July. She's tough and genuine with a nice pedigree. I think she can improve with age and with a bit of dig in the ground as well. She could be all right. 10-09-25

runners.breeder: B Kennedy & Mrs Ann Marie Kennedy
runners.comment: Returning to 1m from further is a good thing but all his best form is on drier ground
runners.spotlight: Returning to 1m from further is a good thing but all his best form is on drier ground.

runners.comment: Consistent; runner-up to one in form here on Thursday and that was at 7f; better at 1m
runners.spotlight: Consistent and ran another sound race in defeat when beating all bar an in-form opponent here on Thursday; that was at 7f and this trip should suit him better.

runners.comment: Had been running well but not the last twice, including here on Thursday
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who had been running well but not the last twice, including here on Thursday.

runners.comment: Regular here and escapes a penalty for last weekend's win at Musselburgh
runners.spotlight: Regular here and escapes a penalty for last weekend's win at Musselburgh, which was fairly comfortable in the end (1m, good); had soft-ground form earlier in career.

quotes.race: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Golden Valour is much better than his mark. I thought he wasn't helping Donagh up the straight but it was hard work in that wind. There's a valuable handicap here next month which we might go for - Richard O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We ran him over hurdles during the winter but he's had a break since February and will be running on the Flat again soon, where he's still only rated 55. He's a nice-looking, well-bred horse. He might do a bit of both, Flat and jumps, just to sweeten him up. He's been a bit unlucky and I‘m hopeful he can improve from his current mark when he comes out. I'm looking forward to running him. 16-05-24

runners.comment: Best run yet when nosed out over a similar trip at Hamilton (good to soft)
runners.spotlight: Best run yet when nosed out over a similar trip at Hamilton (good to soft) and being out of the weights is not too damaging with Lauren Young taking off 7lb.

racecards.verdict: Richard Spencer's ATMOSPHERE came from a long way behind to take third at Sandown and he has greater upside than many in here.

racecards.race_name: Graeme Allan's 70th Birthday Pension Scheme Handicap (Div II)

runners.comment: Never involved over C&D on Thursday and he's now 0-12 on the grass
runners.spotlight: AW winner; placed in last week's Legends race at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) but was never involved over C&D on Thursday and he's now 0-12 on the grass.

runners.comment: Took advantage of a career-low mark to gain his first success at Ayr (7f, good) last month
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a career-low mark to gain his first success over 7f here last month (good); nudged back up 3lb but should run his race as slow ground is no issue.

stable_tour.quote: We're still learning about him. He's quite big and probably didn't handle Musselburgh that well and was a little bit unlucky as he got a bump at the wrong time. He got closer at Doncaster when second on Saturday when he ducked right when challenging. Hopefully he'll be a player in a similar race soon. 07-05-25

runners.comment: A winner on good to firm latest; 2lb higher and drying conditions will help
runners.spotlight: Winning his second 1m handicap this year when doing it narrowly at Ripon (1m, good to firm); only 2lb higher and drying conditions will help.

runners.comment: Won a seller in October but no worthwhile form in five handicaps since then
runners.spotlight: Won a seller in October but no worthwhile form in five handicaps since then.

quotes.race: Join Racing TV Now Selling Stakes
quotes.quote: Bint Al Karama won in nice style and I'm pleased to have kept hold of her. She looked a reasonable filly for the money at the sales so we took a chance and we're hopeful she'll win again. She has a nice pedigree and might even make a broodmare - Iain Jardine, trainer.

runners.comment: Not progressing in handicaps, despite making the frame at Newcastle last week (7f)
runners.spotlight: Not progressing in handicaps, despite making the frame at Newcastle last week (7f); unraced on ground this slow and has stamina to prove.

runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
runners.comment: Continues his progressive year with a battling success over 7f here on Thursday
runners.spotlight: Generally progressive this year, showing improvement from 7f to 1m2f; continued his good work with a battling success here over the shorter trip on Thursday; penalised accordingly but hard to knock.

prev_owners.owner: Sunday Racing Club/Grey Lady Racing Syndicate

runners.comment: Recent course winner but inconsistent and probably wants better ground
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably over 1m2f here last month but couldn't back it up on his return visit; probably wants better ground than this.

quotes.race: Return Of The Revival Festival Handicap
quotes.quote: Cisco Disco is a grand little horse that we decided to run him back quickly. He's a versatile horse and a turning track suits him - Jack Davison, trainer.

quotes.race: www.swordsecurity.com Nursery Handicap (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: He's improving with every run, it's just taken a while for the penny to drop. The headgear is just to help him concentrate, mentally he's a little slow to take everything in. Physically, he'll improve with time as well. He wants a bit better ground and further. Whatever happens this year is a bonus for him - Ronan Whelan, rider of Cisco Disco

runners.comment: In form but was some way behind Dwindling Funds when fourth here on Thursday
runners.spotlight: In a good run of form for her current yard but was some way behind Dwindling Funds when fourth here on Thursday.

runners.comment: Struggled here on Thursday when the ground may have been too soft for him
runners.spotlight: Only sixth on his penultimate run and again struggled here on Thursday when the ground may have been too soft for him.

runners.comment: 12 defeats include bumpers, hurdles and Flat races; in poor form
runners.spotlight: 12 defeats include bumpers, hurdles and Flat races; in poor form.

runners.comment: Second last time but finds it hard to follow one good run with another
runners.spotlight: Went close last time at Catterick (7f, good) but following one good run with another can be challenging these days; has form on soft ground but 7f is his trip.

quotes.race: totequadpot Insure Your Last Four Maiden Stakes
quotes.quote: Pat gave Desert Dream a good ride. We'll run him over further in handicaps now. The ground was perfect. Absolutely perfect - Sir Evelyn De Rothschild, owner-breeder.

racecards.verdict: The 3yo NATIVE HONEY exploited a reduced mark last time and may follow up as conditions won't bother him.

racecards.race_name: Slater Menswear Handicap

runners.comment: Won this in 2022 and 2023; returns for another crack in reasonable form
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2022 and 2023; returns for another crack in reasonable form and certainly not handicapped out of contention; seems fine on most ground.

runners.comment: Has the form to feature but has mostly steered clear of slow ground
runners.spotlight: Both wins at 1m2f, on good to firm and the AW; third at Beverley last month before struggling in a Class 4; could go well, though has mostly steered clear of slow ground.

runners.comment: Could be happier than some on this ground and he has run well here in the past
runners.spotlight: Below best on last two runs and he loves Hamilton; however, could be happier than some on this ground and he has run well here in the past.

quotes.race: Sodexo Live Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: Two Auld Pals definitely likes the track - he's a Hamilton specialist. I'm very pleased for him and he deserves to win. I thought he was coming back to form last time. He's on a mark where he could probably follow up because of his attitude and bravery. We're going to keep him on the Flat for a little while longer and might mix hurdling in the future - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

runners.comment: Threw it away with some late wandering at Hamilton (1m5f, good to soft); respected
runners.spotlight: Now 0-11 but was just ahead of a few of these when second to Perseus Way at Hamilton (1m5f, good to soft) and would have won had he not wandered around for pressure; slower ground is a grey area but firmly enters the reckoning.

stable_tour.quote: He's one we have from John and Thady Gosden and he could potentially be nice - he showed some promise behind Faylaq at Ayr on Sunday. He's big, 16.3hh, and a late-maturing type. He's not very quick but they don't need to be if they keep galloping. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Bang there at Hamilton (1m5f, good to soft) last time in the race won by Perseus Way
runners.spotlight: 9yo who hasn't won for ages but he was bang there at Hamilton (1m5f, good to soft) last time in the race won by Perseus Way; should be in the thick of it.

quotes.race: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap
quotes.quote: Billy No Mates was too high in the handicap last year. He hadn't run this season because he needs soft ground and it's been too firm for him. I was worried the trip would be too short but he outstayed them on the ground - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
quotes.quote: The hood has definitely helped Billy No Mates. He's still strong early on but he settled into the race and picked up well - Clifford Lee, jockey.

runners.comment: Beat some of these at Hamilton but was below par at Musselburgh last weekend
runners.spotlight: Had a few of these hot on his heels when winning at Hamilton (1m5f, good to soft) but was below par at Musselburgh last weekend.

quotes.race: Weatherbys Chatteris Fen Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Perseus Way is a very nice horse and I'm pleased that he has done it well today. His jumping is getting better all the time, but it needs to. He is getting slicker and quicker and he is starting to enjoy his racing. It was a good run in defeat at Chepstow last time and I felt that Chepstow was probably soft enough for him. We rode him too far back that day and that is why we rode him handier today. I don't think he is good enough to run in the Triumph but I think he could have a strong chance in the handicap - Gary Moore, trainer.

quotes.race: Dreweatts Handicap
quotes.quote: Perseus Way has not run for months because we've been waiting for the ground. Jim said he'd get as far as you'd want. There is talk Gary Moore could have him for jumping at some stage. We were waiting to see what he'd do today because if he was well beaten, he might have gone to Gary's sooner! We just got away with the trip today as he's a grinder who will appreciate more give in the ground - Owen Burrows, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Reference Point Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was very pleased with Perseus Way. I hadn't sat on him at home and it was a proper slog on this ground, but he was quite fit and was able to keep going. He coped with conditions well. He's definitely a nice type for next year, he's big and raw and should furnish away over the winter - James Doyle, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: Ms S Johnstone & W M Johnstone

runners.comment: A winner here last month on good to firm; opposable on his form on slower ground
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in this last year off a lower mark; has won four races in the meantime, the latest over C&D a month ago (good to firm); opposable on his form on slower ground.

stable_tour.quote: He missed the break at Nottingham last week, was caught wide and got involved in quite a rough race, but he got up in the dying strides. He was clearly the best horse in the race and will improve as he goes up in trip from 1m2f. 18-04-21

runners.comment: Hat-trick here earlier in the year; a worry that soft ground has found him out in the past
runners.spotlight: Third in this 12 months ago; well treated on his hat-trick of wins here earlier in the year (1m2f-1m5f) but soft ground has found him out in the past.

quotes.race: CPMS Handicap
quotes.quote: I've won on Glasses Up a few times. We thought the mile might be short for him but he's proved us wrong. They went a good gallop and he picked up well - Paddy Mathers, jockey.

quotes.race: Get So Much More With Racing UK Handicap
quotes.quote: Glasses Up is just getting better and better and in time I'm sure he will step up to one and a half miles. His target is the Kilkerran Cup at the Gold Cup meeting - Mike Smith, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a super horse. When I bought him at the sales I was told I could spend £8k, maybe £10k at a push, and I rang the boys up and said, “I'm going to have to go to full budget for this.” The boys said, “Mike, that's a lot of money for a horse who hasn't run”, and I said “yes, but he's a cracking horse.” He was actually bought with an entry for the Derby. He's now won eight races for us and £121k in prize-money, so it was money well spent. The boys have turned down fairly good offers for him because they wanted to keep racing and having fun with him, which is commendable. He was a good third on his seasonal reappearance at Ayr behind Fiver Friday. He benefited from a racecourse gallop on the track after the Scottish National meeting, which is a fantastic thing and I think that's one of the reasons the Irish horses do so well. 16-05-24

runners.comment: Becoming frustrating but recent efforts give him definite each-way claims
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come at Musselburgh on good ground, though probably handles soft; becoming frustrating but recent efforts give him definite each-way claims; up 2lb in future.

stable_tour.quote: He won at Musselburgh over 2m2f in late August and has finished third the last twice- at Chester and Ayr on Tuesday. He's probably going to have another couple of runs this autumn as he seems to handle soft ground. He's a young horse who's improving. He ran on good ground at Ayr on Tuesday and it would be good if we can get another run with him on soft ground before the end of the season. I doubt we'll run him on the all-weather this winter and we'll probably wait until next year with him. 02-10-24

runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing
runners.comment: A rallying second last week at Newcastle and this soft ground won't faze her
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner who hinted that her turn might be near when a rallying second at Newcastle last week (1m4f); soft ground won't faze her; due a 1lb rise.

runners.comment: Recent Musselburgh winner but well held here on Thursday and prefers drier ground
runners.spotlight: Beat four rivals with some ease at Musselburgh last month (1m4f, good) but was well beaten here on Thursday and drier ground suits her better.

stable_tour.quote: She's a progressive filly who has won over 1m2f and 1m4f this season and is still on the upgrade. She's out of a Galileo mare from a very good family, of which the granny was the best. She's up to a mark of 71 and I expect more progress. 12-09-22

runners.comment: An assured stayer as his sole win came over 2m on good to soft at Redcar in July
runners.spotlight: An assured stayer as his sole win came over 2m on good to soft at Redcar in July; his good run of form came to a halt last time but should be staying on when others have cried enough, provided he handles the softer ground.

stable_tour.quote: He's done really well at his level. We'll probably continue on the Flat but he'll probably end up going hurdling. He wants a trip and would be ideal for places like Musselburgh. 10-09-25

prev_owners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell
B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.comment: Has course form, handles soft ground and ran well last time; tries blinkers
runners.spotlight: Handles soft ground, has competitive form over C&D and returns to Ayr after a close third at Hamilton (1m5f, good to soft; closely matched with some in here); should go well provided the new headgear doesn't backfire.

quotes.race: Metges Lane Maiden
quotes.quote: Ebony Maw did that lovely. He's still green, he dosn't have many miles on the clock, and he wouldn't want the ground any better than yielding. There could be a nice handicap in him over 1m2f plus - Shane Lyons, assistant trainer

runners.comment: Winning hurdler; very lightly raced on the Flat and could do with drier ground
runners.spotlight: Not beaten too far over hurdles 11 days ago but very lightly raced on the Flat and could do with drier ground.

quotes.race: Edinburgh Gin Seaside Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: It's a track Just Dottie likes. Although Ben [Smith] wasn't riding today, he did tell me of the jockeys available Sean Bowen was the man for it and it'd suit him perfectly. She'll go two or three times in the summer. It was a big step up today to find out just what we've got, but to do that was fantastic - Mike Smith, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: DM Hall Surveyors Mares' Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: I'm very happy that we've got Just Dottie to win it for the second year running. She's done everything right again today. When I've asked her to pick up between the last two she really has and she's gone away and won quite nicely - Ben Smith, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: She was fifth on her first run back following a 227-day break at Perth last time and, if you listened to the commentary, you'd say she was something of an eyecatcher. She goes to Perth again on Thursday in the race she won last year and she should go well. 16-05-24

racecards.verdict: Marginal preference is for FLEUR DE MER who narrowly missed out on the AW last week and she won't be fazed by the conditions.
