racecards.race_name: Kennet Valley Syndicates Nick Robinson EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Has a useful pedigree and looks open to improvement with Windsor outing under his belt
runners.spotlight: Has a useful pedigree; recorded only a modest RPR with midfield finish at Windsor (6f, good to firm) but is open to improvement with the experience under his belt.

runners.comment: Trailed home last of 15 in Glorious Goodwood maiden; gelded since
runners.spotlight: Trailed home last of 15 in 6f maiden at Glorious Goodwood, having been friendless in the betting; gelded since; needs a huge step forward in form terms.

runners.comment: Gelded since two spring efforts; latest form has been boosted by the useful winner
runners.spotlight: Gelded since two spring efforts (5f/7f, good to firm) that suggest 6f may prove his ideal trip; latest form has been boosted by the useful winner subsequently; possibilities.

runners.comment: Appeared to run well in latest Ffos Las start, albeit was only a modest third
runners.spotlight: 150-1 shot in a couple of Ffos Las maidens but appeared to run well, albeit only a modest third, the latest occasion (6f, good to soft); needs further improvement.

runners.comment: Has shown ability with his similar efforts over 6f; again has frame possibilities
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability with his similar efforts over 6f, finishing fourth at Windsor (good) then third at Salisbury (good to soft); again has frame possibilities.

runners.comment: Needs big improvement for this drop to sprinting and first-time hood
runners.spotlight: Plenty to find on his Goodwood efforts over longer trips; thus needs big improvement for this combination of drop to sprinting and first-time hood.

runners.breeder: Lynch Bages, Camas Park & Summerhill B/S
runners.comment: Seems the stable second string but is one of the main form contenders
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in a couple of sprint races on good to firm, latest at Ripon; seems the stable second string on jockey arrangements but is one of the main form contenders.

runners.comment: Promising second, behind odds-on winner, at Ripon; leading contender on the figures
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a 6f 2yo winner; promising second of nine, behind odds-on winner, in Ripon maiden over this distance on good ground; leading contender on the figures.

runners.comment: May be more interesting in handicaps after this
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced colt who has modest claims on form and may be more interesting in handicaps after this.

runners.comment: Had a better experience when second to ready winner in latest Windsor outing
runners.spotlight: Both outings over 6f at Windsor, stumbling and unseating rider in the early stages on debut then having a better experience when second to ready winner; could go well.

runners.comment: Down the field in C&D event when last seen ten weeks ago
runners.spotlight: Down the field in C&D event when last seen ten weeks ago; looks likely to find better opportunities in handicaps shortly.

runners.comment: Iffraaj half-sister to two winners; sole newcomer in the field; market instructive
runners.spotlight: Iffraaj half-sister to two winners, notably Shock Action (Japanese 1m turf 2yo including Grade 3; RPR 101); dam Italian 1m/1m2f Listed scorer; sole newcomer in the field; market instructive.

racecards.verdict: The shortlist is headed by the Hannon runners CREDIT FORGEDD IT (apparent first string, narrowly preferred) and Noble Vow.

racecards.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Full Of Surprises British EBF Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Won at Haydock then ran creditably in Sandown Listed event; may do better still
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 7f on good ground, winning at Haydock then creditable fourth in Sandown Listed event; debut form ties her in closely with True Test on a line through the runner-up; may do better still.

runners.comment: Held in French Group 2 last time; 2-2 on home soil; leading player back down in class
runners.spotlight: Two from two on home soil, namely 7f wins on good to firm at Lingfield and Newmarket; bubble was burst (beaten nearly 7l) in French Group 2 since but holds a leading chance on the figures back down in class.

runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling; largely sprinting pedigree raises a doubt over 7f on debut
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; Dark Angel half-sister to Swedish 6f 2yo winner Imperial Bling; dam 5f/6f scorer (RPR 72), half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Yafta; pedigree raises a doubt over 7f on debut.

runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling; by Starman out of half-sister to G1 winner; interesting newcomer
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; Starman half-sister to 6f-8.6f winner Soi Dao (RPR 74); dam Listed-placed 6.5f-8.6f scorer (including 2yo; 99), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Bayside Boy; interesting newcomer.

runners.comment: Came from off the pace to make a winning debut in 7f AW maiden; open to progress
runners.spotlight: 140,000euros breeze-up purchase; came from off the pace to make a winning debut in 7f AW maiden at Wolverhampton, belying market weakness; goes up in grade looking open to progress.

runners.comment: Debutante by New Approach and first foal of a maiden; check the betting
runners.spotlight: Debutante by New Approach and first foal of maiden (RPR 82) half-sister to two useful winners (at about 1m-1m2f, including 2yo) and daughter of 7f/1m Group 3 winner; check the betting.

runners.comment: From a family that has served her connections well; respected newcomer
runners.spotlight: Oasis Dream filly; sibling to three winners, notably Accidental Agent (6f-1m including Group 1; 118); dam 5f 2yo/7f scorer (90); from a family that has served her connections well; respected newcomer.

racecards.verdict: The vote goes to once-raced maiden winner GHAADAH, ahead of newcomer Violet Goldsmith. A few others have possibilities.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Ghaadah, 5/2 True Test, 9/2 Florida Suite, 6/1 Tavana, 9/1 Aura Champagne, 10/1 Violet Goldsmith, 25/1 Neat And Tidy

racecards.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Good efforts in valuable nurseries the last twice; 7f could bring out more; much respected
runners.spotlight: Won on debut (5f, good to firm) and good to soft ground proved fine when showing improved form to finish second in a 6f nursery at Glorious Goodwood; got going too late but it was still a good effort when beaten about 2l in another valuable nursery at York one month ago; shaped like he's well worth a go at 7f that day so much respected.

stable_tour.quote: He's nice and won really well on his debut at Leicester. He should run a good race in thew Windsor Castle. 10-06-25

runners.comment: Won by 4l in June; not seen since down the field in 5f Listed since; handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of his debut fifth at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May to win by just over 4l at Bath (5.7f, good) the following month; down the field in the Listed Windsor Castle (5f) at Royal Ascot 12 days later and not seen since; handicap debut.

stable_tour.quote: He's a lovely type. He was very green first time but learned a lot from it and won a good pot at Bath second time out. It was an ambitious target to go to Royal Ascot for the Windsor Castle on his third run - he ran very well but looked a bit light and tired afterwards, so he's had a break since. He's done well for it and is looking great. Hopefully he'll find a nursery in September. 20-08-25

runners.comment: Awkward start, kept on for 6th in valuable Goodwood nursery (7f, heavy);  has been gelded
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in first three starts, failing odds-on backers in the last two of them; went off favourite again for a valuable Glorious Goodwood nursery (7f, heavy) in July and kept at it, after an awkward start, to finish sixth of 11; might be looking for 1m now but he's far from ruled out; has been gelded.

runners.comment: Weakened on good to soft; saw 7f out better on good to firm since; drying ground a help
runners.spotlight: Easily made all at Brighton (6f, good) in May on third start then mid-division in the Group 3 Albany at Royal Ascot; she weakened on handicap debut at Doncaster (good to soft) in July but saw it out better on good to firm when second of six at Newmarket (also 7f) last time; probably wants the ground to dry out.

quotes.race: Star Sports We Believe In Bookmaking EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qual') (GBB)
quotes.quote: Senorita Vega ran two good races without winning and probably bumping into nice horses and we thought today she should win and she did it well. She was fine on the track and on the ground and, as she's had two runs before, she knew what it was all about - Pierre-Louis Jamin, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Won last two at 6f; all out latest but likely capable of better; considered on h'cap debut
runners.spotlight: Ran green on debut here (6.5f) in June but he left that form a long way behind when winning at Haydock (good to firm) last month; had to roll his sleeves up but it was still another step forward when defying a penalty at Carlisle (also 6f, good) 18 days ago; considered on handicap debut.

runners.comment: Eyecatcher when 5th on heavy ground two starts ago; forgiven latest run and not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins on good to firm, the second on nursery debut at York; upped to 7f on heavy ground at Glorious Goodwood and hit a flat spot before running into plenty of trouble, eventually keeping on for fifth (Victorious One sixth) in July; can put a line through latest start at York and he's not ruled out.

runners.comment: All starts on AW; good step forward when second (7f) three weeks ago; turf/handicap debut
runners.spotlight: All starts on AW; showed abililty on first two starts and it was a good step forward when second of nine at Chelmsford (7f) three weeks ago; well backed that day and can likely do better again; turf/handicap debut.

stable_tour.quote: A lovely, big horse out of a Nathaniel mare. He looks more like a miler in the making and I thought he did really well on his first start to finish fourth in a warm-looking 6f race at Kempton. He should keep progressing through the second half of the season and next year. 09-07-25

runners.comment: Won here (good) in August; beaten short head (7f, heavy) one week ago; 2lb well in
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to entering nurseries and followed a narrow win here (good) in August with a close third at Ffos Las (also 6f, good to soft) 13 days later; took it up a notch when beaten a short head over 7f on heavy ground at Salisbury one week ago; same mark today (will be 2lb higher in future) and yard won five of the last ten renewals of this.

quotes.race: Daily Bet Boosts At BetVictor Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: It's nice to get French Affair to win because he's been a little disappointing up to now. We gelded him and it's brought the best out of him - Richard Hannon, trainer.

runners.comment: Again placed on nursery debut when tried in cheekpieces in July; has been gelded
runners.spotlight: Two seconds include a near miss over 7f at Haydock two runs ago; again placed on nursery debut when tried in cheekpieces (removed) in July and now returns from a gelding operation; taken out on account of heavy ground at Salisbury one week ago.

runners.comment: Promising fifth in useful-looking maiden (7f) in July; worth considering in first nursery
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward from Windsor debut when a never-nearer fifth in useful-looking maiden at Sandown (good) in July; looked a potential improver but didn't build on that when fourth of nine on AW debut at Kempton (also 7f); by no means one to give up on returned to turf in his first nursery.

runners.comment: Patchy form but was second over C&D (good to firm) in July; has been gelded
runners.spotlight: Patchy form in qualifying runs and two nurseries, but it may be encouraging that his best form in the latter sphere came when second over C&D (good to firm) in July; has been racing freely so a gelding operation may have helped too.

racecards.verdict: The nod goes to FRENCH AFFAIR (nap), who is ahead of the handicapper and hails from a yard with a fine record in this race.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Hardy's Hero, 4/1 French Affair, 13/2 Shaman Champion, 15/2 Sovereign View, 10/1 Gorey Gold,  Victorious One,  Senorita Vega, 12/1 Amazing Journey, 14/1 Eyes Front, 16/1 John Barleycorn, 20/1 Robert Anstruther

racecards.race_name: Haynes, Hanson & Clark Conditions Stakes (Colts & Geldings)

runners.comment: Ran poorly in the Acomb; leading player on his Vintage Stakes effort the time before
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who ran poorly in the Acomb most recently; good fourth in the Vintage Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) the time before and drops back in class with a leading chance on that form.

quotes.race: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Blagrave Maiden Stakes (GBBRace)
quotes.quote: Goodwood Galaxy did everything right. We don't train to win first time out, so we never really go there with a massive expectation. But his work was good and his two working companions have won well in the past few days. He can be a bit opiniated at times, so the plan was to keep it simple. By keeping him happy he kept everyone else happy. He has his quirks and I was told his stallion [Kodi Bear] was the same. He wants to do things his way at times and instead of stopping him from doing them, you have to channel them into positive attitudes - David Menuisier, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won really well on his debut at Salisbury and his Racing Post Rating of 92 was quite high for a debutant. Looking at previous winners of the Vintage, that rating is usually what horses ran to on their first or second start and he went for that race on Tuesday. Of course there's his name and owners [Goodwood Racehorse Owners Group] and he ran well to be fourth, beaten about two lengths. Nobody was under any illusions that it was a tough race, but it was well worth a go. 30-07-25

runners.comment: Gelded prior to AW win last time; this Godolphin runner is open to further progress
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to half-length success at Kempton (7f, AW) two weeks ago, taking novice record to 221; this Godolphin runner, who cost 400,000euros as a yearling, is open to further progress.

runners.comment: Awarded a Sandown maiden last time, having suffered interference; may improve further
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut but took a huge step forward at Sandown (1m, good) where, having been hampered by the first past the post, he was awarded the race after a stewards' inquiry; may improve further.

runners.comment: Recorded 2l success at Kempton in latest AW start; brings potential to this better race
runners.spotlight: Cost 340,000gns as a yearling; both starts over 7f on AW, finishing second at Southwell then recording a 2l success at Kempton; in excellent hands and brings potential to this better grade.

runners.owner: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners
runners.comment: Fighting chance on ratings but is quite exposed and was below par upped to 1m last time
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-6 reflects that he's more exposed than these rivals; brings Group form (6f/7f) and appears to have a fighting chance on ratings but he was below par when upped to 1m last time.

runners.comment: 300,000gns foal; by Wootton Bassett; notable connections; interesting newcomer
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns foal; by Wootton Bassett and first foal of unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Ivawood and daughter of 1m2f-1m4f scorer; notable connections; interesting newcomer.

runners.comment: Showed promise in Kempton AW event; faces a stiffer task switched to turf
runners.spotlight: First foal of dual AW winner for her owner; duly showed promise with third of 11 in 1m contest on the Kempton Polytrack; faces a stiffer task switched to turf and will need to show good progress.

racecards.verdict: Some of the last-time winners look open to further progress and the suggestion is TIERRA DEL TORO, ahead of Look To The Stars.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Look To The Stars, 3/1 Goodwood Galaxy, 11/2 Tierra Del Toro,  Kokbastau, 7/1 Loz Vegas,  Bourbon Blues, 12/1 Uniter

racecards.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Cup Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.breeder: Ecurie Ades Hazan& Mme Karine Morice
runners.comment: Has to overcome 5lb penalty for Group 3 win on the continent; third run for new yard
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on the continent and returned to German soil for creditable Group 2 effort this month on second start for new yard; has to overcome 5lb penalty for earlier Group 3 success, however.

runners.comment: Useful sprinter; has something to find on ratings and something to prove over new trip
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter who commenced his 2024 campaign with a handicap hat-trick and has been placed in Group/Listed races since; has something to find on ratings and something to prove over the new trip.

quotes.race: BetUK AWC Sprint Trial Handicap
quotes.quote: Ferrous has done that well in a smart race. It's his first go round here and he took a bit of getting round the bend but he's in a great spot - Tom Marquand, jockey.

quotes.race: Betonline At BetGoodwin Cock O'The North EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
quotes.quote: We've had to wait a while for Ferrous as he's been slow maturing but he's worth waiting for. He did that well as he had to tough it out - Mick Channon, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He has started back really well, winning a 6f handicap at Lingfield in February. His form screams he's a better horse on the all-weather than turf, but I believe that's more down to circumstances than anything else. He's a little bit tricky to get spot on but I feel like we have him there at the moment. There are two big handicaps to target him at - one at Southwell on Sunday and one at Newcastle. We may look at something on the turf later on, maybe the Stewards' Cup or the Wokingham, but then he'll have an early break with a view to having him back for the all-weather again. 02-04-25

runners.comment: Last two attempts over 7f comprise a very useful win and creditable third in Group 3 event
runners.spotlight: Failed to get home over 1m in French race most recently; last two attempts over 7f comprise a very useful handicap win at Ascot (soft) and creditable third in Haydock Group 3 (good); major player back down in trip.

quotes.race: BetMGM Challenge Cup (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: I couldn't believe going into the two how well Volterra was going. I didn't want to get caught napping, so I gave him a squeeze and he didn't half pick up. I can't thank [owner] Sheikh Mohammed Obaid enough for putting me on. He could have used anyone but had confidence in me. Ascot is the premiership of racing and it's lovely to get a winner here. He's getting stronger and I definitely think he's a black-type horse going forward - Shane Gray, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He was quite keen early on when he won on his comeback at Newmarket but won going away. The Jersey would be a drop in trip and a step up in grade, but it takes a Group horse to win the Britannia as well and he'd go down there with a big shout. He's a horse we've always thought a lot of. 10-06-24

runners.comment: AW spell culminated in Listed win; has failed to transfer that form to turf since
runners.spotlight: Started his career with a progressive spell on AW that culminated in Listed success; has failed to transfer that form to turf since and may be more interesting when returned to an artificial surface.

quotes.race: Gamble Responsibly At BetUK Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Rebel's Gamble was really more professional today. The first day at Southwell he was very green and babyish, and today he's sharpened up a lot from that first run. He picked up and put the race to bed pretty quick - Clifford Lee, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He's had a little break since winning the Listed Spring Cup at Lingfield and we're preparing him for the Pavilion Stakes at Ascot at the end of the month. That will tell us if he wants six or seven furlongs on turf but I think he's plenty quick enough for a stiff six like Ascot. He's going to be an exciting horse for later in the season. 11-04-25

runners.comment: Latest effort took his record to 11413; ties in with Spy Chief; progressive contender
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; ran well at the Ebor festival when bidding for a York handicap (1m) double, taking form figures to 11413; ties in with Spy Chief on Jersey Stakes (7f) running; progressive contender.

quotes.race: John Smith's Racing Handicap
quotes.quote: Getting weight for age in this race obviously helped a little bit but it's always harder taking on the older horses. Stepping up to a mile was probably always the plan for Remmooz, he obviously ran a very nice race at Ascot - though they probably went a stride too quick for him in that [over seven furlongs in the Jersey Stakes] and got a little bit too far back probably. Today we had a nice draw and a nice horse in Kevin Ryan's to aim at but our horse lay down really nicely and hit the line strongly. The step up to a mile was definitely a plus and he's probably a Group horse in the making - Kevin Stott, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Below par over C&D last time; progressive otherwise; ran well in the Jersey and July Cup
runners.spotlight: All turf starts on good to firm; took his first step backwards in the Hungerford Stakes over C&D most recently; ran well in the Jersey Stakes and July Cup, and holds a leading chance provided he rebounds.

quotes.race: Jersey Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: A great run, Spy Chief is an inexperienced horse coming from a novice win at Yarmouth. He's still learning and still needs to relax a bit. I think the more racing he does, the better he will get - Rab Havlin, rider.

runners.comment: Irish mare who won a handicap in June; balance of form leaves her with difficult task
runners.spotlight: Irish mare who landed a Curragh handicap in June; seemed to run well in Listed grade on penultimate start but failed to back up that form latest; balance of form leaves her with difficult task.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: It's lovely to get my first Flat winner out of the way. Bellaphina had two nice runs under her belt and we knew she had a lot of improvement in her, but the ground was a concern. She had sore shins after her last run and we will see how she comes out of this, ideally she would want better ground - John Andrew Kinsella - trainer.

racecards.verdict: Spy Chief is set for an interesting rematch with Remmooz. VOLTERRA is the other main player on these terms.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Remmooz,  Spy Chief, 11/4 Volterra, 9/1 Ferrous, 12/1 Rebel's Gamble, 16/1 Short Final,  Bellaphina

racecards.race_name: Dubai Duty Free Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Looked out of sorts on all three appearances this year; needs to turn a corner
runners.spotlight: Very well handicapped on some of last season's form and will be suited by any lingering juice in the ground here but has looked out of sorts on all three appearances this year; others have much less to prove.

runners.comment: Close sixth in competitive 1m2f handicap at Glorious Goodwood; looks ready for 1m4f now
runners.spotlight: Ran well in competitive races on both handicap starts, more recently when close sixth of 14 at Glorious Goodwood (1m2f, good) in late July; proven over 1m3f and shapes as though today's trip will suit; must be considered.

runners.comment: Ran well in August and recent defeat on heavy ground is excused; others preferred, though
runners.spotlight: Gave it a good shot from the front when second at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last month and her subsequent defeat on heavy ground can be overlooked but others in this field appeal more all the same.

runners.comment: Progressive 3yo who made the frame in valuable fillies' handicaps on last two outings
runners.spotlight: Came good with two wins in June and has made the frame in two valuable 1m4f fillies' handicaps since (both on good to firm); probably still on the upgrade, and will be a tough nut to crack here if the new cheekpieces have any positive effect.

runners.comment: Lacked the pace to land a significant blow when sixth of seven on handicap debut
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner in April (1m3f); lacked the pace to land a significant blow when sixth of seven on last month's handicap debut at Newmarket (1m6f, good to firm) and subsequent 2lb drop is not enough to tempt.

runners.comment: Collected two easy wins (1m5f/2m) when cheekpieces were added last month; well held since
runners.spotlight: Took form to whole new level when fitted with cheekpieces in August, collecting two easy wins at Lingfield (2m/1m5f); raced too freely and was disappointing at Goodwood (1m6f) last time but further progress is not out of the question if this drop back to 1m4f suits.

runners.comment: Useful hurdler; could quite plausibly be on a good mark for first Flat start since 2022
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who posted a career-best performance when collecting a good prize at Cheltenham in April; unraced on the Flat since two down-the-field finishes in France in 2022 but could quite plausibly be on a good mark.

quotes.race: Catesby Estates Mares' Challenger Series Final Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Greyval has been great. She was a good juvenile and then we lost her a bit last season. She's very fast at home but isn't the quickest on the track. I've forgiven Johnny for the two times she's been beaten at Stratford! - Fergal O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Fillies' Juvenile Hurdle (Listed Race) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Greyval was superb value at 36,000euros and I loved the stamp of her. She wasn't going to be in the Triumph but we popped her in this week, but I doubt she'll go for that. I don't do social runners but we can dream. She's in the Mares' Novice and I half prefer that, but there's a lot of water and bridges - Nick Brown, racing manager of the owners Oakley Partnership.

runners.comment: Second to odds-on favourites on last two outing; open to improvement in handicaps
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only three starts and ran well in defeat behind odds-on favourites on her last two outings (both 1m4f); should have some improvement to come in handicaps and features prominently in calculations.

racecards.verdict: This might go to ORIONIS, who was one of two to pull a long way clear of the others when second at Salisbury last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Orionis, 3/1 Quebella, 4/1 El Megeeth, 7/1 Harpsichord, 8/1 Greyval, 12/1 Lalique,  Autumn's Breeze, 25/1 Flash Bardot

racecards.race_name: Fitzdares Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Glorious Goodwood winner last year; wind surgery ahead of this stable debut; could revive
runners.spotlight: Looked to be going places when winning a Glorious Goodwood handicap (1m2f, good to firm) last year but he missed the rest of that season; nothing to shout about in four starts this term but a change of scenery (has left William Haggas) may do some good and it's interesting his owners are sticking with him; reduced mark, wind surgery and first-time hood are other reasons why a revival could be on the cards.

quotes.race: Coral Kincsem Handicap
quotes.quote: Approval would have been unlucky if he got beat. He does drift a little right anyway but he did well to win and accepted being dropped in. He seemed to enjoy the track and probably found it quite entertaining - Maureen Haggas, assistant trainer, talking to ITV Racing.

runners.comment: Backed before finishing second last time; winner has won again since; respected
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner on French soil; ran to market expectations (rank outsider of seven) in June on British debut but it was a different story, having attracted support, when second of eight at Sandown (1m2f, good) the following month; winner went in again next time; respected.

runners.comment: Won over C&D in April; off since June but has gone well after a break before
runners.spotlight: Admirable handicapper who is also prone to the odd off day and showed both sides of his coin in two 14-runner C&D handicaps this year, winning in April but only ninth in May; solid third of eight at Sandown when last seen in June; has gone well after a break.

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Millennium Millionaire Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: Sweet Reward has had a lovely winter break and it's so nice to see these older horses come back year after year. - Jonathan Portman, trainer.

quotes.race: William Hill Pick Your Places Handicap
quotes.quote: It's a shame Sweet Reward has won so far because it's rather flattered him. These older need a bit of help sometimes and they go and do that and the handicapper thinks they've improved a stone. He's getting on and you have to get what you can, when you can. You never know what you're going to get from him. He doesn't like it firm and he doesn't like it heavy - Jonathan Portman, trainer.

quotes.race: Sandown Park Breeders' Day September 15 Handicap
quotes.quote: Sweet Reward seems to like it here. He is tough, brave and he stays and Rob Hornby knows how to ride him. He doesn't like sharp or undulating tracks but this track seems to bring out the best in him. It is great for the Old Stoic Racing Club. I was worried about him staying the mile and a quarter on soft ground last time but I had none of those worries today - Jonathan Portman, trainer.

quotes.race: Play Coral "Racing-Super-Series" For Free Handicap (Jockey Club Grassroots Middle Dist' Series Qual)
quotes.quote: Sweet Reward's ideap trip is 1m1f but he did stay today. I worried he wouldn't stay on the ground. He likes to get his toe in and is very brave. It took him a couple of races to blow the cobwebs away after his winter break - Jonathan Portman, trainer.

quotes.race: Unibet New Instant Roulette Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: I thought Sweet Reward would cope best of my runners on this ground and Rob [Hornby] gave him a super ride at Sandown last time when the horse got his confidence. I wasn't in a rush to put him in an apprentice race but was keen to get him to Newbury, which is a lovely track, nice and straightforward, while Tyler is a good chap. The handicapper can get hysterical when something wins that far and if he does that, it's going to be very dull. He's a horse who has developed nicely and I knew he'd be a slow-burner. If we race him conservatively he could improve again next year; he's still developing. I always thought nine furlongs was ideal and ten would be okay on certain tracks. He reminds me very much of Balmoral Castle, whom I used to train - Jonathan Portman, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was set to run the week before Goodwood, but he had a setback that's still affecting him. Hopefully he'll be out before the end of the season, but he's slightly looking like racing is catching up with him. He's having a bit of a pause and we'll try to get him back out before the ground gets too bad. 20-08-25

runners.comment: 2 AW wins this spring; didn't transfer that to turf; off since May; needs market check
runners.spotlight: Couple of AW wins this spring but didn't transfer that form to turf in two runs shortly after; has been off since May; needs a market check.

quotes.race: Win £1M With BetMGM's Golden Goals Handicap
quotes.quote: We didn't go crazy fractions and managed to get a nice position. San Juanito was stepping up in trip and it brought about a better performance. He quickened up for a lot longer and seems to be improving. He's going to be a better horse on turf -Rab Havlin, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: 2-2; goes up in trip for this handicap debut with opening mark fair; leading contender
runners.spotlight: 8-1 and came from the rear when beating eight others in a Nottingham novice (8.5f, good to soft) on debut in July; flipped that round when making most to defy a penalty in Southwell novice (1m, AW; 5-4) last month; goes up in trip for this handicap debut off a fair opening mark.

runners.comment: Was Oaks candidate but Listed sixth (soft) in June has been the best she can manage; risky
runners.spotlight: Potential Oaks candidate at the start of the year but her Listed sixth (soft) in June has been the best she can manage from three starts; heavy defeat on her handicap debut (also Goodwood, 1m2f, good) in July; risks attached but she still needs a market check with Rossa Ryan taking over.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We always regarded Janey Mackers quite a bit, but it was really eyecatching. It's the race I started Wonderful Tonight in and she finished third. You can't get too carried away, but that's her last run of the season and she'll have to be entered in the nice middle-distance races next year. I'm not sure we'll enter in the 1,000 Guineas but I'm pretty sure she'll be entered in the Diane and the Oaks. I've had a lot of success with the sire New Bay and she was good from day one at the sales - David Menuisier, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's in the Kincsem Handicap at Goodwood on Thursday. I won that race with Caius Chorister three years ago. She handles Goodwood, seems really well in herself and I just hope the ground is decent for her. There's more to come from her this season. 30-07-25

runners.comment: Three wins & no worse than 4th in eight starts this year; h'capper may be in charge now
runners.spotlight: No worse than fourth in eight starts this year, including three wins, the latest at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) in July; respectable efforts in three subsequent starts, although the handicapper isn't budging.

stable_tour.quote: He was one of the few failures I've had, until this year. When we've been lucky enough to get hold of nice horses from other yards we've usually done very well with them. He was a three-year-old at Joseph O'Brien's and won his maiden and, when he came to me, he'd been gelded and was quite light. I was hoping to sweeten him up and have a good campaign last year, but it's taken me a whole season to get him back to form, which he's done with a vengeance, winning three times. He's looking good now and ran a cracking race at Windsor last time in a very competitive Racing League contest. There should be more to come from him but when you win three in one year, it's hard to win the fourth. 27-08-25

runners.comment: Flopped when chasing quick-fire hat-trick in the summer; rested since; may bounce back
runners.spotlight: Improved to win twice within a week in July (good) and her hat-trick attempt was foiled when favourite but eighth of nine at Newmarket (also 1m2f, good to firm) shortly afterwards; possibly was feeling the effects of three quick starts and no surprise if she bounced back after a break.

runners.owner: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell
runners.comment: Below recent form when fifth of six at Leicester (1m2f, good) last Tuesday; same mark
runners.spotlight: Two comfortable wins on good to firm in July included a success over C&D; creditable third at Windsor next time but bit below that form when only fifth of six at Leicester (also 1m2f, good) last Tuesday; same mark.

runners.comment: Wind surgery & back on track when beaten about 3l in two 1m AW h'cap last month; possible
runners.spotlight: Wind surgery and back on track when beaten about 3l in two 1m Kempton handicaps (AW) in August; unexposed on turf after just three starts; cheekpieces join his regular tongue-tie.

runners.comment: Promising start for yard when 33-1 but third over C&D (good) three weeks ago; considered
runners.spotlight: Made all in a small-field maiden at Bath (1m, firm) in May and best form when second on handicap debut at Sandown (1m, good to firm) the following month; left Andrew Balding for 35,000gns after two lesser efforts in July and it was a promising start for this yard when 33-1 but third of nine over C&D (good) three weeks ago; considered.

runners.comment: Improved form for this yard; close 2nd in July; won last month; of interest on h'cap debut
runners.spotlight: Improved form for James Tate and she followed a narrow defeat in a Nottingham novice in July by opening her account in a Ripon maiden (also 1m2f, good) last month; opening mark looks workable and she's of interest on this handicap debut.

racecards.verdict: Plenty to consider but it may be worth forgiving GLORYOUS her latest start and she's taken to resume her progress back from a break.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Vincent Rocks, 7/2 Final Secret, 7/1 Alcaraz, 8/1 Approval, 10/1 Gloryous, 12/1 Sweet Reward,  Shady Bay, 14/1 San Juanito,  Just An Hour,  Zoffandia, 16/1 Star Of Dubai, 20/1 Janey Mackers

racecards.race_name: High Gosforth Park Golf Club Handicap

runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who returned from a break with a close third here last month; respected
runners.spotlight: Six-time hurdles winner and both Flat wins have come over C&D; his last win in this sphere was in March 2023 but he returned from a break with a close third over C&D three weeks ago; remains well treated on old form and is a big player if he can back that up; cheekpieces reapplied.

quotes.race: Top Odds Every Day With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: They put this extra meeting on and Russian Virtue has been a bit unlucky for us on the Flat, being placed plenty of times. So while the weather is not great for going hurdling we thought we'd give him a spin on the Flat and it's great that he's won a third race in a row. I hope he'll be one for the two-mile hurdle at the Northern Lights finals at Musselburgh. He may run at Sedgefield on Sunday and get him qualified - Rebecca Menzies, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: A fun dual-purpose horse who's won eight times for us, with six coming over hurdles and two on the Flat. He thrives on racing and his work at home, and he hasn't finished winning yet. We're keen to keep his mark similar to where it is now so he has a nice weight for the Go North two-mile hurdle series final, which he finished fourth in last year. He'll be lightly raced before that and we might even go back on the all-weather. 24-01-24

prev_owners.owner: D Fish And Mrs J Aldridge

runners.comment: Just one win from 21 starts and he's struggled this season; hood added
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner for Tom Ward last autumn but he's struggled this season and was beaten 12l when third of five at Carlisle (1m1f, good) last month; continues to drop down the weights but he's now 1-21 and others are preferred; hood added.

runners.comment: Regressive 14-race maiden and he needs new headgear to make a difference
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for Michael Bell and his third over 1m2f at Pontefract in June is his only effort of note for current yard; still unexposed on AW and mark continues to fall but he needs new headgear to make a difference.

runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who has failed to beat a rival in last two starts and has lots to prove
runners.spotlight: Generally regressive ten-race maiden who has failed to beat a rival in his two starts; now 13lb lower than his opening mark but has lots to prove after another short break; cheekpieces worn on last two starts are removed.

prev_owners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 51 & Mrs E Burke

runners.comment: Won at Catterick in May; up 5lb but she's open to more progress on return for new yard
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at sixth attempt when landing a four-runner handicap at Catterick (1m4f, good) in May; left Karl Burke since that narrow success but she held off a triple subsequent winner from the Prescott yard and is open to more progress on this drop back in trip; respected off 5lb higher on stable debut.

runners.comment: 0-11 but was close third over C&D three weeks ago; in the mix with blinkers now tried
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but he gave it a good shot off this reduced mark when a close third over C&D three weeks ago; wore cheekpieces last time and has claims if he can repeat that form with blinkers now tried.

stable_tour.quote: He just got beat at Newcastle last month and, while he's low-grade, he should definitely win. His best runs have been on the all-weather or slow ground. He could make into a nice dual-purpose horse. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Won with authority at Wolverhampton; up 5lb but he's a big player again back up in trip
runners.spotlight: Made his breakthrough (tenth attempt) when justifying support off a reduced mark at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) 13 days ago; back up 5lb but he finished well to score with authority last time and is a big player again on this step back up in trip.

runners.comment: 0-12 but she's reached the frame on turf in last two starts and is not ruled out
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but she's finished close up with her frame efforts at Redcar (1m handicap) and Ripon (1m2f classified) in last two starts; nudged up 2lb but has each-way claims if she can transfer her turf form back to AW.

runners.comment: Big prices and has finished in rear in all six starts including three handicaps
runners.spotlight: Big prices and has finished in rear in all six starts, including three handicaps; dropped another 4lb but she needs a transformation back up in trip.

stable_tour.quote: She works really well at home and should go well. She needs 7f to a mile. She had her first run in a handicap at Carlisle on Monday. She's a lovely filly and a half-sister to the Britannia winner Khaloosy, but she was a May foal and is still a little bit weak. She's my dark horse. 21-05-25

runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo but she's finished a tailed-off last in all three handicaps
runners.spotlight: 3yo who is lightly raced but she's gone off at massive odds in three handicaps and has failed to beat a rival (1m4f-2m).

racecards.verdict: Preference is for DUFFUS CASTLE who justified support off a reduced mark at Wolverhampton and is a big player again back up in trip.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Duffus Castle, 3/1 Grizzieblanca, 5/1 Russian Virtue,  That Lucas Fella, 15/2 Heartened, 12/1 Cloud Free, 20/1 Toronto Raptor, 25/1 Muhib, 33/1 Miss Raincloud, 66/1 Bovey Belle

racecards.race_name: Ardor Bull Run Handicap

runners.comment: Scored at 28-1 over C&D last Thursday; 5lb penalty but this is a weak event
runners.spotlight: Won his fourth race at this track and third over C&D when scoring at 28-1 last Thursday on his return from a break; 5lb penalty doesn't leave him well in but this is a weak event.

quotes.race: BetUK Over 40,000 Live Streamed Races Handicap
quotes.quote: We didn't go that quick and it can't turn into a sprint with Quercus Robur, he needs everything to go smooth. The drop back in trip didn't suit him last time. Back up to two miles today was ideal. He's a bit quirky, going down to the start he was a bit reluctant but you just have to kid him into races a little bit - Kevin Stott, jockey.

runners.comment: Won (1m4f) in July; C&D mid-div in Racing League event next; this is considerably easier
runners.spotlight: Comfortable winner of a selling handicap at Beverley (1m4f) on the back of wind surgery in July; mid-division over C&D (40-1) in a Racing League event next time; this is considerably easier, to put it mildly.

runners.comment: 0-11 and modest form, beaten 5l in a 1m2f classified here last time; first go at this trip
runners.spotlight: 0-11 and modest form, beaten 5l in a 1m2f classified here (first-time tongue-tie; stays further) last time; first go at this trip.

runners.comment: Went down by a head at Ripon (2m, good to firm) 24 days ago; player in this company
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in a row over hurdles in the spring; hasn't run badly back on the Flat and went down by a head at Ripon (2m, good to firm) 24 days ago; a player in this company.

runners.comment: Beaten 16l (2m, good to firm) last time; has AW form; not ruled out back on this surface
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Ian Williams with two runner-up spots at this trip on AW in 2023; all starts on turf since and was second of seven at Catterick three starts ago; beaten 16l at Ripon (2m, good to firm; Lillistar just denied) last time; not entirely ruled out back on this surface.

runners.comment: 35-race maiden but good 2nd (1m4f, first-time cheekpieces) 24 days ago; this is weak
runners.spotlight: 35-race maiden (bumper/hurdles/Flat) but she posted a good effort when runner-up at Musselburgh (1m4f, first-time cheekpieces) 24 days ago; removal of headgear not an obvious positive but stays this trip and finds herself in a weak contest.

racecards.verdict: Quercus Robur is respected under a penalty but QUEEN ROSLYN will find this easier than the Racing League event contested last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Quercus Robur, 5/2 Lillistar, 5/1 Queen Roslyn,  Star Of Markinch, 8/1 Team Endeavour, 12/1 Parish Record

racecards.race_name: Jimbo Thompson Bluesky EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Modest form in two 6f events in recent weeks; likely a stiffer test is required
runners.spotlight: She has run to a modest level in two runs in 6f events (good to firm/Polytrack) in recent weeks; shapes as though she will be seen to better effect when faced with more of a stamina test.

runners.comment: £32,000 half-sister to two winners out of a 1m winner; yard in form; betting to guide
runners.spotlight: £32,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Lawful Command (7f/1m including 2yo; RPR 98) and Electric Blue (5f-7f including AW/Qatar; 78); dam 1m winner (77), out of 1,000 Guineas third; hooded for debut; stable in form and she's worth a market check.

runners.comment: Sister to three winners out of a useful 2yo winner; yard's 2yo strike-rate down this year
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to winners Key To Cotai (Listed-placed 5f 2yo/7f; RPR 97), Cotai Hero (6.5f/7f including 2yo/AW/France/Italy; 75) and Cotai Pearl (6f/6.5f including 2yo/Qatar); dam 5f-7f 2yo winner (85); stable strike-rate with 2yos is down this year but she makes enough appeal on paper to warrant a market check.

runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners; nice pedigree but yard just 2-80 with AW 2yos in last 5 years
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to winners Townsend Manor (5f-6f including 2yo/Qatar; RPR 86) and Marry The Night (1m; 66); dam a 6f AW winner (82) from the family of Irish Derby winner Westover; starts out at a modest level and her yard is having a good year; just 2-80 with AW 2yos in the last five years, however.

runners.comment: Improved when 3rd at Carlisle ten days ago; work to do with Gwen's Girl on that run
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on her Haydock debut but she fared better when keeping on for third, beaten the thick end of 5l, at Carlisle ten days ago (6f, soft); finished behind the reopposing Gwen's Girl on that occasion and needs to improve again to take this.

runners.comment: Dam a 5f/6f winner; should have a future but only of interest if backed on debut
runners.spotlight: Second foal of a 5f/6f winner (including AW; RPR 77), herself a half-sister to winners Dark Thunder (7f) and Cat Royale (8.6f-11.4f); only of interest if backed.

runners.comment: 50-1 and green when a well-beaten sixth on her Lingfield debut (6f, AW) 16 days ago
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and ran green when sixth of 11, beaten just under 7l, on her Lingfield debut 16 days ago (6f, AW); big step forward required.

runners.owner: E Holmes, M Graham And Partner
runners.comment: Promising 2nd on Carlisle debut ten days ago (6f, soft); open to improvement; contender
runners.spotlight: Shaped nicely when second of ten on her Carlisle debut ten days ago (6f, soft), looking likely to drop away early in the straight but keeping on nicely in the final furlong (Thornaby Annie back in third); open to progress and she's high on the list.

runners.comment: 4,000euros half-sister to two winners; stable just 6-72 with 2yos in the last five seasons
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling; half-sister to winners Mansalu (7f; RPR 83) and Diamine (Italian 1m2f); dam a maiden half-sister to Italian Oaks winner Menhoubah; stable just 6-72 with 2yos in the last five seasons.

runners.comment: Too free upped to 7f last month; earlier placed efforts bring her into the reckoning
runners.spotlight: Second and third starts (6f, Tapeta and good) bring her into the reckoning but she dropped right away at Yarmouth when upped to 7f last month (raced too freely); has a point to prove after that.

runners.comment: Did plenty wrong on Windsor debut but kept on nicely for fourth; open to improvement
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and slowly away but recovered to finish fourth of 12 on last month's debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm; beaten 5l); this half-sister to three winners is open to improvement and not ruled out.

runners.comment: Three promising efforts over further last month; quick enough for 6f; high on the list
runners.spotlight: Three runs last month, all over further, and she showed a fair amount of ability on each occasion; the drop to 6f should be in her favour and she's high on the list.

runners.comment: Useful pedigree and starts out in a modest event; no surprise to see her feature
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners including Mountain Brave (6f/7f including AW; RPR 100), Lucky Dip (6f-1m including AW; 90) and Delft (6f/7f AW; 87); dam 7f winner (91), half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Wunders Dream and 1m Group 3 winner Grecian Dancer; lots to like on paper and no surprise to see her feature.

racecards.verdict: Ten Sixty Six looks worth a crack at sprinting but GWEN'S GIRL may be the answer with her promising debut behind her.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Ten Sixty Six, 4/1 Gwen's Girl, 7/1 Startled Lady, 8/1 Sweet Echo,  Zarinca, 9/1 Thornaby Annie, 12/1 Aly A'Ali,  Clementines Star,  Cotai Starlight,  Storm Esme, 20/1 All Good, 25/1 Mereside Princess, 33/1 Control Room

racecards.race_name: Border Minstrel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Stepped up to 7f at Kempton and went down by under 2l after racing prominently
runners.spotlight: Stepped up to 7f on Kempton AW recently and went down by under 2l after racing prominently; this may require more from him but he's having only his third start.

runners.comment: 7f rather than 1m could be a better fit for now and he has strong form claims
runners.spotlight: Good pedigree; has advanced his RPRs with each start, last time appearing not to quite last home over 1m on handicap debut at Southwell; 7f could be a better fit for now and he has strong form claims.

runners.comment: Both races were won by the same horse and he finished closer at Doncaster last time
runners.spotlight: Both races to date were won by the same horse and he finished closer at Doncaster (7f) last time, beaten 2.5l in a first-time tongue-tie; may need to find more again but that's feasible.

runners.comment: Completely tailed off over 1m at Southwell one month ago when 80-1
runners.spotlight: Completely tailed off over 1m at Southwell one month ago when 80-1.

runners.owner: T S Ingham & Mrs Liz Ingham
runners.comment: 45,000euros yearling; second foal; half-sister to 1m2f winner Dolce Far Niente (RPR 60)
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros yearling; second foal; half-sister to 1m2f winner Dolce Far Niente (RPR 60); dam unplaced 6f (54), sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Sir Prancealot; would be a surprise winner on debut.

runners.breeder: A S Denniff & Makabi Investments Uc
runners.comment: In a first-time hood when close third in a 7f novice at Haydock in May
runners.spotlight: First-time hooded when close third in a 7f novice at Haydock in May; has been off for a while but clearly up to winning a race of this nature.

racecards.verdict: With just about the best form on show and returning to 7f, GREAT DREAM is preferred to Fierce Fortitude.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/8 Great Dream, 2/1 Thaisa, 3/1 Fierce Fortitude, 8/1 Potomac River, 50/1 Nanyuki, 200/1 The Grand

racecards.race_name: Mini Golf At High Gosforth Park Handicap

runners.comment: 3-8 on AW; all starts on turf this year; second in July was not backed up last time
runners.spotlight: 3-8 on AW, the wins coming when she rattled off a nursery hat-trick at Wolverhampton (8.5f/9.5f) on Tapeta around this time two years ago; mostly kept to turf since and she looked to be finding her level again, following wind surgery, when second of nine in a Redcar amateur riders' race (1m, good) in July; didn't build on that when beaten 15l next time, though, and others are more persuasive.

runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd And David Buist
runners.comment: Course winner; good 2nd last month wasn't backed up but not ruled out at this venue
runners.spotlight: Course winner (7f) in late September 2023; also placed at 1m in last two starts here; came back from a small break with good second of ten at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm) last month but didn't back it up when fifth in a Carlisle amateur riders' race (1m, good; Kaaress behind) 18 days ago; not ruled out back here, though.

stable_tour.quote: A lovely, big horse who hasn't run a bad race all year. He's no superstar but at his level he's very consistent. The only issue I have with him is he's a massive horse who's vlight-framed and it's hard to keep condition on him. His last two runs have been very good and he'll be winning sooner rather than later. 26-07-24

prev_owners.owner: Kennet Valley T'Breds Ix - Desert Raider

runners.comment: Running well here, 2nd (7f) last month then third (1m) four weeks later; much respected
runners.spotlight: Both wins on Polytrack (7f/1m) when making all in first-time headgear; currently running well here, however, and he followed a 40-1 second (7f) at the start of last month with a respectable third of 14 (1m) four weeks later; should give it another good go.

runners.comment: 4-time AW winner, twice here (7f/1m); signs he was on the way back last time; considered
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, twice here (7f/1m); struggled off his revised rating since winning at Southwell (1m, Tapeta) in April but there were signs he might be on the way back, despite finishing ninth, when not getting the breaks over C&D three weeks ago; far from dismissed even though the handicapper hasn't budged.

runners.comment: Beat two home when 50-1 for stable/handicap debut a fortnight ago; check the market
runners.spotlight: Best qualifying run for Iain Jardine came at Ayr (1m, good) but it was still modest stuff; 50-1 for stable/handicap debut when beating two home at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) a fortnight ago; still early days, however, and he'll require a market check.

runners.comment: Solid start for yard when fourth of 14 over C&D three weeks ago; candidate to step forward
runners.spotlight: Just five runs and it was a solid start for this yard when 12-1 and fourth of 14 over C&D three weeks ago; shaped like that first start in 89 days may have just been needed and he's a candidate to step forward.

runners.comment: Regressive form and first-time cheekpieces (retained) didn't provide the answer latest
runners.spotlight: 7f winner last term for previous yard; weak claims on this year's regressive form and first-time cheekpieces (retained) didn't provide the answer at Ayr (1m2f) last time.

quotes.race: John Sharp Memorial EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Three Runs Or Less) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Jachin is going to be a better three-year-old. The ground was a concern but he stayed on well and was going away from them. He's been mentally immature but he'll take a big step forward from that and he'll have a nice winter break now - Craig Lister, trainer.

runners.comment: Hinted something was coming when 6th on h'cap debut; tongue-tie and cheekpieces go on
runners.spotlight: Promising debut when sixth of 11 here (7f) in June; hasn't matched that since but there was a faint whiff something better could be coming when keeping on for sixth on handicap debut at Carlisle one month ago; tongue-tie and cheekpieces go on for just his fifth start.

runners.comment: Best qualifying run here (7f); needs a market check for this handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Encouraging that his best qualifying run came here (7f) when seventh of 11, despite being slowly away, in June; dwelt last time too but he'll need a market check for this handicap debut.

runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club
runners.comment: Convincing first success (C&D) 11 days ago; 1lb well in under the penalty; big player
runners.spotlight: Attracted support and it was a convincing breakthrough (at the 23rd attempt) when having her first go at 1m over C&D 11 days ago; led the main group against the near rail that day and she's well drawn to adopt similar tactics; 1lb well in under the penalty; big player.

runners.comment: Well held last 3 starts but has run well here before and is back to her winning mark
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 11th attempt when scoring at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) in May, but she's been well held all three subsequent starts; on the plus side she's run well here before and is back to that winning mark.

stable_tour.quote: I think she was a bit unlucky on her return at Newcastle at the end of March as she got a little bit squeezed out, so I'm hoping that with a run under her belt she'll be seen to better effect next time. She has entries at Haydock and Doncaster on Saturday. 23-04-25

racecards.verdict: Sassy Glory should rightly prove popular but the vote goes to YAFAARR, whose recent fourth on stable debut looks a run to build on.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Sassy Glory, 4/1 Desert Raider, 9/2 Yafaarr, 7/1 Tees George, 9/1 Water Tiger, 10/1 Spirit Of Bowland, 12/1 Kaaress, 16/1 Oh So Cool, 20/1 Dutch Duke,  Jachin,  Rose Of New Jersey

racecards.race_name: Border Minstrel Sunday Lunch Handicap

runners.comment: Two 7f wins for Karl Burke as a 2yo; not progressing but lurks on a dangerous mark
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f for Karl Burke as a 2yo; not much to celebrate since but his stable debut in January (C&D) wasn't without hope and he's now 10lb lower; not solid but a big run wouldn't be a bolt from the blue.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Respectful jumped very smartly from a wide draw and I was able to just tack across. I didn't think I was going that quickly and we got an easy lead in front and he's improved a hell of a lot from his first couple of runs. He did well to stick it out when the Charlie Johnston horse came up, I thought he was going to go by me, but he was tough and did it nicely. His improvement has surprised me a little. He would have learned a lot from Catterick the last day when finishing behind a nice stablemate in Ice Max and didn't seem to mind the ground here, he's won nicely in the end - Sam James, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: I like him. He wasn't beaten far in two runs over 6f on the all-weather in the winter. He was staying on when he didn't get the best of runs at Carlisle this week and he's probably on a mark he can win off now. We may step him back up to 7f but he shows plenty of speed at home. 14-08-25

runners.comment: Positive impression when winning at Ayr in July; still of interest but needs a fast pace
runners.spotlight: Overcame an awkward start to win a 16-runner handicap at Ayr in good style in July (6f, good to soft); unplaced twice since but suspicion remains that she is still well treated; whether this will set up favourably for her remains to be seen.

runners.comment: Promising seasonal debut but he hasn't built on it since; losing run set to continue
runners.spotlight: Went close on his seasonal return at Pontefract in June (6f, good to firm); backwards steps since then and losing run looks set to continue.

stable_tour.quote: He's very tough but has been a bit unlucky to win only one of his last four races. He might be holding a little bit back for himself and I may put cheekpieces on him for his next start just to try to get that little bit more out of him. He runs at Newmarket on Friday, which looks like it'll suit him. 27-07-22

runners.owner: Grange Park Racing Xviii And Ruth Carr

prev_owners.owner: Grange Park Racing XVIII & OFO Partners

runners.comment: Met trouble when 2nd over C&D last week; handicapped to win; strong claims down in class
runners.spotlight: Tumbled down the weights this year and looks poised to capitalise soon judged on his C&D second last week, a run where he didn't enjoy a clean passage; due to be 1lb higher from the weekend; strong claims down in class.

runners.comment: Three C&D wins this year, the latest 11 days ago; 1lb well in under a penalty; respected
runners.spotlight: He was recording his third C&D win of the year when getting the better of Bazball 11 days ago; this looks a stronger race but he's respected nevertheless under a 4lb penalty (1lb ahead of the handicapper).

runners.comment: Modest form thus far but down in weights and return to 6f looks sure to suit; considered
runners.spotlight: Her dam has produced a couple of smart performers and although she's shown just modest form in her six starts, she has slipped down the weights and looks sure to benefit from the return to 6f; still has time to do better.

runners.comment: Placed on last two starts, behind Asadjumeirah over C&D latest; needs more to win
runners.spotlight: Placed efforts on her last two starts, latterly behind Asadjumeirah over C&D; may have to make do with more minor money.

runners.comment: Latest Southwell 4th has been franked since; suspicion an even bigger effort is lurking
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all 13 starts but has hinted at ability and has dropped a long way in the weights; latest Southwell fourth was a solid effort though and the winner and third have won since; still has a bigger effort in him.

runners.comment: Poor strike-rate; creditable fourth on return from a break but this is a stronger race
runners.spotlight: Her only win from 24 starts came in a 6f classified event at Thirsk last August; returned from a 145-day break with a creditable fourth at Southwell two weeks ago; needs more in this stronger race though.

runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden; of interest on her best form though & needs market check on stable debut
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in Ireland but she did show promise, notably when flying home for second in a big field at Navan 12 months ago; she hasn't found her best in three runs this year but this is far less competitive than the races she is used to contesting and she's on a lowly mark; no surprise to see better on her first run for Iain Jardine.

racecards.verdict: Pancake can go well but INVITORIO (nap) is handicapped to win and he shaped with plenty of promise when second here last week.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Invitorio, 9/2 Asadjumeirah, 6/1 Bazball, 8/1 Respectful,  Pancake,  Captain Pickles, 10/1 South Road, 12/1 She'sashambles, 14/1 Little Mi Mi, 16/1 Golden Duke

racecards.race_name: ARC All-Weather £1Million Bonus Returns Handicap

runners.comment: Placed over C&D and at Carlisle on last two starts; easy lead unlikely; vulnerable late on
runners.spotlight: Two course wins to his name; placed over C&D and at Carlisle on his last two starts; unlikely to enjoy an easy lead with Saisons d'Or in the field and he could prove vulnerable late on.

prev_owners.owner: Rasio Cymru 1 & Windmill House Tb Ltd

runners.comment: Met trouble when fourth at Chelmsford last week; well treated; strong claims
runners.spotlight: On a dangerous mark and he did well to finish as close as he did at Chelmsford last week (just behind Mister Mojito), having to wait for a gap at a crucial stage; mixed record at this track but has run some big races here and this is much weaker than previous C&D runs.

stable_tour.quote: He was placed in the Buckingham Palace for previous connections and has dropped down the handicap now. He had a few runs for us on slower ground but he wants it faster and could be well handicapped for the summer. He runs at Chelmsford on Thursday. 04-05-23

runners.comment: Closely matched with Riot, who was also unlucky, at Chelmsford last week; contender
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate and confirmed his wellbeing when keeping on well for third at Chelmsford last week, meeting trouble and nearest at the finish; closely matched with Riot, who also had a miserable trip through that Chelmsford event, and he shaped well in one previous run on Tapeta.

quotes.race: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Handicap
quotes.quote: Mister Mojito is still improving. I was a little disappointed with him last time at Newmarket, I don't think the ground was an issue, I just think he ended up getting behind the wrong horse and tactically we got it wrong. He came out of the race really well and I would have been a little bit disappointed if he wasn't involved today. He doesn't like soft ground but other than that he's not let us down - Terry Kent, trainer, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Mixed bag in 2025; needs to raise his game to come out on top
runners.spotlight: Easy win in a 1m selling handicap at Thirsk last summer (for George Boughey); mixed bag for new yard this year and others make more appeal.

stable_tour.quote: He's a new arrival this year and we ran him twice quickly at the end of last month, when he was second at Wolverhampton and then fourth when favourite at Southwell. He's still a maiden but I'm sure we can get him winning. 04-04-24

runners.comment: Latest run not the one to judge him on and this two-time course winner is well treated now
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come here over 6f; a couple of promising turf efforts in July but he couldn't justify favouritism back here (7f) three weeks ago, not ideally positioned and meeting a bit of trouble; another 2lb lower today and he's capable off this mark.

runners.comment: All wins at 6f but stays this far; down in grade after struggling at Southwell latest
runners.spotlight: All wins at 6f but stays this far; good run at Wolverhampton last month and although last time wasn't so persuasive, it was a better race than this; not ruled out.

runners.comment: This year's two wins have come in Class 6 company; only fifth last time and more is needed
runners.spotlight: He has added another two wins to his haul this year, the latest at Catterick last month (7f, good to firm; Class 6); creditable fifth at Southwell two weeks ago; needs more this time.

quotes.race: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
quotes.quote: We were expecting a good run but were worried about the draw and the rain-softened ground. Saisons D'Or has proved his versatility and it opens up more options - Jedd O'Keeffe, trainer.

quotes.race: racingtv.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Saisons D'Or battled well to the line. He's a consistent horse and the mile suited him well here. He was a bit gassy as a youngster but settles much better now and that enables him to stay this trip - Jack Garritty, jockey.

racecards.verdict: Without a repeat of the traffic issues that plagued RIOT and Mister Mojito last week the former can turn the tables.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Riot, 100/30 Mister Mojito, 4/1 Flying Fletcher, 9/2 Blazing Son, 9/1 Sovereign Knight, 10/1 Saisons d'Or, 12/1 Yaajooz

racecards.race_name: ARC £1Million Bonus Has £150K Increase! Handicap

runners.comment: In top form and last week's impressive Musselburgh success leaves him 4lb well in; solid
runners.spotlight: Comes here in top form, winning a classified event at Wolverhampton last month and then taking a marked step forward when running away with a Musselburgh handicap last week (5f, good to soft); 4lb well in under his penalty and he has to be high on the list.

runners.comment: C&D winner; mixed bag this year and needs to bounce back from some modest efforts
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; up and down this season and his last two runs have been well short of his best; the drop into a 0-55 will help but others come here with more pressing claims.

runners.comment: A repeat of either of his last two AW runs would give him solid claims
runners.spotlight: Not fired on turf on last two starts but he had gone close on AW in his two preceding runs; should be in the thick of the action.

quotes.race: Read Katie Walsh On Betway Insider Handicap
quotes.quote: Newyorkstateofmind picked up for me in the straight, he's very honest and genuine. The gap opened up for us. He's been in great form lately - Ray Dawson.

stable_tour.quote: Three wins from 13 races last year was a nice return for this fellow, two on the all-weather and one on grass. Six of his nine wins have been on the all-weather and when it comes to the turf, he has to have theound fast. He was a bit disappointing when he returned to Wolverhampton from his winter break, but it is onwards and upwards. There's a race at Thirsk on Saturday that looks right for him. 09-04-25

prev_owners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock And Miss J R Macey

runners.comment: Capable at this level but far from straightforward; minor money perhaps his best hope
runners.spotlight: Won a weak race at Redcar last month, his first success at the 21st attempt; third over 6f here 11 days ago, again looking a hard ride; has the ability to feature but others are preferred for win purposes.

runners.comment: Bumped into one over C&D 11 days ago; should go well off the same mark
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but she comes here on the back of a good second over C&D 11 days ago, a race where the winner was a dark horse for the grade; should go well off the same mark.

stable_tour.quote: She's a lovely filly who's like a pet and very well behaved. She was very consistent over the winter, winning once at Newcastle and being placed there on three other occasions. Last time at Redcar she was drawn right out on the wing and got caught up in a bit of traffic. She hasn't won on turf yet but has had loads of seconds and I'm sure she'll win a little race. She'll run again soon. 21-05-25

runners.comment: C&D win off 8lb higher last September; heavy defeats on last three runs; risky
runners.spotlight: C&D win 12 months ago came off an 8lb higher mark; pick of this summer's form gives him claims but he has suffered heavy defeats on his last three starts; too much to prove for now.

quotes.race: Bresbet Rewards Loyalty Handicap
quotes.quote: Golden Rainbow has been brilliant for us. We picked this race out when he won here before and both that and his last run at Musselburgh have worked out. He's the only horse to beat Phil McEntee's Jacquelina on turf this year, who's now rated in the 80s and well thought of. He loves the fast ground and I hope he can continue to progress to pick up a couple more prizes in the Yorkshire area - Rebecca Menzies, trainer.

runners.comment: Beat Sanbona last month and better off with her old rival here; disappointed twice since
runners.spotlight: Made all to beat Sanbona at Redcar last month (5f, good to firm) and she's much better off at the weights today; two heavy defeats have followed, the latest at Redcar on Tuesday.

runners.comment: Return to AW in his favour; not much room for error off his current mark
runners.spotlight: Conditions no problem and he will appreciate getting back on AW after a couple of turf defeats; prominent racer who is likely to face some competition for the early lead.

runners.comment: On dangerous mark and this should be run to suit; likely to be arriving on the scene late
runners.spotlight: Has C&D form and he has slipped down the weights this summer; latest fourth at Wolverhampton was a bit more encouraging and this should be run to suit.

quotes.race: Know Your Limits With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: We were drawn badly so Anthony [Brittain, trainer] said to just tuck Rogue De Vega in and ride for a bit of luck. He's a little bit of a tricky horse but he enjoyed that, coming with one smooth run - Mark Winn, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: Another who never ran until April, but this son of Lope De Vega has already shown he has a bright future. We tried to buy him as a yearling but could not afford him, but we did eventually purchase him at the Goresbridge breeze-ups. After a debut second place he returned to Chelmsford for his second run and won nicely over 5f. For his last run we went to Bath where he met an in-form horse of George Boughey's yet still ran an encouraging race (second). We will be ready to go any time now and there is a glut of races he could run in so I will speak to Tony before deciding, but I believe he can step up again as he has with each of his three starts. 06-07-23

runners.comment: Disappointed on slow ground last time; earlier Carlisle 3rd gives him a squeak
runners.spotlight: Ran a good race at Carlisle on September 1 (5f, good) but failed to back it up on softer going one week later; others appeal more.

runners.comment: Latest 6f run excusable and he's fine on Tapeta; lots of other pace on show though
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; the return to 6f didn't have a positive effect at Catterick last time but he had finished third on Tapeta on his penultimate start; others look safer.

runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner
runners.comment: C&D winner; cheekpieces didn't help last time and now tried in blinkers; not solid
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; went close in a classified event over C&D last month but he failed to back it up in a handicap here 11 days ago; cheekpieces which were tried there are now replaced by first-time blinkers; not sure quite what to expect.

stable_tour.quote: Another who is bred to be both fast and early. The dam was a useful black-type two-year-old for us but so far hasn't produced anything. Hopefully by sending her to Blue Point she can enjoy some overdue success. I have him entered this week and again he has done plenty of work. 19-04-23

runners.comment: 3lb lower than for his C&D win in February; mixed record since but one to take seriously
runners.spotlight: Picked up smartly to win a C&D handicap in February and this 3lb lower mark should be within range; in and out since then but this is likely to be run to suit and he holds each-way claims at least.

quotes.race: Killashee Hotel Nursery Handicap (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Latin Five is a horse we thought might win his maiden, but this was the first time he really raced professionally. He loved the stiff six and should go the seven at some stage. We'll see what the handicapper does - Joseph O'Brien, trainer

stable_tour.quote: He's been a bit frustrating. He relies on the others to set the race up for him and he's been unlucky a few times. A gap didn't come at the right time for him at Beverley, then at Ripon the one in front of him came back and he had to switch round, which is never good for him as he's better going through them. When everything sets up perfectly he wins. 14-08-25

runners.owner: Mrs J Spivey & Mrs J Robinson

prev_owners.owner: Grid Iron Racing And Mrs J Spivey

runners.comment: 0-13 but she has shown winning potential, including over C&D; each-way shout
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but she has shown winning potential, including over C&D; respectable effort in a handicap here 11 days ago but she was behind Lady Of The Garr on that occasion.

racecards.verdict: Plenty of possibles but LADY OF THE GARR bumped into one here 11 days ago and she can get the better of Latin Five.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Sanbona, 6/1 Latin Five, 8/1 Lady Of The Garr,  Arlington, 10/1 Newyorkstateofmind,  Bibendum,  Northerner, 12/1 Rogue De Vega,  Dandy Fitz, 20/1 Let's Go Hugo,  Doralee,  Desert Master, 25/1 Golden Rainbow,  Bust A Moon

racecards.race_name: Colin Stratton Retirement Novices' Chase (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Ready winner of Class 2 handicap at Bangor this month, completing a chasing four-timer
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for ready success in Class 2 handicap at Bangor (3m, good) a fortnight ago and is now 4-4 over fences since cheekpieces were refitted in June; 2m5f on slower ground will also suit him; ought to be very hard to beat in current mood.

runners.comment: Front-runner; won two of his last three races but is not favoured by the weights here
runners.spotlight: Made all for comfortable success in Stratford handicap (2m1f, good) this month and has now won two of his last three; will be fine over today's longer trip and may gain a tactical advantage if allowed to dictate from the front but has quite at bit to find at the weights with the other two.

quotes.race: BetVictor Proud Sponsors Of Newbury Juvenile Hurdle (Nov' Championship Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We hoped In The Air would win and hopefully he'll improve for the run. He's still a baby, but his next run will be at Aintree in the Grade 1 juvenile. I shouldn't think he'd want extremes of ground and I think he can improve a bit. He's a horse for the future, a bit like Porticello, although he wouldn't be as big as him and I don't think he's quite up to that level yet. Gary Moore, trainer

stable_tour.quote: We got him from Adam West this summer and he's started the campaign very well. He won easily on his first run for us at Southwell and got put up 11lb. He won with a 7lb penalty the following week at Exeter and might run again over the weekend. I'm looking forward to seeing what we achieve with him in the future. 22-10-24

Another recent purchase out of Nicky Henderson's from the Olly Harris dispersal. He's a five-time winner and might be a bit of fun and one we aim at the Summer Plate. He's got bits of good form and was a cheap purchase at £15,000 - he might have slipped through the radar. 19-02-25

runners.comment: Developed into very useful hurdler last season; faces bang-in-form rival on chase debut
runners.spotlight: Developed into very useful hurdler last season and was excellent third in Pertemps Final at Cheltenham (3m, good to soft) in March; signed off with another very good effort at Aintree's Grand National meeting but takes on a bang-in-form rival on this chasing debut.

quotes.race: Pertemps Network Final Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Going to the last for a moment I thought we were going to get there, but Catch Him Derry has run so well and taken his owners on a phenomenal journey. If you look back where he started off, which was 96, he has come a long way. He looked very good at Ayr on okay ground and today was a career best on okay ground. While he is effective on slower ground you can't say he didn't act on it today as he has run unbelievably well - Dan Skelton, trainer.

quotes.race: Pertemps Network Handicap Hurdle (Pertemps Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Catch Him Derry just needed it at Cheltenham last time, which is unusual for one of ours, but we've done a lot of work with him since and he's improved. Hopefully we can get into the race at Cheltenham - Harry Skelton, jockey.

quotes.race: Celebrating David Freeman's 35 Years Service Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: I'm delighted with Major Fortune and Catch Him Derry [his two winners]. They won a few races each last year. I thought they'd both need the run today and this was the first chance I've had to run them on some slower ground because it's been flooded down here the last few meetings. They were both very much suited by the ground, but hopefully they can continue to improve.
