It's early season form on a testing surface so I'm not sure what we'll do with them next, but they're going the right way - Dan Skelton, trainer.

quotes.race: Get Home Safe With Thistle Cabs Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: The pair of them pulled clear last time and it was obviously good form. Catch Him Derry has come an awful long way. He was a bit of a wayward baby but he hasn't half made up for it. He'll have a holiday and he'll stay hurdling next season, he'll still get in a 0-120 and we'll go quietly - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He got the hang of things last year on soft ground and made a very pleasing comeback at Newton Abbot, although you wouldn't know quite what the form's worth. He's progressive for sure, loves a staying trip and could go to Haydock for a £100,000 handicap hurdle on November 23. 12-11-24

racecards.verdict: It's hard to oppose ART OF DIPLOMACY, who has won his last four chases and is in rampant form.
racecards.betting_forecast: Evs Art Of Diplomacy, 6/5 Catch Him Derry, 13/2 In The Air

racecards.race_name: Bay Tree Cottage Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm, M Ward & D Skelton

prev_owners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm, M&t Ward & D Skelton

runners.comment: Clear second to a subsequent Listed winner on bumper debut; very good hurdling prospect
runners.spotlight: Second foal from a well-connected 2m5f/3m hurdle winner; clear second to a subsequent Listed winner on bumper debut at Chepstow (soft) last autumn; slightly disappointing when only fifth at Sandown (heavy) in February but still looks a very good prospect for novice hurdles this season; had wind surgery in June.

stable_tour.quote: He was second at Chepstow on his debut, staying on really strongly, which took us a little by surprise because his work hadn't been exceptional. He should win a bumper and go on to be a nice novice hurdler next year. 12-11-24

runners.comment: Fairly useful on the Flat at best but recent stable/hurdle debut was very underwhelming
runners.spotlight: 6-28 on the Flat for Richard Hughes, the latest win gained on Polytrack (2m; RPR 81) in April; made very underwhelming stable/hurdle debut when well-held sixth at Fontwell (2m3f, good) 12 days ago.

quotes.race: Athlone Handicap
quotes.quote: Cinnodin didn't do much wrong at Wetherby, he was just a bit unfortunate in the circumstances. We thought this race might cut up and we had it as a plan a month ago. I don't normally like going a mile and three quarters with a three-year-old this early on but he would get two miles tomorrow as he is an out-and-out stayer - Richard Hughes, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's probably his own worst enemy. He keeps running so well and it's hard to find a four-year-old 0-80 2m race, but he'll go and win once he gets one of them with a bit of pace. 18-09-24

runners.comment: Looked promising in both bumpers last season and can make his mark over hurdles soon
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in heavy-ground Irish point in 2023 and returned from long absence with two promising bumper runs (soft/good) towards end of last season, albeit not looking straightforward on latter occasion; can make his mark over hurdles soon.

stable_tour.quote: He's one to keep an eye out for. He ran at Fontwell on Sunday in his first bumper and came second. It was a lovely run after a long time off. We'll probably be looking for something at the end of March and see if we can pick up a bumper somewhere. I'm very pleased with him. 26-02-25

runners.comment: Looked promising in bumpers last season and has big role to play on this hurdling debut
runners.spotlight: Out of a useful staying hurdler; easily won three-runner bumper on debut last November and rebounded from disappointing run in Listed grade with pretty good second at Wincanton (good) in March; upped in trip for this hurdling debut; likely contender.

runners.comment: Ran okay in Chepstow maiden in February but others have more pressing claims
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; bettered inauspicious rules debut when midfield in Chepstow maiden hurdle (2m3f, soft; first-time tongue-tie) in February and can probably improve again in due course but others have more pressing claims here.

runners.comment: Pulled up when 40-1 for recent debut at Worcester (2m, good); moves up in trip here
runners.spotlight: Pulled up when 40-1 for recent debut at Worcester (2m, good); moves up in trip here.

racecards.verdict: Over 18l clear of the third when second to a subsequent Listed bumper winner last autumn, A PAI DE NOM has a good future over jumps.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/5 A Pai De Nom, 2/1 Maldini Milano, 7/1 General Briar, 10/1 Cinnodin, 14/1 Roe And Co, 100/1 Tara Time

racecards.race_name: First Office Print And It Solutions Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Won run-of-the-mill Worcester maiden on stable/hurdle debut; should have more to offer
runners.spotlight: Did not build upon last season's promising bumper debut for Nigel Twiston-Davies but returned from break to make winning stable/hurdle debut in run-of-the-mill Worcester maiden (2m, good) a fortnight ago; faces tougher task under penalty here but should still have more to offer.

runners.comment: Fair form when winning Market Rasen maiden last month; likely to give another good account
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 6yo who came good for Olly Murphy at second attempt, in Market Rasen maiden (2m4f, good) last month; should be okay back at 2m and can handle some underfoot ease; likely to give another good account.

runners.comment: Ready bumper winner last season and commands respect on this hurdling debut
runners.spotlight: Readily won Ffos Las bumper (good to soft) on debut last November and bettered that form when close second in Class 2 race at Warwick (soft) in February; well beaten in Aintree festival Grade 2 in April but in much calmer waters for this hurdle debut and commands respect.

runners.comment: Made low-key rules debut when well-held fourth in weaker C&D maiden than this one
runners.spotlight: 2-9 in Irish points, having signed off in that sphere with front-running win in April (good to yielding), but made low-key rules debut when well-held fourth in weaker C&D maiden than this three weeks ago.

runners.breeder: V & F Berouards, Haras Des Sablonnets &
runners.comment: Fair form when place three times on the Flat in France; makes stable/hurdle debut
runners.spotlight: 0-5 on the Flat in France but ran to fair level when placed in three maidens (1m4f-1m7f) and seems versatile regarding ground; hood removed after one use; worth a glance in the betting before this stable/hurdle debut.

runners.breeder: S C E A De La Perrigne Et Al
runners.comment: Only fourth of seven when warm favourite for debut in February but open to improvement
runners.spotlight: French-bred 4yo; did not convince with her jumping and finished only fourth of seven when warm favourite for junior hurdle at Wetherby (2m, soft) in February but represents a top stable and has obvious scope for improvement.

runners.comment: Tailed off when 150-1 for good-ground Worcester bumper in July
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when 150-1 for good-ground Worcester bumper in July; hood removed for this hurdling debut; cannot be recommended.

racecards.verdict: Fergal O'Brien's HIGHLAND HAVEN looked quite useful in bumpers last season and is taken to make a winning start to her hurdling career.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 La Belle Argentee, 5/2 Joueuse Royale, 11/4 Highland Haven, 7/2 Hill In Brazil, 16/1 Best Night, 40/1 Lynsey Larue, 100/1 Lizzielooe

racecards.race_name: Dartmoor Magazine Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: In good form at Hexham and Cartmel this season; leading contender
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times in June/July, most notably when third in quite valuable female jockeys' race at Cartmel (2m1f, good), and did not run badly when seventh in another competitive Cartmel race last time; leading contender.

runners.breeder: Chapel Lane Farm L T D
runners.comment: Started off for stable with two wins in June; not discredited when third last month
runners.spotlight: Started off for Jennie Candlish with two wins in June (2m/2m6f); had to settle for third when odds-on at Hexham (2m, good; last-flight error not helpful) last month but should cope if the ground remains soft here and remains very much of interest.

stable_tour.quote: He missed last season but he's back working well and is just about ready to start, although he'll probably want a run to take the fizz out of him. He's got a very workable handicap mark over hurdles and I'd like to think he can do some damage off that. 25-10-24

He missed last season but he's back working well and was a promising fifth on his return at Chepstow, a run that should take the fizz out of him. He's got a very workable handicap mark over hurdles and I'd like to think he can do some damage off that. 07-11-24

runners.comment: Irish point winner; makes handicap debut after a layoff but still has potential
runners.spotlight: Joined Dan Skelton after 10l Irish point win in December; began rules career with three low-key hurdle runs (2m-2m3f) at start of this year and makes handicap debut after 205-day absence but still has potential.

runners.comment: Shock novice winner in June but well beaten before last-flight fall on handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Caused 66-1 shock in Worcester (2m, good to soft) in June but was well beaten before last-flight fall there on handicap debut in July and lost all chance when virtually refusing to race on the Flat last week; too risky.

runners.comment: Arrested decline with two seconds in blinkers at Cartmel this summer; major player
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old but arrested his decline with two good seconds in blinkers at Cartmel (2m1f, soft/good) this summer; major player if the headgear continues to aid.

stable_tour.quote: He's a new horse from Highclere who starts next Sunday at Kempton. He's a juvenile hurdler who had a couple of runs in France over hurdles. He had some good Flat form for Jean-Claude Rouget and he goes nicely. 18-10-23

runners.comment: Dual Flat winner in Europe; well held all three hurdle starts; may improve in handicaps
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in Germany in 2022 and in France in 2023; returned from long absence with three very underwhelming hurdle runs (2m-2m1f) for David Pipe in early part of this year but improvement is possible in handicaps.

runners.comment: No form claims; remains unexposed but could be considered only if lively in the betting
runners.spotlight: Last of five on handicap debut at Chepstow in March and pulled up over C&D (good to soft) when back in action three weeks ago; remains unexposed but could be considered only if lively in the betting.

racecards.verdict: The first ever runner at Newton Abbot for Cumbria-based trainer James Moffatt, BOOK OF SECRETS (nap) gets the nod.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Top Guy, 9/4 Dickens, 7/2 Book Of Secrets, 7/1 Saccary, 10/1 Chelsea Annie, 14/1 Jackson's Bay

racecards.race_name: Colin Willcocks Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Second in this race in 2024; makes stable debut after layoff; dangerous if tuned up
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who posted some good efforts over hurdles and fences for Neil King in second half of last year, including a second in this race; makes debut for Ben Pauling after eight-month break but will be a danger to all off current mark if fully tuned up.

quotes.race: 6 Horse Challenge At bet365 Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Onemorefortheroad will have learned plenty from that. He's always schooled fantastically over fences at home. He was a bit brave for his own good there but Jack felt if they'd been going a gear quicker he'd have been more positive on him and he'd be better. That will have done him the power of good. It's very difficult to find the ground for a horse like him this winter so we had to come here as it's the best ground in the country at the moment - Neil King, trainer.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Committed To Safer Gambling Intermediate Hurdle (Listed Race) (A Limited Handicap) (GBB)
quotes.quote: We've always thought the world of Onemorefortheroad, but my horses were badly out of form last season. His homework is so, so good and he's done nothing wrong this season. He wasn't the bravest or most confident at the start and he'll be a better horse when we can come from behind with him. If the ground stays dry I'd like to run him in the Betfair Exchange Trophy at Ascot just before Christmas and a more stamina-testing track will help him - Neil King, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: A really nice young horse who has done precious little wrong in his five runs for us. He won a Newton Abbot bumper in October and his two subsequent runs over hurdles have both been good, finishing third each time. He has always been an excellent work horse at home and I feel he'll be winning over hurdles when we get better ground. He'll not be out again until the ground is more to his liking. I think we'll keep him to 2m for the moment but he'll get further and is one I'd be very disappointed in if he didn't win over hurdles this spring. 20-01-21

prev_owners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith
Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

runners.comment: 2-10 over hurdles after last month's Hexham novice win; has more on his plate here
runners.spotlight: Won Hexham novice (2m4f, good) in first-time visor last month, form that was seemingly well advertised by the runner-up on Wednesday, and is now 2-10 over hurdles; tackles stronger company on this handicap return and needs to prove he can handle a mark this high but won't mind if the ground remains slower than good.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well last time out at Market Rasen when third behind Moonset over hurdles in first-time cheekpieces and has been in good form. He was a good little Flat horse in Ireland when trained by Aidan O'Brien and hopefully now we'll get a couple of wins with him over the summer. If he progresses you don't know where you'll end up with that sort of horse. Once he gets his head in front I'm sure he'll win one or two more hopefully. 26-03-25

runners.comment: Signed off in Ireland with good effort last month; taken very seriously on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 8yo who won a 3m1f chase for Philip Rothwell in June and signed off for him with good 2m6f hurdle run in first-time blinkers last month; now in the care of Dan Skelton (same owners); goes without headgear today but still needs to be taken very seriously.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Prince Zaltar jumps carefully; you get a bit too close and he jumps too far forward. There's a fine line with him, but he finished the race well and got there very easily - Dyland Robinson, jockey.

quotes.race: Navan Racecourse Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Prince Zaltar won at Tramore last year and I thought we were on an upwards curve but anything that could have gone wrong did go wrong. But I thought he ran a cracker at Fairyhouse last day and I couldn't see him out of the four today - Philip Rothwell, trainer

quotes.race: Family Fun Day July 3rd Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Prince Zaltar was very disappointing the last day on heavy ground at Clonmel. He really struggled on it. He jumped better and travelled better on that ground - Philip Rothwell, trainer.

runners.comment: Went up 8lb for winning selling handicap by 17l this month but still considered
runners.spotlight: Flat and dual hurdle winner during busy spell for new stable since the spring, most recently when comfortably drawing 17l clear in selling handicap hurdle at Stratford (2m2f, good) this month; 8lb higher in a stronger race today but still considered.

stable_tour.quote: A nice horse who had kissing spines and has been operated on. There were a few times last year when he went to win his races and his head came up. We were wondering whether he was thinking about the game, but we found out he had severe back problems. He's back in now and working away well and should be ready to run in a month. We think a lot of him and I reckon he could be all right. 03-04-24

runners.comment: Not quite at best on recent outings but now only 1b above May's winning mark
runners.spotlight: Not quite at best on recent outings but now only 1lb higher than for clearcut win at Plumpton (2m4f, good) in May and can't be ruled out.

stable_tour.quote: I'd say he was lucky to win probably the worst novice hurdle to be run this year, at Hereford in November. I think that was good placing by the trainer! He ran okay the other day at Plumpton, when he was fourth, but his mark of 115 is very overrated and he has no chance of winning off that. He'll be a bit of sport around the country and will pick up some prize-money, but in my view he's way too high in the handicap to be winning. 24-12-24

racecards.verdict: A chance is taken on the fitness of ONEMOREFORTHEROAD, who was second in this race a year ago and makes his stable debut today.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Prince Zaltar, 9/4 Mohawk Chief, 3/1 Chillhi, 4/1 Onemorefortheroad, 12/1 D'Jo Dela Barriere

racecards.race_name: RCA Summer Jumps Championship Finale Handicap Chase

runners.comment: Dual chase winner in France but generally disappointing in Britain; now with new stable
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground chase winner in France in spring 2024 but 0-6 and generally disappointing for two different stables in Britain; needs to turn a corner on debut for latest new trainer.

prev_owners.owner: J Bedi, P Ellerby And Partners

runners.comment: 3-3 for new stable this month, having cruised clear on chase return on Tuesday
runners.spotlight: Revitalised 8yo who started off for James Owen with two hurdle wins in refitted visor this month (2m5f/2m4f) and barely came off the bridle when scoring by 12l over fences at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) on Tuesday; has further to go here but has good chance to extend winning spree under 7lb penalty.

quotes.race: Kilmanahan Beginners Chase
quotes.quote: Shantou Lucky ran very well at Leopardstown last time and he is taking to fences. He will stay on the go probably but he might not run on good ground - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well enough at Fairyhouse this month. He'll mix and match between hurdles and fences and hopefully pay his way. 27-10-22

runners.comment: Followed last month's Perth win with good third at Stratford two weeks ago; front-runner
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to fences this year and deserved last month's win at Perth (3m, good); again ran well from the front when third at Stratford (2m6f, good) a fortnight ago and ought to make another bold bid here.

runners.comment: Chase winner for Tim Easterby in 2023-24 season; makes stable debut after long layoff
runners.spotlight: Won over fences at Market Rasen (3m, good to soft) for Tim Easterby on Boxing Day 2023 and was still in good form when last seen in spring 2024; changed hands for £9,000 last summer; entitled to benefit from this outing.

runners.comment: Won a chase here in April and has added two hurdle races to his tally since; respected
runners.spotlight: Won a heavy-ground chase here (2m5f) in April and has added two hurdle races to his tally since, the latter at Worcester (2m7f, good) 19 days ago; usually ridden very patiently; could have untapped potential over this new trip.

runners.comment: Won twice over C&D by clear margins last month; flopped on recent hat-trick attempt
runners.spotlight: Struck form in new visor last month, winning twice over C&D (good/good to soft) by clear margins; needs to bounce back quickly from disappointing run at Fontwell 12 days ago but has possibilities if he responds well to first-time blinkers.

runners.comment: Unplaced all seven starts under rules, most recently when pulled up in April
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at Ffos Las in April, after a wind op, and has now been unplaced all seven starts under rules; needs improvement from somewhere.

racecards.verdict: The ease with which SHANTOU LUCKY cruised clear at Uttoxeter on Tuesday suggested he's still well ahead of the handicapper.
racecards.betting_forecast: 8/15 Shantou Lucky, 7/1 Mcgregors Charge,  Pooroldmackley,  Blackacre, 16/1 Benny Baloo, 20/1 Sheeka Supreme, 33/1 Kate Madrik

racecards.race_name: Randox Maiden Hurdle

runners.comment: Point and bumper winner; good second over 2m6f last time; drop back in trip is of concern
runners.spotlight: Point and bumper winner; left poor form of three maiden hurdle starts behind when upped in trip for handicap debut at Galway last time (2m6f, good), finishing a 5l second behind a subsequent winner; the drop back to this trip is concerning.

runners.comment: Rated 83 on Flat with three wins; big contender if he can transfer his ability to hurdles
runners.spotlight: Rated 83 on the level with three wins to his name at 7.5f-10.5f (including on heavy); now makes hurdling debut and is a big contender if capable of transferring ability to hurdles.

quotes.race: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
quotes.quote: Sea Eagle seemed to relish the ground and there was hardly a blow out of him. The plan was to slot in and it worked out a dream. When he was going well and going through the ground, it was just a matter of keeping him going - Shane Foley, rider.

quotes.race: Abbey Hotel Roscommon Handicap
quotes.quote: He seemed to love the 1m2f, we figured Sea Eagle would handle it and told Jack (Cleary) to ride him cold, just drop him in and be the last to challenge and he did it to a tee. It opens options for us now, trip-wise. He handles soft but the lads said it was on the fast side of good out there - Ger O'Leary, trainer. speaking to Racing TV

runners.breeder: Mrs S M Rogers & Sir Thomas Pilkington
runners.comment: Placed in three runs for Andre Fabre; back from mammoth absence when tailed off last time
runners.spotlight: Favourite when placed for all three French Flat runs for Andre Fabre; made stable debut returned from a mammoth absence in a Tramore maiden hurdle last month when tailed off; can only be watched for now; goes in first-time hood.

runners.comment: PU in two French hurdle starts; bumper run here last time was okay; others preferred
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two hurdle races in France last year; more recent of two bumper runs at this track was satisfactory, second in a five-runner contest; others are more appealing.

runners.comment: Good efforts in defeat on Flat giving rating of 77; of obvious interest given partnership
runners.spotlight: Some decent efforts in defeat on the Flat for Matthew Smith behind respected opposition, giving him a rating of 77; makes stable debut now and of obvious interest given the partnership; trainer also runs Boston Queen.

runners.comment: 69-rated on Flat; keen last time when fading late; return to shorter may suit
runners.spotlight: Rated 69 on the Flat; built on a middling hurdles debut at Listowel to finish an 8l fourth of seven at Down Royal when running too keenly and fading late on (2m4f, good to firm); return to this distance may suit and not totally ruled out.

runners.comment: Big prices for two maiden starts and ran like it; ruled out
runners.spotlight: Big prices for two maiden starts and ran like it; ruled out.

runners.comment: Hasn't been involved in bumper and two maiden hurdle starts and unlikely to do it now
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been involved in bumper and two maiden hurdle starts and unlikely to do it now.

runners.comment: Chased home the favourite at Tramore last month and that form brings her into this
runners.spotlight: May want further but chased home a De Bromhead favourite at Tramore last month and that form brings her into this.

runners.comment: Well beaten in two maiden hurdle starts; trainer also runs Tudorista
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maiden hurdle starts; trainer's other runner, Tudorista, looks more likely.

runners.comment: Unsighted in two maiden hurdle starts; similar story likely
runners.spotlight: Unsighted in two maiden hurdle starts; similar story likely.

runners.comment: Rated 46 on Flat; best of three maiden starts came here when fifth over 2m6f; unlikely
runners.spotlight: Rated 46 on the Flat and big prices for three recent maiden hurdle starts; best of those was at this track in June when a 9.25l fifth (2m6f, yielding to soft); unlikely.

runners.comment: Struggled in two bumper outings; same story in a maiden hurdle over C&D last month
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two bumper outings; same story in a maiden hurdle over C&D last month.

runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners; dam unraced half-sister to Cousin Vinny; check market
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-sister to hurdle winners I Am Spider Man (2m4f/3m1f), Maggie O (2m) and Skin Full (2m); dam unraced sister to useful 3m/3m1f chase winner A Good Skin, half-sister to Champion Bumper and smart 2m/2m2f hurdle winner Cousin Vinny; worth a market check; one of two for trainer.

runners.comment: Pick of three runs was an 8.5l fourth on penultimate; needs to improve
runners.spotlight: Looks the best of trainer's two runners with the pick of his three maiden hurdle runs coming at Ballinrobe on his penultimate start when a 8.5l fourth (good); will need to improve to win.

runners.comment: Tailed off on debut; hard to recommend based on that; makes stable/hurdle debut; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; tailed off on bumper debut and hard to recommend based on that; makes stable debut.

runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners; hooded for debut and probably best watched; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Amarillo Sky (high-class 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase) and Leahs Tricks (2m hurdle); dam unraced sister to useful winners Beg To Differ (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Ashtown Lad (2m3f-3m2f hurdle/chase); hooded for debut and probably best watched.

runners.comment: Good bumper debut but not same level since and tailed off over hurdles last time; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; chased home a clear winner on bumper debut at Clonmel in May but hasn't reproduced that form in four starts since and was tailed off on hurdles debut at Wexford last time.

racecards.verdict: Former Flat performers First Ascent and SEA EAGLE are the most interesting, with preference given to the latter
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 High Court Cave, 4/1 Sea Eagle, 9/2 October Hill, 6/1 First Ascent, 15/2 Scary Askari, 10/1 North Winterfell, 12/1 Tudorista, 18/1 Trixaboutmaisie, 25/1 Mount Hotham, 33/1 Imperial Miss,  Notmiwadi, 40/1 Boston Queen, 50/1 Hello Yes, 66/1 Annie Grey,  Wings Of Eve

racecards.race_name: MB McGrady And Co Chartered Accountants Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Has lost her form over fences but could do better returned to hurdles today
runners.spotlight: Has won a point, maiden hurdle and beginners' chase but lost her form over fences for Gordon Elliott last autumn; no better on debut for this yard but could do better returned to hurdles today.

quotes.race: Connolly's RED MILLS Irish EBF Auction Maiden Hurdle (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: It looked a nice opportunity for Purse Price. She's been consistent so it was nice to get her head in front. She was travelling and jumping well and made it fairly easy so it all went to plan. I'd imagine she can make her mark in handicaps and hopefully there's another race or two in her - Jack Kennedy, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She's a nice mare and will come on plenty for her first start for us at Fairyhouse. 18-10-22

runners.comment: Second in a Bellewstown maiden hurdle in April but hasn't been as good twice since
runners.spotlight: Point and bumper winner was second in a Bellewstown maiden hurdle in April but hasn't been as good twice since while he was below form over fences too; has to return to best.

runners.comment: Good Flat run latest; on the same mark as when fourth at Killarney over hurdles; a player
runners.spotlight: Has been in good form on the Flat and over hurdles since joining this yard, just denied on the level at Navan last time; on the same mark as when fourth at Killarney over hurdles; a player.

quotes.race: Cusack Hotel Group Family Raceday July 13th Handicap
quotes.quote: Arcland's first run for us over hurdles in Downpatrick was very encouraging. He was off a very low mark there today and it was nice to win with him - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

quotes.race: Happy Retirement Eamonn Lloyd Apprentice Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: He'll go jumping after that and should make a nice hurdle horse as he jumps well - trainer Harry Rogers.

runners.comment: Decent 7th at Galway; his poor run there next time was possibly too close; needs best
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in 2024; some decent runs in defeat this year, notably a close seventh at Galway; his poor run there next time was possibly too close; needs best.

quotes.race: Connolly's RED MILLS Irish EBF Auction Maiden Hurdle (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Half A Chance is a nice horse. He probably wants further and the ground seems yielding, I wouldn't have run him on ground any quicker - Shark Hanlon, trainer

runners.comment: Good effort over 2m at Kilbeggan last time; raised 1lb and visor replaces blinkers; chance
runners.spotlight: Five hurdle wins; decent efforts last thrice including a cracking effort over 2m at Kilbeggan last time; raised 1lb and a visor replaces blinkers; a chance.

quotes.race: Book Your Winter Festival Tickets Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Getting Must Meet Cecil's head in front in the first claimer really helped him and he's enjoying it a lot more now. He was passed up the run-in but battled back, it was a very brave effort. He'll stay going now as long as the ground doesn't get too heavy - Cian Collins, trainer.

quotes.race: Bright Motor Group Claiming Hurdle
quotes.quote: Must Meet Cecil loves good ground and to make plenty of use of him, he's just a big galloper - Cian Collins, trainer.

runners.comment: Good run over fences here in July; questions after being pulled-up last two hurdle starts
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins; best effort this season was when just denied over fences at this track in July; not as good since and questions after being pulled-up on last two hurdle starts.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Selling Hurdle
quotes.quote: I'd have been disappointed to lose Wajaaha and delighted we were able to keep him. He's a fun horse, despite having a few issues with his back. He's got lots of ability and carried a nice rating on the Flat. Charles Byrnes, trainer

quotes.race: KilbegganRaces.com Novice Hurdle
quotes.quote: They went real hard up front on that ground. There was no way they were going to get home. It's holding ground. It's nice to sit on these type of horses. Wajaaha jumped and travelled like the best horse - Kevin Brouder, rider

quotes.race: Fedamore Handicap
quotes.quote: He had a great draw today compared to a bad draw the last day at Roscommon. There might be something for him at Galway. The ground was perfect for him today, he handles a slight dig in it - Charles Byrnes, trainer of Wajaaha

quotes.race: Kids Go Racing Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Wujaaha had a nice run at Naas and he has come forward a good bit. He's a bit temperamental but when he gets things his own way he looks good. He'll tip away in handicap hurdles - Charles Byrnes, trainer

runners.breeder: E A R L Elevage Des Loges, Francis Dunn
runners.comment: Best maiden run when beaten 16l at Cork and has a chance if back to that level
runners.spotlight: The best of four maiden hurdle runs when beaten 16l at Cork in July; was poor at Ballinrobe since but is fairly handicapped on that earlier run so a chance if back to that level.

runners.comment: Poor in his last two starts so not easy to fancy back after another absence
runners.spotlight: Flat winner won a Ballinrobe handicap hurdle in May 2024 but has been poor in just two starts since; not easy to fancy back after another absence.

racecards.verdict: ARCLAND was fourth in a stronger Killarney handicap on last hurdle run and was just denied on the Flat last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Must Meet Cecil, 9/4 Arcland, 100/30 No More Silver, 9/1 Purse Price, 10/1 Mic Drop, 11/1 Half A Chance, 20/1 Wajaaha, 28/1 Monty's Way

racecards.race_name: Tennents NI Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Winner on Flat and over hurdles; not a lot of encouragement in recent runs; new stable
runners.spotlight: Winner on the Flat and over hurdles, with his hurdles win coming in a maiden at this track in 2022; not a lot of encouragement in recent runs; new stable needs to spark something.

quotes.race: Racing TV Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Rodney Bay had loads of experience, as well as some form around here, and he deserved to get his day. He was very game and kept trying. The ground is yielding or a little bit slower - JJ Slevin, rider

quotes.race: Racing Again On 27th May Handicap (DIv II)
quotes.quote: Rodney Bay was weak but we've taken our time with him. He's improved a lot and while it was disappointing to get the hike in the weights, the handicapper was right and it was nice to be the right side of the photo today - he's still learning and green but got his nose up. We'll see what the handicapper does now and hopefully he can pick up another one before the end of the summer - Lisa Sheridan, winning part-owner.

runners.comment: Added third hurdles win in July; not at same level in two runs since; down 1lb
runners.spotlight: Added his third hurdles win at Ballinrobe in July (2m1f, good) off 96; not at the same level in two runs following that off 4lb higher; dropped 1lb now, but his form is erratic.

runners.comment: PU in four points; decent fifth in a Wexford maiden hurdle last month; may improve
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in four point-to-point starts; best of four maiden hurdle runs was a 6.5l fifth last month at Wexford at 200-1; may do better in handicaps and wears first-time cheekpieces but others preferred.

runners.comment: Only mild promise in maidens; makes return from long break for new stable; best watched
runners.spotlight: Only mild promise shown in maidens and now makes return from a lengthy break for a new stable; best watched.

runners.comment: Off the mark at Sligo last time with a good front-running ride; up 5lb but can go in again
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her ninth start with a win at Sligo last time (2m1f, good); got a good front-running ride by today's jockey for her win; up 5lb but could go in again.

runners.comment: No wins from 12 but went close over C&D last time; form is in and out but can go well
runners.spotlight: No wins from 12 but went mighty close over C&D (good) last time when denied by a head; his form is in and out, but a repeat of his last run will see him go close off 2lb higher; ground is a slight unknown.

runners.comment: Slight promise in bumpers and maidens; can't be ruled out now entering handicaps
runners.spotlight: Slight promise in bumpers and maidens; can't be ruled out now entering handicaps.

runners.comment: 23-race maiden; not bad on Flat back from a break at Tramore; less promising last time
runners.spotlight: A 23-race maiden whose last run over hurdles came in 2023 over C&D when well beaten off 93; returned from a break with a decent third at Tramore (1m4f, good to yielding); not as good at Galway last time; others more likely.

runners.comment: Beat few rivals in juvenile runs last year and similar story on h'cap debut last time
runners.spotlight: Beat few rivals in juvenile runs last year and similar story on handicap debut last time; hard to recommend.

runners.owner: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership
runners.comment: Winner over hurdles and Flat in April/May; hasn't shown anything subsequently
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Clonmel in April and followed up with a Flat win at Sligo; well held from 8lb higher over hurdles at Roscommon in July; didn't show anything on the Flat subsequently and has questions to answer now.

runners.comment: Three h'cap runs have been over C&D with best of those last time when a 5.25l fourth
runners.spotlight: Midfield finishes in maiden runs; three handicap starts have been over the C&D with the most promising of those coming last time when keeping on for a 5.25l fourth (good); runs off the same mark now so could get involved but likely to find a few with more ability.

runners.comment: Makes handicap debut following poor runs at Bellewstown and Kilbeggan; unlikely
runners.spotlight: Makes handicap debut following poor runs at Bellewstown and Kilbeggan; unlikely.

runners.comment: Tailed off in two starts following long break, although slight improvement last time
runners.spotlight: Yet to make much of an impact under rules with lack of fluency in jumping a consistent issue; tailed off in two starts following 527-day break, although slight improvement last time; others bring more pressing claims.

runners.comment: Winless from 18; tailed off on Flat and over hurdles last two runs; new headgear on
runners.spotlight: Winless from 18 starts, only making the podium once; tailed off on the Flat at Tramore in July and same story over hurdles at Wexford last time; new headgear combination employed now.

runners.comment: Enters h'cap company following comprehensive defeats in maidens; needs to step up on that
runners.spotlight: Enters handicap company following comprehensive defeats in maiden hurdles; would need to step up a lot on form shown to date.

runners.comment: Dual-purpose winner in Britain years ago; not a lot of runs in recent times; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; dual-purpose winner in Britain with last win coming in 2021; not a lot of runs in recent times; slight encouragement in 10l fifth at Wexford last time.

quotes.race: MansionBet Beaten By A Head Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: At some point we'll step into handicap company and see how far Leroy Leroy can go but he's certainly turned into the fun horse we thought he was going to be when we bought him as a Flat horse - Jim Boyle, trainer.

quotes.race: Napoleons Casinos & Restaurants Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Leroy Leroy is a lovely horse, he's suited by this mile - Tim Palin, Middleham Park Racing racing manager.

prev_owners.owner: A G Beatty & Mrs Peter Casey

runners.comment: 28-race maiden on Flat and over hurdles, breakthrough doesn't look imminent; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 28-race maiden on Flat and over hurdles; breakthrough doesn't seem imminent based on return to action at Galway last time.

racecards.verdict: A number make their handicap debut here. Yet, on form to date TIR OG is the pick of them following a good win at Sligo last time
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Tir Og, 11/4 Silent Dawn, 15/2 Centaq, 10/1 Eastern Blue Bird,  You Make Me Smile, 11/1 Only One Plan, 13/1 Blackberry Jack, 18/1 Marvellous Margo, 22/1 King Bob, 25/1 Rodney Bay,  Chasing Crowns,  Lily's Choice, 33/1 Morell River,  Boston Bongo, 40/1 Syosset

racecards.race_name: Charles And Kathleen Caldwell Memorial Rated Novice Hurdle

runners.comment: Winner over 3m2f in June and shapes as though return to that distance would suit best
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles in a handicap at Sligo in June (3m2f, good); decent runs in defeat following that but shapes as though he may prefer a return to the distance of his victory; below his best back at Sligo last time when favourite but well beaten (2m5f, good); others are more appealing and Kennedy prefers Penny Perfect.

runners.comment: Two of three hurdles runs came over C&D; loves the track but ground is a question mark
runners.spotlight: Two of three hurdles victories have come over C&D, including last time when winning off a mark of 109 (good); a pair of decent runs in defeat in prior chasing starts; he's in fine spirits at the moment and loves this track so has a lot going for him but softer conditions would pose a question.

runners.comment: In flying form at the moment with two recent wins; this trip can bring more improvement
runners.spotlight: Flying at the moment after getting his maiden victory at Kilbeggan in July and following that up with a runner-up finish back at that track against a very well handicapped rival; won again in a small-field novice at Tramore last month when upped in trip (2m4f, good); shapes as though he should appreciate this trip.

runners.comment: Excelled when stepped up in trip here last time; Kennedy's choice and improvement likely
runners.spotlight: Excelled after being stepped up in trip here over 2m6f on second start last time (good), winning by 9.5l in the end; the choice of Kennedy with further improvement likely.

runners.breeder: Mme Sarah Faulks & Mr Nigel Faulks
runners.comment: Winner of Galway h'cap last time off 98; may not appreciate softer conditions
runners.spotlight: Winner on fifth hurdle start in a Galway handicap earlier this month (2m6f, good to yielding) off 98; was soundly beaten on penultimate start on soft at Cartmel in June (2m6f), so may not appreciate these conditions.

runners.comment: Six-race maiden but decent runs in defeat; this is the longest trip he has tackled
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden but some decent runs in defeat, including back-to-back runner-up finishes on last two starts; best run was at Roscommon when a lot went wrong (interference, mistake, hung), and he was still a 1.25l second; this is the longest trip he has tackled under rules.

racecards.verdict: Following an easy victory when upped in trip here last time, PENNY PERFECT is the choice of Kennedy and makes most appeal
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Penny Perfect, 4/1 Our Zebo,  Faceman,  Sporting Glance, 7/1 Boston Rajj,  Following Orders

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase

runners.comment: Runner-up in two maiden hurdles over 3m on slow ground last year; trip might be too sharp
runners.spotlight: Point winner was a back-to-back runner-up in maiden hurdles over 3m on slow ground last year; not as good over hurdles or in two chase runs since but is rated 100 so has a chance; trip might be too sharp.

runners.comment: Might have found 2m6f too far on chasing debut at Tramore; needs to find improvement
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden under all codes; was beaten 6l on the Flat last time and previously might have found 2m6f too far on chasing debut at Tramore; needs to find improvement.

runners.comment: Hurdle winner tends to jump left but not ruled out on chase debut
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo handicap hurdle over 2m2f in the summer and put a poor Galway run behind when a respectable fifth at Kilbeggan; tends to jump left but not ruled out on chase debut.

runners.comment: Second over hurdles here last time; should be involved if able to match that on chase bow
runners.spotlight: Maiden after six hurdle starts but put up his best run when second in a 2m2f handicap here last time; should be involved if able to match that on chase debut.

runners.comment: Has run well enough in three chase starts but this trip might be a bit sharp
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-20 over hurdles but posted two solid runs in defeat last twice over 3m; has run well enough in three chase starts but this trip might be a bit sharp.

quotes.race: Your New Tote At Limerick Racecourse Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Rebellious Gale ran well last time and that trip [one mile and seven furlongs] around Kilbeggan was probably sharp enough for him. I'd say the track sharpened him up, though, as he had been lacklustre. He's pulled it all together today and hopefully will progress from that - Peter Carberry, rider.

runners.comment: Has run well at this track and could get involved if her jumping holds up on chasing debut
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-26 over hurdles but not bad runs in defeat last thrice; has run well at this track and could get involved if her jumping holds up on chasing debut.

prev_owners.owner: Exors of the Late Kathy Kenna

runners.comment: Hasn't been at his best here the last twice and has to do better on chasing debut
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-17 over hurdles but has run well in defeat at this track; however, hasn't been as good here the last twice and has to do better on chasing debut.

quotes.race: Kilmolash (Q.R.) Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
quotes.quote: Ar Nos Na Gaoithe is just a small, summery mare and one that will give her owners tremendous fun. I told them we will keep trying and we will have our day - Philip Rothwell, trainer

runners.comment: Maiden has been poor the last twice over fences and has to do much better
runners.spotlight: One of three runners for yard; maiden over hurdles has run reasonably well on some of her four chase starts but has been poor the last twice and has to do much better.

runners.comment: Hasn't progressed in four hurdles starts; could do better in this discipline but needs to
runners.spotlight: Point winner hasn't shown progression in four hurdles starts and was reluctant to start at Kilbeggan last time; could do better in this discipline but needs to.

runners.comment: Hampered when unseating on chase debut here last time; best form is on a sound surface
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times over hurdles, the latest at Tramore last month; was hampered when unseating on chase debut at this track since; best form is on a sound surface so the less rain the better.

runners.owner: G King & Nick Courtney & Joe Murphy & Conor Brett
runners.comment: Poor last time but capable of a good run on chasing bow; the pick of her top yard's trio
runners.spotlight: Point winner is a maiden over hurdles but was pulled-up in a Wexford handicap last time; however, is capable of a good run on chasing debut and looks the pick of her top yard's trio on jockey bookings.

runners.comment: Maiden is capable of a good run on chase debut but best form is on a sound surface.
runners.spotlight: Maiden on the Flat has run well in defeat over hurdles at times, including at this track; capable of a good run on chase debut but best form is on a sound surface.

runners.comment: Solid hurdle run here last time; isn't the yard's first-string but can't be ruled out
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden has run respectably in her three hurdle starts including here last time; isn't the yard's first-string on jockey bookings but can't be ruled out.

racecards.verdict: A chance is taken on PARKGARVE, who ran well over hurdles here last time and his yard does well here too.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Quarry Tiepy, 6/1 Parkgarve,  Stretchemout, 13/2 Mickey Cohen, 7/1 Lightning Legs, 8/1 Navy Guitar, 9/1 Sea Of Doubt, 11/1 Driscolls Hill, 12/1 Rebellious Gale,  Angelsworknovrtime, 14/1 Figurehead, 18/1 Ar Nos Na Gaoithe, 22/1 Gina Moon

racecards.race_name: Peter Fitzpatrick & Sons Estate Agents And Chartered Surveyors Handicap Chase

runners.breeder: G A E C Delorme Freres
runners.comment: Tailed off here over hurdles on stable debut in July; form for Sophie Leech poor as well
runners.spotlight: Useful sort for Paul Nicholls early in his career; last win was March 2023; tailed off in a handicap hurdle here in July on stable debut and form prior for Sophie Leech is not a pretty picture either; one of two for trainer.

quotes.race: Follow @BetVictor On Twitter Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (For The Jacky Upton Trophy) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: There's a three-mile novice handicap chase at Ayr's Scottish National meeting which would suit Flash Collonges. He'd gallop all day and could go another mile at that pace, but he does want good ground - Paul Nicholls, trainer.

quotes.race: bet365 Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was a really good performance. We'd suspected Flash Collonges would be a better horse in a better race and he jumped really nicely. He wandered in the last 50 yards but he won with plenty in hand. Looking at the size of him, he should definitely be a better chaser. He'll probably have another run this season but I don't know where he's going to go. He's a lovely horse to look forward to - Harry Derham, assistant trainer.

quotes.race: MansionBet's Watch And Bet Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Flash Collonges is a nice horse. The race didn't quite suit him the last day, he's a big, raw horse so it was better making most of the running. He'll be a nice horse for the future - Harry Skelton, jockey.

quotes.race: Kenn Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div II)
quotes.quote: That was a pleasant surprise. Flash Collonges is a big baby, he's never been off the pace or away for a gallop or anything. He's taken time to come to himself, he's a great big horse and I thought if he finished fifth or sixth today he'd improve like tenfold for the run. He's still green as grass. Harry said he gave him a slap and he just took off and got to the front a bit soon. I can't stress how much improvement there'll be to come once he learns what he's doing. He's a lovely, lovely horse - Paul Nicholls, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He'd be a possible for the staying handicap chase at Cheltenham's Paddy Power meeting, but the problem is he needs decent ground and it doesn't seem to stop raining at the minute. He's unexposed as a long-distance chaser and when we stepped him up to four miles in the Scottish National in the spring he was going really well when falling, although it was still some way from the finish. Given his preference for good ground, he may well end up back there next spring. He's normally a good jumper. 05-11-23

runners.comment: Regressive over fences and tailed off when last seen; makes stable debut in new headgear
runners.spotlight: Point winner; won two of his three races over hurdles for Kim Bailey; regressive over fences after a solid first run at Exeter in 2023; tailed off at Wetherby on his final start last winter; new headgear tried now for stable debut; trainer also runs Flash Collonges.

quotes.race: Old Boston Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Broomfield Present won nicely at Ffos Las and then at Catterick the ground was plenty soft enough for him and he raced lazily, so we went for the visor. His mark of 122 first time in a handicap looked high enough, but he's won comfortably in the end - Matt Nicholls, assistant to trainer Kim Bailey.

stable_tour.quote: I'm absolutely desperate to run him but he wants soft ground. Until that changes we can't go anywhere, but he's in a really good place and is a really nice stayer in the making. 19-11-24

runners.comment: Off the mark over fences last month; poor off 7lb higher last time; can do better
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences at the sixth attempt at Kilbeggan last month (3m1f, good to yielding); raised 7lb and failed to make an impact at Perth last time (3m, good); she is better than that, so could bounce back.

runners.comment: 3-16 over hurdles but not at same level over fences yet; signs of improvement though
runners.spotlight: Is 3-16 over hurdles but not at the same level over fences as of yet; there are signs of improvement, though and her run at Perth last time was her best so far; could be more improvement in new set of headgear.

quotes.race: Tote Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Jumping Susie is learning how to win now. She is consistent and got a lovely ride. She jumps very well and I'm looking forward to her over fences - John McConnell, traine

prev_owners.owner: Cullentra Racing, J Mooney, Ray Moore Jnr
J A Mooney/Raymond Moore Jnr/Exors of Late Raymond C Moore
J A Mooney & Raymond Moore Jnr & Raymond C Moore

runners.comment: Some decent beginners' chase runs last year but recent hurdling starts not great
runners.spotlight: Got sole win from 18 starts in a maiden hurdle here in 2023; some decent beginners' chase runs last year but recent runs over hurdles leave something to be desired and he was pulled up in a novice chase in January when last seen in this sphere.

stable_tour.quote: He's not the easiest horse to keep sound, but he's got a nice mark and could win a nice handicap somewhere along the line, probably over two and a half miles. 24-10-23

runners.comment: Failed to complete on h'cap chase debut but won at Galway last time; soft ground a worry
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward after failing to complete on handicap chase debut to win at Galway 11 days ago off 100 (2m6f, good to yielding); trainer suggested win could be put down to the better ground that day so he won't want to see it too soft here.

runners.comment: Won on chase debut but tailed off in two following chase starts inc' last time.
runners.spotlight: Won on chase debut when taking a Tramore handicap in November off 95 (2m5f, soft to heavy); tailed off there in May on second chase start and same story here last time (2m2f, good); has been in and out over hurdles in the intervening period but was a winner here in July (2m6f, good); goes on all ground.

quotes.race: Denvir's Of Downpatrick (Q.R.) Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: I wasn't really planning on making the running, but there was no speed and Portarlington went around with his ears pricked and travelled better than he's ever done. I seem to get on with him, which is great - Josh Williamson, jockey. speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: John Thomas McNamara Series (Q.R.) Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Portarlington didn't really come down the hill on quick ground at Limerick but he was suited by today. We might go at Navan on Sunday, it's only 2m but it's a stiff track - Ian Donoghue, trainer.

runners.comment: Poor initial chase runs but much better last time when behind Solly Attwell; 3lb pull now
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle here on second start last year (2m2f, good); first two runs over fences were poor but much better in a Galway handicap (2m6f, good to yielding) last time when a 3.25l fourth behind Solly Attwell; has a 3lb pull at the weights now with that rival.

quotes.race: Downpatrick Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Tell Nobody Nothin travelled great. He had a break over the summer and I'm thrilled with him. We'll take it steady, we'll look for a rated novice with him or something like that - Noel Meade, trainer.

runners.comment: Good placed form in beginners' inc' when pulling clear with winner over C&D last time
runners.spotlight: Is 1-24 over hurdles but good placed form in beginners' in her last two runs at Tipperary and when upped in trip over C&D last time (good) when pulling well clear with the winner; makes handicap chase/stable debut now and on a workable mark.

quotes.race: Thomas A. Murray Bar Roscommon Mares' Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Betty Dutton might not do a lot more this year but I hope she will be a good mare in the spring - Edward O'Grady, trainer.

runners.breeder: S C P Ecurie Pegase & Mme S Mottier
runners.comment: Two-time winner over fences with his best form on soft; big player if he gets his ground
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner over fences with his last win coming at Ayr in March when a comfortable winner off 99 (3m, soft); disappointing in three starts since but all of those have been on good ground while his best form is on softer; big player if the ground stays soft.

runners.comment: Off the mark over timber on penultimate; not at same level over fences but place chance
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over timber on his penultimate start; yet to show the same level of form over fences but may have finished closer without a late mistake when last seen in this sphere; place chance.

prev_owners.owner: Last One For The Road Syndicate
Last One For The Road Syndicate

runners.comment: Tailed off in three starts this year; hard to recommend
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three starts this year (two over fences); was a two-time winner in 2023 but hard to recommend now.

runners.comment: Best effort this year came here in July when a .75l third; not ruled out if back to best
runners.spotlight: Best of his efforts this year came over the C&D (good) in July when a .75l third off 89 (doing well in the circumstances given saddle slipped and rider lost irons); tailed off at Roscommon when last seen over fences and well behind at this track over timber last time; can't be completely discounted if back to his best.

runners.comment: Poor in two chase starts but in good form over hurdles recently inc' win last time
runners.spotlight: Winner of fifth point start; two chasing runs under rules have seen him tailed off and pulled up; nonetheless, there is encouragement to be found in his recent hurdles form with back-to-back runner-up finishes before a wide margin victory at Sligo last time (3m1f, good); he is a huge player if he can bring that form to fences.

racecards.verdict: If the ground stays soft, then LE GRAND VERT (nap) is capable of a bold show and runs off just 1lb higher since his win at Ayr in March
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Solly Attwell, 6/1 Betty Dutton,  Tango Theatre, 15/2 Hardy Diamond,  Tell Nobody Nothin,  Le Grand Vert, 10/1 Jumping Susie, 12/1 Jimli's Cave, 14/1 Midnight Moonshine, 15/1 Portarlington, 18/1 Eagle Terrace, 20/1 Ossifer Hops, 22/1 Broomfield Present, 28/1 Flash Collonges

racecards.race_name: J Murdoch Contracts INH Flat Race

runners.comment: Unsold for Eur15,000 as a 3yo; best watched on debut unless strong in the market
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners incl' Are Ya Right Chief (bumper/2m6f hurdle), Skorcher (bumper/2m6f hurdle) and  Tartan Tiger (3m/3m2f hurdle); Eur19,000 foal was unsold for Eur15,000 as a 3yo; best watched on debut unless strong in the market.

runners.comment: By a leading jumps sire and related to some smart Flat winners; worth a market check
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to German winners incl' Ajalo (1m-1m2f inc 2yo/HK; RPR 111) and Wustenlady (7f/9.7f inc France); dam German 1m3f winner, out of German 7f/1m winner; by a leading jumps sire and related to some smart Flat winners; worth a market check on debut.

runners.comment: Always in rear when beaten 35l on debut at Roscommon and has to improve hugely
runners.spotlight: Was always in rear when beaten 35l on debut at Roscommon and has to improve hugely.

runners.comment: Beaten 6l in third on debut at Sligo; is entitled to improve from that and go closer today
runners.spotlight: Beaten 6l in third on debut at Sligo over 2m2f, rallying after being outpaced; is entitled to improve from that and go closer today.

runners.comment: Hasn't built on her promising debut over C&D last year and others preferred
runners.spotlight: Hasn't built on her promising debut over C&D last year and was pulled-up in a maiden hurdle last time; others preferred.

runners.comment: Beaten a long way on debut at Sligo and can't be fancied on that evidence
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on debut at Sligo and can't be fancied on that evidence.

runners.comment: Gave herself no chance on debut at Cork when pulling too hard; has to settle in the hood
runners.spotlight: Gave herself little chance on debut at Cork when pulling her way into the lead and was a spent force in the straight; has to settle better in the added hood.

runners.breeder: E A R L Haras Du Quesnay
runners.comment: Beaten 6l on debut at Kilbeggan; should have improved from that and go closer today
runners.spotlight: Well-bred French import was hooded for her debut at Kilbeggan and ran a decent race, beaten 6l; should have improved from that and go closer today with hood retained.

runners.comment: By a decent sire and from the family of useful Tropical Lake; respected from top yard
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam well held bumper/2m4f hurdle, half-sister to winners Lord Baddesley (useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Robins Reef (bumper/2m hurdle), from the family of useful 2m hurdle winner Tropical Lake; by a decent sire and is respected from top yard.

racecards.verdict: Mojo Dama and Bells Grove don't set a particularly high bar and maybe the well-bred MINELLA JURY could upstage them on debut
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Moja Dama, 3/1 Tropical Twist, 7/2 Minella Jury,  Bells Grove, 22/1 Double Order, 28/1 Desertmore Charm, 33/1 Katie's Casper, 40/1 Barrys Spirit, 100/1 Loughdoo

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Race

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

prev_owners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.comment: Naas maiden winner; Listed 2nd in July and back to form with close 4th latest; respected
runners.spotlight: Naas maiden winner who hit the crossbar in a Listed race at Tipperary in July and bounced back from a lesser run in a Group 3 when a close fourth at Navan 13 days ago; return to 5f is no issue and ought to go well.

stable_tour.quote: He's got a nice bit of experience. He won nicely over five furlongs at Naas before bumping into Albert Einstein in the Marble Hill. He's a fast colt so he'll go to the Windsor Castle.  13-06-25

runners.comment: Not beaten far in Gr 3 before Down Royal maiden win on first go at 5f; should go well
runners.spotlight: Wasn't beaten all that far in a Curragh Group 3 despite finishing last of five and took advantage of the drop in class back in a Down Royal maiden on his first go at this trip; that didn't look like the deepest maiden but any further progress should see him firmly in the mix.

runners.comment: Bellewstown maiden winner (5f); had competition for lead over 6f since; bit to find
runners.spotlight: Left first two runs behind with a Bellewstown maiden success over 5f; had competition for the lead when last of six at Navan since; return to 5f may help but has a bit to find on these terms.

runners.owner: Kildare Racing Syndicate & Kilmichael Racing Syndicate
runners.comment: Odds on when scoring in 6f maiden here last month and return to 5f fine with stall 2
runners.spotlight: Sent off at just 4-1 for her debut when a 7l fifth of 14 and that form worked out; off the mark with an all-the-way 6f course success last month; second, third and fifth from that race have all gone close without winning since and she gave the impression the return to 5f could be in her favour given that she showed plenty of speed; stall two a positive too.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: I thought as Bear On The Run is a half sister to Dun Na Sead that we'd be coming back for the Legacy over five [furlongs] back here to get a bit of Listed place or something but Shane [Foley, jockey] just thought that she'd be better off going an extra furlong and, looking at the way she was going to the line, she seemed to be going away. She got a little bit of a sore shin after her run at the Curragh [in May] and we decided to give her a six-week break so it looks like it's paid off - Daniel Murphy, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan
runners.comment: 5f Naas winner (form knocked); respected Curragh 6th on Sunday and return to 5f may help
runners.spotlight: 5f winner at Naas on debut (6-1) but that form didn't really work out; shaped like she may appreciate a drop back in trip in the sales race at the Curragh on Sunday when sixth of 16; respected now switched to AW.

quotes.race: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: I was contemplating not running By The Lake because of the five furlongs. I was going to start her at six but I said I'd do the entry and see what rocks up because I just like the track. She's a lovely filly with a good temperament. I've no big plans, we'll just see what she's like when she comes home - Ger Lyons, trainer

runners.comment: Shown ability in pair of 6f Curragh maidens so far but a lot more needed dropped to 5f
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in a pair of 6f Curragh maidens so far without threatening; would need a sizeable step forward on first run at 5f though.

runners.comment: No improvement for drop to 5f last time out and has plenty to find
runners.spotlight: Distant third of four in a Group 3 on debut; hasn't built on that in two maidens since, including when dropped to this trip latest; yard going well with their juveniles but this one has plenty to find.

runners.comment: Went close at Cork last week over 6f when caught on line but this is much tougher
runners.spotlight: Went close to getting off the mark at the sixth attempt over 6f at Cork last week, travelling well and only caught on the line; this looks much tougher though and may find 5f on AW on the sharp side; drawn widest.

runners.comment: Off the mark with 50-1 course win; never involved in French Gr 3 since; needs career best
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt with surprise 50-1 Cork success (5f); never involved in Longchamp Group 3 since despite a good draw; will need a career best here and drawn wide.

prev_owners.owner: Michelle Connolly And Mrs Paul Shanahan

runners.comment: Well held in Leicester maiden when green; bought for 88,000gns in interim; hard to fancy
runners.spotlight: Well held in a Leicester maiden for Alice Haynes when green; bought for 88,000gns in the interim but hard to recommend here.

runners.owner: Derek Murray/Poetry In Motion Syndicate/Patrick McGuinness
runners.comment: Encouraging placed efforts on her first 2 runs but others have achieved a lot more
runners.spotlight: Encouraging placed efforts on her first two runs before a lesser effort latest but others have achieved a lot more.

runners.comment: Has shown ability in trio of maidens but as her rating suggests, she's up against it
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in trio of maidens but as her rating suggests, she's up against it in this field.

racecards.verdict: Several nice prospects with a chance taken on BEAR ON THE RUN who may be hard to catch having shown lots of speed in her 6f course win
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 First Approach, 7/2 Bear On The Run, 9/2 By The Lake, 5/1 Killourney Reigns, 10/1 Methodtomy Madness, 12/1 Ennoble,  Soulful Diva, 16/1 Rosie Frith, 20/1 Prospect Thunder,  Plushy, 40/1 Unauthorized, 50/1 Practical Move

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.breeder: Irish National Stud Mare Syndicate Iv
runners.comment: Son of Kodi Bear; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 82); cheekpieces for debut perhaps not a good sign
runners.spotlight: Son of Kodi Bear; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 82), sister to useful 1m2f-1m4f Flat and 2m hurdle winner Aaddeey, closely related to 1m winner Tajawal, out of useful 7f/1m 2yo winner; cheekpieces for debut perhaps not a good sign and he's already been gelded; has a wide draw to overcome.

runners.comment: Ran well for a long way in barrier trial; down the field at Naas yesterday
runners.spotlight: Bungle Inthejungle colt; closely related to Italian 5f/6f winner Chiaroscuro (including 2yo), half-brother to Swedish 6f-7.5f winner I Am A Fighter; dam 5f winner (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 73), half-sister to useful 6f winner Smokey Laneran; ran well for a long way from the front in a recent barrier trial; down the field at Naas on Thursday.

runners.owner: Total Recall Racing Club/J Fairhurst/N O'Hare/Kabin Racing
runners.comment: Beaten half a length when 3rd at Sligo; hasn't kicked on since; more required
runners.spotlight: Came on from debut when finishing just half a length third in a Sligo maiden; hasn't kicked on twice since; needs improvement.

runners.comment: Tailed off on debut but much improvement when beaten 2l since; extra 1f may help
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut but much improved when beaten roughly two lengths in a Bellewstown maiden finishing third of 13; extra furlong will probably help and yard flying with their 2yos this term.

runners.comment: Disappointing fav on debut; 6f 3rd at Fairyhouse was better; Ballyhane run worth forgiving
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite when down the field in a Leopardstown maiden (7f, soft) on debut but showed more when third in a 6f Fairyhouse maiden; Ballyhane run can be forgiven (drawn wrong side) and remains with potential.

runners.comment: Left impression she would improve from debut when 4th and should be sharp
runners.spotlight: Left the impression she would come on for the experience when outrunning her odds (16-1) to some extent to finish fourth of 14 in Leopardstown maiden; sharper test here may suit and ought to go well despite wide draw.

runners.comment: Improved from debut with a Curragh maiden 4th; form as solid look; needs this for a mark
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of the run on debut but better when staying on for fourth in a Curragh maiden last month; 3l behind re-opposing runner-up White Smoke and that form has a solid look to it with the winner running with credit in a Group 3 since and fifth winning in the interim; needs this for a mark but not ruled out.

runners.comment: Alkumait colt; dam 6f winner; yard 6-18 with 2yos here in last 5 years; worth market check
runners.spotlight: Alkumait colt; dam 6f winner (RPR 79), half-sister to winners Puzzlemaker (UAE 1m), Fort Hughes (US 5.5f 2yo/6f stakes), Spirit Of Scotland (UAE 1m/1m2f) and Astrologist (US 6f 2yo); yard 6-18 with 2yos here in last five seasons; worth a market check.

runners.comment: Similar level in all 3 runs in maidens with RPRs in mid-70s but disappointing on h'cap bow
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in all three starts in maidens with RPRs in mid-70s but disappointing on handicap debut last month; not sure to appreciate drop to 6f and needs career best.

runners.comment: Speedy pedigree and promising run despite a wide trip in a recent barrier trial
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns filly; half-sister to winners Shepherds Way (Listed-placed 5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 95), Brummell (5f/6f including AW; 93), Zonik (1m AW; 77) and Dragon Glory (5f AW 2yo; 71); dam unraced half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Slade Power; runner-up in a barrier trial and had a wider trip than the winner; looked green and hood may help her here; interesting for a yard that do well with their juveniles.

runners.comment: 40,000Eur 2yo; this looks sharp on debut; cheekpieces on and has widest draw
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns filly; cost 40,000Euros as a breeze-up purchase; half-sister to winners Technician (1m2f-15.5f including Group 1 French St Leger; RPR 115),Water Hole (1m including AW 2yo; 103), Caherdaniel (1m2f; 80) and Fastnet Lightning (French 1m-10.5f); dam 9.4f/1m4f (US stakes) winner; this looks sharp on debut; cheekpieces on and has widest draw.

runners.owner: Elaine Shevlin & Cormac Farrell & Ms Denise McCourt
runners.comment: Yard 0-10 with their 2yos this term but plenty to like about her pedigree; drawn wide
runners.spotlight: Sands Of Mali filly; cost 2,000Euros as a yearling; dam placed 5f/6f AW 2yo (RPR 66), half-sister to winners Starlust (5f Grade 1 BC Turf Sprint) and Queen Of Desire (5f Listed), out of 5f Group 3 winner; yard 0-10 with their 2yos this term but plenty to like about her pedigree; drawn wide.

runners.comment: Coulsty filly; yard 7-28 with their 2yos here last five years and warrants a market check
runners.spotlight: Coulsty filly; half-sister to US 1m/8.5f turf winner Buzz Of New York; dam 1m7f winner (RPR 67), half-sister to Italian Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner Avomcic; yard 7-28 with their 2yos here last five years and warrants a market check.

runners.comment: Big step forward from debut when 100-1 2nd and will be involved if improving again
runners.spotlight: Big step forward from debut when 100-1 runner-up in a Curragh maiden last month with Stella Amorosa back in fourth; will be involved if she improves again and handy draw for prominent tactics again; new yard.

runners.comment: Down the field in two maidens so far and likely one for handicaps now; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; down the field in two maidens so far and likely one for handicaps now.

runners.breeder: Haras De Son Altesse L'Aga Khan Scea
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
runners.comment: Only beaten 6l when 33-1 for a Curragh maiden (7f) last month; others preferred; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; only beaten 6l when 33-1 for a Curragh maiden (7f) last month; others preferred.

runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many home in two races so far at huge odds; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; hasn't beaten many home in two races so far at huge odds.

racecards.verdict: There was plenty to like about REAL ENCOUNTER showing up well for a long way on debut and expect improvement second time out
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Real Encounter, 4/1 Stella Amorosa, 5/1 Strait And Narrow, 6/1 Cotai Island, 9/1 California Baby, 12/1 Dancing Saxon, 16/1 Boston Max,  Lamar Canyon, 20/1 Coincidental Glory, 28/1 Nazario, 40/1 Thistle Cross, 66/1 Tenarosa,  All The Girls

racecards.race_name: Friday Night Lights Are Back At Dundalk Handicap

runners.comment: Quiet campaign this year; drops in grade here so respected
runners.spotlight: Some decent turf form last year, winning at the Curragh off 74; quiet campaign this year, most recently midfield at same venue last month; drops in grade here so respected from a good draw.

runners.comment: Trip possibly on the short side and draw hasn't been kind
runners.spotlight: AW winner as a 2yo in Britain last year; yet to win for current yard but good run at the Curragh on penultimate; trip possibly on the short side and draw hasn't been kind.

runners.comment: Upped in trip and blinkers added for AW debut after two poor turf runs
runners.spotlight: Only narrowly denied on handicap debut off 1lb lower mark at Navan in July (5f) but no sort of race both starts since at same trip; upped in trip and blinkers added for AW debut.

runners.comment: AW/handicap debut after definite step forward in Curragh maiden;  should appreciate trip
runners.spotlight: Makes AW/handicap debut after taking a definite step forward in Curragh maiden last month; should appreciate longer trip.

prev_owners.owner: Ben Dalton & Bryan Cooper & C G Bryson

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo making AW/handicap debut; market could be informative
runners.spotlight: Some debut promise a year ago in Punchestown maiden but well held twice since, latest when keen enough at Leopardstown last month on return; switches to AW for handicap debut with obvious scope for more.

runners.comment: C&D maiden second in January but recent claiming run here was poor; more needed
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best runs have been here, notably when second in C&D maiden in January; well below best on last month's yard debut in C&D claimer, however.

runners.comment: 21,000gns buy after Windsor novice second in June; upped in trip on yard/handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Pick of three runs in Britain for James Tate when second in Windsor fillies novice (6f, good-to-firm), having forced it; bought for 21,000gns following month and debuts for new yard here over a new trip; fair mark.

runners.comment: Curragh third encouraging; poor draw so will need to be sharply away
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden is 0-5 on turf but has shown improved form last two starts, here in 6f maiden and and when third in Curragh handicap (off 71) over today's trip, having forced it and hanging left; poor draw so will need to be sharply away.

runners.comment: Needs to recapture summer form at 1m; hood persisted with
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins in 1m handicaps at Bellewstown and Limerick in July but safely held of late; hood tried latest kept on; more needed.

runners.comment: Fine turf form of late makes him a leading player from a good draw back on AW
runners.spotlight: Model of consistency on the turf during the summer, including good third on last two starts at the Curragh; effective at 7f, tongue-tie added to usual hood here and potentially big player from a good draw.

runners.comment: Bellewstown third his standout run this term; unproven at 7f and makes AW debut
runners.spotlight: Useful form in 6f nurseries last season, Bellewstown third his standout run this term; remains unproven at 7f and makes AW debut.

runners.comment: Progressive on turf over 1m of late; hood fitted for AW debut; good draw, player
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicapper on the turf of late, winning over 1m at the Curragh and good third at Bellewstown latest; plenty keen last time and hood now fitted for AW debut; good draw, seems sure to go well.

quotes.race: Robbie Dolan Melbourne Cup Winner Handicap
quotes.quote: She's been a frustrating sort as Tofino has been too keen and missing the break. She travelled really well and, when you let her down, all she does is gallop. The stiff track here suited her. She's always promised a bit more than she has showed, so after getting her head in front hopefully she can improve - Gary Carroll, jockey.

runners.comment: Trips too far of late on turf; previous C&D third gives him claims
runners.spotlight: Better for the new cheekpieces when C&D third at Dundalk in July but both turf runs since underwhelming (1m4f too far latest); return to this trip to suit so don't rule out.

runners.comment: Killarney winner well held twice since; opposable back on AW from a wide draw
runners.spotlight: Best turf form at 1m, second at Limerick before winning off 60 at Killarney on slow ground; well held off 9lb higher mark twice since; opposable back on AW from a wide draw.

runners.comment: C&D winner in March off 72 so can't be ruled out off reduced mark; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; six of nine wins have been here, including over this trip in March off 72; declining turf mark since so can't be ruled out back on favourite surface.

quotes.race: Gift & Art Gallery Dundalk Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Comfort Line ate up this morning, did a half-hour on the beach this morning and we decided we had nothing to lose. He's solid and a good fun horse, when he comes back down to this sort of grade he wins - Ado McGuinness, trainer.

quotes.race: www.navanracecourse.ie Handicap
quotes.quote: The handicapper gave Comfort Line a bit of a chance by dropping him a few pounds. I was a bit disappointed at Galway when he had an outside draw and didn't get a chance. He was coming back to form and, all being well, he'll run in the Cambridgeshire in a couple of days. I used the cheekpieces on him (when second) in the Lincoln earlier this year and had been kind of saving them until he was back where I thought he'd be - Mick Mulvany, trainer.

quotes.race: PWC Kilkenny Handicap
quotes.quote: He's a horse I always thought would make up into a nice horse and I think he'll make a far better four-year-old. He's very big and he's filling into his frame. The Irish Lincoln is the plan for him. You'd be hopeful he'll turn into a Lincoln horse - Mick Mulvany, trainer

quotes.race: LimerickRaces.ie Maiden (Div I)
quotes.quote: Leigh Roche came in the last day and said he just didn't stay the mile and a quarter. I always thought he'd be a nice horse and still think he'll get a mile and two furlongs, but he's still only maturing into a horse. He's a big frame of a horse and the breed of them only get better as they get older. You'd be hoping he'll develop into the type of horse we think he is. We'll probably go to Galway after this for a three-year-old race. I'd blame myself for the last few runs and last time we found out for sure he didn't stay a mile and a quarter yet. Gary [Carroll] said today that he felt the horse was idling in front so hopefully he can keep progressing - Mick Mulvany, trainer of Comfort Line

runners.comment: Touched off here in December off 70, needs to improve on turf form in the interim; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; all six wins over 1m (three here); touched off here in December off 70, needs to improve on turf form in the interim.

quotes.race: Bar One Racing Guaranteed Multiples All Shops Handicap (Div II)
