quotes.quote: It took Rock Etoile a while this season to get back into the swing of things. Andy (Slattery) brought him back from a break and he ran well at Leopardstown a week ago and he was back down in grade here. He was drawn wide today but got out sharply  - Cian Horgan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: No show on yard debut at Galway; may be best watched, especially from this draw; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 1m winner here last winter off 65; no show on recent yard debut at Galway, may be best watched, especially from this draw.

racecards.verdict: A model of consistency on the turf during the summer, SEND HARRY has plenty going for him on his AW return
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Send Harry, 9/2 Tofino, 7/1 Bint Elnadim Star, 9/1 Imperial Dream,  Hugo's Girl, 10/1 Green Icon,  Cali Case,  Land Of The Giants, 12/1 Best Suggestion,  Smoke Them Out, 14/1 Late Night Talking,  Flynn Ryder, 16/1 Oiche Rua,  Spanish Temptress

racecards.race_name: Bar One Better On Football Handicap

runners.comment: AW winner in Britain; drops in grade on AW return so respected
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins over 7f for Jane Chapple-Hyam; has shaped well in two 6f Curragh handicaps since joining this stable; drops in grade on AW return so respected.

stable_tour.quote: She had her first run for me the other day at Doncaster, having come from Phil Kirby. She ran well under a positive ride by Laura Coughlan, a jockey I like, and wasn't beaten far. She's had a few different stables but is a lovely-natured filly. Hopefully we can build on that and I'd be confident of getting a win with her. 26-07-24

runners.comment: Recent Laytown maiden second but this more competitive and not the best of draws
runners.spotlight: Recent Laytown maiden second encouraging but this more competitive and not the best of draws.

runners.comment: Out of sorts at Gowran latest, but earlier C&D second off 5lb lower gives him claims
runners.spotlight: Two Gowran wins this summer but dropped away tamely at same venue on latest off career-high mark; previously went close over C&D off 52 so certainly has to be respected back on this surface.

quotes.race: KCLR 96FM Apprentice Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Sierra De Gredos did it well and Julian [Pietropaolo] gave him a lovely ride. He rode a horse for us recently at Leopardstown and got it spot on. He got the race report and subsequent recommendation spot on. When this race turned up, I asked if Julian could ride and the divide came in our favour. I spotted on the way down that he was almost favourite and the one that worried me was Cherry Pink, on the form. At the weights reversal it put us there or thereabouts so I could see why. We put extra headgear on him today just to focus his mind and everything went to plan - Andy Oliver, trainer.

runners.comment: AW winner in Britain; solid enough turf form of late so has to be considered
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, with four on AW in Britain; solid enough turf form of late so has to be considered.

runners.comment: Five-time winner here down to career-low mark but has never won from such a wide draw
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner here but not for nearly two years, season's best at Laytown recently off this career-low mark; has to be respected but has never won from such a wide draw.

quotes.race: Bar One Racing Guaranteed Multiples All Shops Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: I can't remember Punk Poet being rated so lowly. He could go the winter programme here - Eddie Lynam, trainer.

quotes.race: Buy Annual Membership Today Handicap
quotes.quote: Punk Poet was suited by the track, it is a front-runner's track. He was hanging left the whole way, the straight 7f would suit him even better - Declan McDonogh, rider.

runners.comment: Went closest at Gowran early this month under a positive ride; can't be ruled out
runners.spotlight: Maiden went closest at Gowran early this month over this trip under a positive ride; bit more needed but certainly can't be ruled out on AW return.

runners.comment: Dropped 7lb for two yard runs so can't be readily dismissed from a good draw
runners.spotlight: Dual AW 7f winner in Britain but 0-13 on turf, latest two for current yard; dropped 7lb so can't be readily dismissed from a good draw with top rider booked.

runners.comment: Bellewstown debut winner for yard; 7lb higher but could have more to offer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Leopardstown in the spring and returned from a break to score at first attempt for new yard, coming from well back over this trip at Bellewstown last month; 7lb rise but could have more to offer and wide draw may not be such a negative under hold-up tactics.

runners.comment: Ran no sort of race when last seen here in July from a wide draw (much better draw here)
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come on AW over 7f, including here in March off 55; ran no sort of race when last seen here in July from a wide draw (much better draw here).

quotes.race: Book Online @ DundalkStadium.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Circles had been running well without winning but she is probably a bit stronger now. We left it late but I didn't want to get there early and get caught - Seamie Heffernan, rider.

runners.comment: Just held on over 7f at Laytown recently; remains unproven over this far though
runners.spotlight: Was gaining sixth career win when just holding on in 6f Laytown handicap early this month, a welcome return to form; remains unproven over this far though.

quotes.race: Gilna's Cottage Inn Handicap
quotes.quote: I'd say it was just the beach that brought out the best in Amanirenas and she is well handicapped on last year's form. She seems to like the beach. We took her up to Donabate for a couple of weeks last month and she seemed to love it. We said we would give it a go here in Laytown, so the plan was to give her her last run here and see how we got on. Whether we are tempted to run her again, I don't know - Michael Grassick, trainer.

quotes.race: Leopardstown Members Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: Amanirenas showed she was coming back to herself at Fairyhouse. She is finding her way back and is well handicapped on last year's win. She enjoys it when she is not overcrowded - Michael Grassick, trainer

quotes.race: Ryans Cleaning Events & Recycling Handicap
quotes.quote: Amanirenas is a filly we liked in the spring. She tweaked something in her knee last day and has had a few easy weeks, so I thought she might need the run today. She will come forward and we will try and find a nice fillies handicap - Michael Grassick, trainer

runners.comment: Plenty green but promise on handicap debut at Tipperary; could well have more to offer
runners.spotlight: Closely related to Group 1 winner, Marcel; lightly raced 3yo filly was still plenty green on last month's handicap debut at Tipperary, hanging right throughout and not handling home turn when third; could well have more to offer (half-brother won on AW over 1m).

runners.owner: Patrick M O'Brien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins
runners.comment: Not clear run at Bellewstown latest so C&D winner remains of interest
runners.spotlight: C&D winner back in March off this mark and good third in similar contest in July; not clear run at Bellewstown latest so remains of interest.

runners.comment: Excellent C&D AW debut second last month; 10lb claimer replaces senior rider here
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo on turf made very encouraging AW debut last month over C&D when clear second from a wide draw; senior rider replaced by inexperienced 10lb claimer here but big player if reproducing that run off 2lb lower.

runners.comment: Nothing as yet in handicaps, much reduced mark but still hard to be confident in
runners.spotlight: Nothing as yet in handicaps, soft ground may not have suited latest; much reduced mark but still hard to be confident in.

runners.comment: Three wins here but plummeting turf mark and down to basement mark; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; three wins here (twice in claimers); plummeting turf mark this year and down to basement mark here.

quotes.race: Derek O'Sullivan Memorial Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: Monzoon disappointed at Galway but was much more in his comfort zone here. He ran well in a maiden here last year so he obviously likes this place - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer.

quotes.race: Floodlit Fridays Return Claiming Maiden
quotes.quote: He had two lovely runs in maidens this year at the Curragh and Gowran but then ran on soft ground in handicaps at Cork and Punchestown. He's really a good ground horse and one for the all-weather. We did fancy him today and Mark gave him a great ride. I would love to keep him as he's progressive and still only a three-year-old. I think he'll be better with a bit of time. The key to him is the ground and I think he'll stay a little bit further - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer

runners.comment: Consistent turf form this year, can't be ruled out if getting a run; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; poor strike rate but consistent turf form this year at around 1m; first AW run in a year and not ruled out if getting a run.

stable_tour.quote: He's basically what you see. He won at Hamilton off 53 in early August over 1m1f and then went back over 1m2f at Ayr. He ran okay but I'm tempted to pull him back a furlong or two now and see how he copes. He's rated 60 and is capable off his current mark. 28-08-24

runners.comment: Can only be watched here on belated return and handicap debut; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; soundly beaten in a trio of late-season 2yo maidens last year; can only be watched here on belated return and handicap debut.

racecards.verdict: Plenty with chances but recent Bellewstown winner SAYFA FAD could have still more to offer, despite the wide draw
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Sierra De Gredos, 11/2 Sayfa Fad, 13/2 Punk Poet, 15/2 Akashaa, 9/1 Nammos, 10/1 Drafted,  Amanirenas,  Pause For Peace, 12/1 Yes Oui Si,  Pinball Wizard,  Rising Sky,  Master Garvey, 14/1 Boutella, 16/1 Circles

racecards.race_name: Enjoy Racing TV With A Free Trial Now Apprentice Handicap

runners.comment: All five wins have been here, once over 1m4f; not so good on turf but respected back here
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been here, once over 1m4f; unable to exploit lower turf mark of late but remains of interest back on this surface.

quotes.race: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Apprentice Claiming Race
quotes.quote: Autocrat ran well up here last week. He got into a good rhythm and when I asked him to pick up he did it nicely. He likes it up here and loves the surface - Dan King, rider.

runners.comment: Improver over this trip at the Curragh latest; interesting if he can transfer that to AW
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo went closest on latest of five turf runs, only collared close home over this trip at the Curragh last month, having been clear for much of way; interesting if he can transfer that form to AW (wide draw not ideal).

runners.comment: Triple C&D winner but last month's comeback run on turf was poor; may need more time
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins, latest came off 82; very lightly raced this year, last month's comeback run on turf was poor; may need more time.

runners.comment: Last month's return here leaves her with plenty to find but entitled to come on for it
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden winner late last year and touch off in C&D handicap in March off 76; last month's return here leaves her with plenty to find but entitled to come on for it.

runners.comment: Wins here have been at 1m2f; remains 4lb above highest winning mark
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner has been inconsistent since back-to-back 1m2f wins here last autumn, more recently exploiting reduced mark over same trip last month; didn't land a blow at Naas nine days later and remains 4lb above highest winning mark.

quotes.race: William Hill Lengthen Your Odds Handicap
quotes.quote: Church Mountain has taken a while to come around to our way of thinking but we got him started at Leopardstown a couple of weeks ago and ran a lovely race. It gave us confidence to drop him in because he's won his other races for Denis Hogan making it. He looked like he won snug enough at the line so hopefully he'll be able to go to Galway and be competitive - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer, talking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Limerick Racecourse Handicap
quotes.quote: You'd call stall 18 a bad draw usually, but not with Church Mountain because he doesn't like traffic around him. If he's drawn low and misses the break, it can be hard for him to get going, but Dan is super value for his 7lb claim off those weights. This horse has won twice at Galway and hopefully will go back there. Even though he's a big horse, he seems to like good ground - Denis Hogan, trainer

quotes.race: James P. Cunningham Electrical Handicap
quotes.quote: Church Mountain picked up well and did everything for me today. I'd say he's going to make a lovely dual purpose horse, he won't be a horse until next year - Joey Sheridan, jockey.

quotes.race: Kenny Galway Volvo Handicap
quotes.quote: Church Mountain is one we always liked, but he's a massive horse and still not the finished article. He was green in front but he needed to be there because he is a 1m2f horse. Making the running was the making of him - Denis Hogan, trainer.

runners.comment: Best form here at 1m2f, okay claiming run latest but opposable at this trip
runners.spotlight: 0-18, placed eight times including twice here over 1m2f a year ago; recent Roscommon claiming third bit more encouraging but more needed here and this trip stretches him.

runners.breeder: Arrow Farm & Stud/Miss A Gibson Fleming
runners.comment: AW wins in Britain came at 1m; hard to be confident in on poor turf form of late
runners.spotlight: Two 1m AW wins for Marco Botti; very little to take from turf runs for this yard, slowly away and hanging badly at Roscommon latest; hard to be confident in on AW return.

stable_tour.quote: He was bought at the February sale and has come from Marco Botti. His work has been good since coming to us and we think he's off a workable handicap mark. He seems to be nice. 23-04-25

runners.comment: Dual-purpose sort opposable on belated AW debut
runners.spotlight: 1m3f maiden winner at Killarney two years ago and added a claiming hurdle this May for Gordon Elliott; just okay runs of late on the flat; opposable on belated AW debut.

runners.comment: C&D winner in March off 67; turf form since generally good; player
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win since 2022 when scoring over C&D in March off 67; has remained competitive, bar latest Tramore run; remains of interest.

quotes.race: William Hill Top Price Guarantee Handicap
quotes.quote: I thought I had got up, it was nip and tuck. Gracesolution had a nice run here the last day and she likes to run fresh. She needs cover, I was delighted with the lack of pace and was there ready to pounce. I knew she had a good turn of foot, I was trying to nurse her for as long as I could, she got there and parked a little bit -Donagh O'Connor, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

quotes.race: Lake Isle Of Innisfree Handicap
quotes.quote: Gracesolution was a little bit harder on herself and maybe she has learned to race a bit better now, so you wouldn't mind stepping her up in trip a little bit, maybe up to 1m4f at some point - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer.

runners.comment: Shock recent Tipperary winner over this trip; 5lb higher and rider's claim reduced
runners.spotlight: All wins on the AW over 7f but breakthrough turf win last month came over this trip at Tipperary, coming off the pace under today's rider; 5lb higher now and rider's claim reduced but remains unexposed at this trip.

quotes.race: Bet On The Best Night Out At Dundalk Stadium Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: El Bello was tough all the way to the line. Two horses came alongside him and he battled well. You'd like to think he could get an extra furlong - Dan King, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: A lightly raced gelding, things are really starting to click with him now. He won back-to-back races at Wolverhampton and Newcastle, both over 7f. He went up a fair bit to 80 from an opening mark of 71 - the handicapper actually hiked him 8lb for his last win at Gosforth Park. He relished the stiff 7f there and although he won at Wolverhampton I was not sure the track suited him, and my fears were proved correct last week when he was never happy and although he ran on well at the end it is not his course. We will keep him to stiffer venues in the future and he can win again. 25-05-22

runners.owner: H Redknapp & B Sheerin & D O'Donovan & Kim Coleman

prev_owners.owner: Frank J Lynch & Michael Hartnett & W O'Brien & A M Ryan

runners.comment: First attempt at 1m4f and of limited appeal after recent claiming run; rider going well
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins have been at 7f/1m; first attempt at this trip and of limited appeal after recent claiming run here; rider going well of late.

quotes.race: Parkview Provisions Handicap
quotes.quote: Benavente got a good ride from Joey. He left it late. We might put him in a claimer again. He has paid his way now - Kevin Coleman, trainer

runners.comment: C&D winner last December off 57; plenty more needed on recent turf form
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last December off 57; fair turf form this summer off lower marks but fair bit of improvement needed on AW return off this higher mark.

racecards.verdict: Nicola Burns got a fair tune out of EL BELLO over this trip at Tipperary recently which suggests there could be more to come

racecards.race_name: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Handicap (Div I)

prev_owners.owner: Mrs M Cahill & Mrs Caroline Hendron & Ciaran Kennelly

runners.comment: C&D winner; arrives on back of hurdle success; fair mark but may want further
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who won over timber on her latest outing; looks on a good mark back in this sphere, although probably more effective over a bit further these days.

quotes.race: Bluegrass Horse Feeds Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Hasten Slowly relaxed well through the race and hit the line well. Her jumping was very good and she got the trip well. Hopefully she can win another few - Ben Harvey, rider

quotes.race: Lucan Bloodstock Build For The Boys Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: Hasten Slowly was one I thought would climb last year but she went backwards. I think she will improve going out in trip and she loves fast ground. Hopefully she will go on from this - Noel Meade, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: A lot of our two-year-olds have been running well without winning. We think Hasten Slowly is quite nice and Encosta is very nice as well. Just because they've been running so green, when we run them, I said we'd better run the other one as well. Colin (Keane) said she's still very laid-back, he always thought he had the runner-up covered but then she dawdled about a little late on. She's a nice, strong filly and loves fast ground - Noel Meade, trainer

runners.comment: Lost his way a little on turf lately and needs to prove he stays this far
runners.spotlight: Wolverhampton winner in March but seems to have lost his way a little on turf lately and needs to prove he stays this far.

runners.comment: 0-4 on Flat; well held on h'cap debut last time; unlikely to appreciate drop in trip
runners.spotlight: 0-4 on the Flat and well held on handicap debut last time; unlikely to appreciate this drop in trip.

runners.comment: Gowran winner in August but not as good from revised mark since
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on turf at Gowran in August but not as good from her revised mark twice since; needs to prove she's as effective on AW too.

runners.comment: Laytown winner in 2024 but unproven at this trip and low-key Laytown run latest
runners.spotlight: Laytown winner in 2024 and got her head back in front on turf this year; unproven at this trip though and it was a low-key effort back at Laytown earlier this month.

runners.comment: Improved for h'caps this term and back to form with C&D 2nd latest; cheekpieces now tried
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens last term but confirmed promise of a big run on return when winning over 1m at Gowran in May and bounced back from some lesser efforts when runner-up (promoted from third) over C&D last month; form knocked since but still lightly-raced and first-time cheekpieces may help.

prev_owners.owner: Brian Ellison Racing Club & P Boyle

runners.comment: 4 Newcastle wins and step back in right direction with Navan 6th latest; on a good mark
runners.spotlight: Four wins at Newcastle when trained in Britain and won over this trip at Down Royal last summer; two low-key runs on first two runs for this yard but showed more when sixth of 19 at Navan last time; on a good mark if he can build on that.

quotes.race: Boost Your Acca-Fenwa With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: Explorers Way is a quirky bugger but he's loved by everyone at home and I think that helps him. Ben (Robinson, jockey) knows him and gave him a great ride and while it wasn't the best race, it's great he's won three times - Brian Ellison, trainer.

runners.comment: Low-key form in maidens; gelded since last seen; blinkers on now h'capping
runners.spotlight: Low-key efforts in maidens over 1m; gelded since last seen and blinkers now tried for handicap debut; may get this trip and worth a market check for yard in form despite 164-day lay-off.

runners.comment: 0-10; ran well here in April; didn't build on that; likely best watched after 123 days off
runners.spotlight: 10-race maiden who ran well for a long way when third over C&D in April; didn't build on that in two subsequent runs and was tailed off when last seen at Roscommon; probably best watched after 123 days off.

runners.owner: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & Ms Denise McCourt
runners.comment: 1m4f winner here in October 2024 but lost her way this year and probably best watched
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner here in October 2024 but lost her way this year and probably best watched.

quotes.race: Rockshore Cider Handicap
quotes.quote: We schooled Glyde Ranger over hurdles a couple of months back and that's always been in our mind. He's not overly big but is a horse who is starting to find his bit of confidence. We might go for another race on the Flat first but he'll go hurdling before the better ones come out - Tom McCourt, trainer, talking to Racing TV

runners.comment: Off the mark at 14th attempt last time on turf; needs to prove as effective on AW; up 7lb
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 14th attempt with a 1m2f success at Navan earlier this month; needs to prove she's at least as effective on AW now operating from 7lb higher.

runners.comment: Sole win in 37 starts came over this C&D; poor efforts in trio of starts this year though
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 37 starts came over this C&D; poor efforts in trio of starts this year though and others are more convincing.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs D F O'Rourke & Mrs Josephine Hogan & Denis F Howard

runners.comment: Won at 18th attempt with a 1m6f Navan success 2 runs ago; suspicion this will be too sharp
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 18th attempt with a 1m6f Navan success and ran well for a long way at Down Royal in follow-up bid a fortnight ago; suspicion this trip on AW will be too sharp.

runners.comment: Never threatened in maidens and tailed off on last month's handicap debut; plenty to prove
runners.spotlight: Never threatened in maidens and tailed off on last month's handicap debut; not sure to appreciate further and plenty to prove.

runners.owner: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox
runners.comment: 16-race maiden and better recent form has come over further than this; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 16-race maiden and better recent form has come over further than this.

runners.comment: Modest form over timber; low-key efforts in three Flat runs so far; cheekpieces; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; modest form over timber and low-key efforts in three Flat runs so far; cheekpieces on.

runners.comment: 14-race maiden and only beat one home in 19-runner handicap on yard debut; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 14-race maiden and only beat one home in 19-runner handicap on yard debut.

racecards.verdict: Perhaps the weaker of the divisions and a good chance for EXPLORERS WAY to get his head back in front after signs of a revival latest

racecards.race_name: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Handicap (Div II)

runners.comment: Showed more with C&D 4th before lesser run but should appreciate further on h'cap bow
runners.spotlight: Showed more when fourth in a C&D maiden before a poor run back on turf but should appreciate the step back up in trip on handicap debut and assessor has given her a fair mark.

runners.comment: Course winner; head back in front at Ayr last month and form worked out; respected
runners.spotlight: Course winner who got her head back in front at Ayr last month; 10lb higher but still below her winning mark here from last year and the form of her Ayr success has been boosted by winner going in again since.

runners.breeder: S N C Ecurie De Castillon Et Al
runners.comment: Placed over C&D and AW winner but lost his way a little recently
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D and has won on AW; some promising efforts earlier in the year but has lost his way a little more recently and others look stronger.

quotes.race: Gilna's Cottage Inn (Q.R.) Race
quotes.quote: Cordouan is a good horse. We bought him from Gosden's, he had not run for over 500 days. He's had a few bits of problems but hopefully we have ironed them out and you could see him in premier handicaps next year - Ado McGuinness, trainer.

runners.comment: Sole win over C&D; generally struggled since joining this yard; poor run at Curragh latest
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over C&D in 2023; generally struggled since joining this yard and didn't show anything at the Curragh last month.

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.comment: Placed in two 1m maidens here but struggled in just 3 runs since; nothing in 2 h'caps
runners.spotlight: Placed in two 1m maidens here but just three runs in the last 18 months and has struggled in two handicap outings; new headgear combination but hard to recommend.

runners.comment: Minor promise on 7f debut; showed nothing over shorter since; tough ask; trip may not suit
runners.spotlight: Minor promise on debut here (7f); showed nothing over shorter trips twice since and not sure to appreciate this trip on back of 125 days off.

runners.comment: Sole win came over 1m2f at Navan in July; struggled more recently; yet to show much on AW
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 1m2f at Navan in July but struggled more recently and yet to show much on AW.

runners.comment: 2 of 3 wins over C&D; struggled this year but 7lb below last winning mark back on AW
runners.spotlight: Two of his three wins have come over C&D but struggled in three runs on turf earlier this year and low-key return at Clonmel last week; 7lb below last winning mark at least.

quotes.race: Racing Again June 14th Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: He jumped out well, travelled well and never got hassled for the lead. Barretstown was doing an even enough gallop, took breathers when he could and kept responding. It's good when you have a willing partner like that - Billy Lee, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Poor form in maidens and tailed off on handicap debut last month; needs a lot more
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maidens and tailed off on handicap debut last month; needs dramatic improvement for switch to AW and a longer trip.

runners.comment: Off the mark at Cork over 1m and bounced back from lesser run with 1l defeat latest
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at 17th attempt with a 1m success at Cork and bounced back from a lesser run when beaten 1l in a Gowran handicap earlier this month finishing fourth of 16; yet to show anything on AW in two attempts so far but not one to put a line through.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Caroline Jordan & Mrs Pauline McCourt & Anthony Davitt

runners.comment: 4 AW wins inc' C&D; beaten 6l at Laytown last time; will appreciate step back up in trip
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins including over C&D; beaten 6l at Laytown last time and will appreciate the step back up in trip.

runners.comment: 0-16; placed over C&D but didn't beat a rival on most recent Flat run
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but hit the frame from a higher mark over C&D last season; tailed off on hurdle bow last time and failed to beat a rival on previous Flat outing.

runners.comment: Dual course winner, latest win over 1m4f; hinted at revival over C&D latest; workable mark
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner, the latest win over 1m4f; hinted at a revival over C&D last time and on a workable mark.

prev_owners.owner: Anthony Vaughan & H Jefferies & Mark Phillips

runners.comment: Shown very little on the Flat and struggling in both codes over the last 12 months
runners.spotlight: Shown very little on the Flat and struggling in both codes over the last 12 months.

quotes.race: Thank You To Our Track Team Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
quotes.quote: Noble Hilltop had bits and pieces of form and we thought there was a little bit of improvement in her. We thought she wanted this trip - Edward Buckley, trainer.

runners.comment: Modest form in maidens and no progress on handicap debut earlier this month; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; modest form in maidens and no progress on handicap debut earlier this month.

runners.comment: Promise on debut here and respectable h'cap debut but struggled in claimers since; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; ran with promise on debut here and respectable handicap debut at Tipperary in July but struggled in claimers twice since.

runners.comment: This trip on easy ground could be ideal and was third over C&D back in April; chance
runners.spotlight: Has put up three creditable runs in defeat lately; 7f looked too sharp at Galway and 1m2.5f at Down Royal perhaps too far; this trip on easy ground could be ideal and was third over C&D back in April.

racecards.verdict: After a return to form from GALICIAN GIRL (nap) at Ayr recently when getting the better of a subsequent winner, she can go well again
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Galician Girl, 5/1 Arrumba, 11/2 Tara Power, 6/1 Shining Aitch, 7/1 Barretstown,  Famous Enough, 16/1 Elegant Grand, 20/1 Cordouan,  Limestone Red,  Tartaraghan,  No More Kisses,  Marians Gal, 25/1 Timana, 33/1 Noble Hilltop

racecards.race_name: Celebrate Your Special Occasion At Dundalk Stadium Maiden

runners.comment: Tailed off in Killarney bumper and never sighted after slow start in Tramore Flat maiden
runners.spotlight: Cheaply bought Outstrip gelding; half-brother to 2m hurdle winner Old Gregorian; dam 7.4f-2m Flat (including 2yo/AW; RPR 72) and 2m-2m1f hurdle-chase winner; tailed off in Killarney bumper in May and never sighted after slow start in Tramore Flat maiden two months later.

runners.comment: Very well-bred newcomer cost just 7,500gns at Tattersalls July Sale; belated debut
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega 4yo half-brother to 1m-1m3f winner Lone Eagle (including 2yo Group 3/Irish Derby runner-up; RPR 120); dam 1m-1m4f winner (inc AW/US stakes; 106), out of unraced sister to Oaks winner Reams Of Verse; well-bred newcomer cost just 7,500gns at Tattersalls July Sale; belated debut.

runners.comment: Not clear run when strong-finishing Navan 2nd; not bred for this longer trip
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward on third maiden outing when strong-finishing nose second over 1m at Navan recently, having been denied a clear run; not really bred for this longer trip but definite contender if staying.

runners.breeder: Mrs F S Williams & Newsells Park Stud
runners.comment: Curragh debut promise last month; more needed and draw not ideal
runners.spotlight: 18,000gns foal; third foal out of a 7f winner (RPR 80) and half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Elronaq and 1m4f-1m6f winner Vindolanda; huge odds but some merit in last month's Curragh debut over this trip when beaten little over 9l; draw not the best.

runners.comment: Out of depth in Listed latest; possibly flattered by current rating but definite claims
runners.spotlight: Promise in a trio of maidens; found Listed company too tough 19 days ago but warrants respect back down in class with hood added to usual tongue-tie; possibly flattered by current rating but definite claims.

runners.comment: Half-brother Saxon Warrior; blinkered on belated debut
runners.spotlight: Kingman colt cost 225,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to winners including 2,000 Guineas winner Saxon and Drumroll (1m/1m2f including Group 3; 110); dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (including Group 1; 114); blinkered on belated debut and market will be interesting.

runners.comment: Soundly beaten at big prices in two starts here late last year; gelded since
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at big prices in two starts here late last year; gelded since.

runners.owner: P M Brant & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Derrick Smith
runners.comment: Top connections and obvious respect on belated debut
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt is brother to 1m/1m2f winner Poet Of Life (including US; RPR 98), closely related to winners Yukata (French/US 1m-9.5f inc 2yo/AW; 98) and Kuramata (US 8.5f stakes); dam French 1m2f winner (75), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Beauty Parlour; top connections and obvious respect on belated debut.

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
runners.comment: Fair form in three maidens; yard in top form and improvement quite likely
runners.spotlight: Fair form in three maidens, probably needs to improve a bit to play a major role but yard in top form and has to be respected.

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
runners.comment: Didn't handle track at Tramore; big player on spring maiden form
runners.spotlight: Second runner for his owner and shades Hamadhan on form in the spring although last month's comeback run at Tramore (first-time cheekpieces worn then, now left off; well below his best when reportedly not handling track; back in trip here.

runners.comment: Never sighted at big odds on last month's Bellewstown debut; can only be watched
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to six winners including Lily Rules (6f/7f 2yo; RPR 99) and Overcoming (5.5f-7f inc 2yo/AW/Malaysia; 88); never sighted at big odds on last month's Bellewstown debut; can only be watched.

runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both recent maidens, eligible for a mark after this
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in both recent maidens, eligible for a mark after this.

runners.breeder: Mr Gary Davis & Ms Sharon Davis
runners.comment: C&D debut promise in April and recent Navan run encouraging; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Shaped promisingly when second to an all-the-way winner on debut over C&D in April and not far off that level on recent return when beaten little over 4l at Navan over inadequate 1m; one to consider.

runners.breeder: John D Stuart & More Than Ready Syndicate
runners.comment: Soundly beaten twice in July maidens for Patrick Hayes yard; best watched for now
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten twice in July maidens for Patrick Hayes yard; best watched for now.

runners.comment: Behind throughout in maidens at Leopardstown and the Curragh in early summer; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; behind throughout in maidens at Leopardstown and the Curragh in early summer.

runners.comment: Never sighted on recent Leopardstown debut (1m); upped in trip now; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; American Pharoah colt cost $85,000 as a yearling; half-brother to US 5.5f dirt winner Justify's Magic; dam 1m 2yo Listed winner (RPR 101); not without market support on recent Leopardstown debut (1m) but never sighted; upped in trip.

runners.owner: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan
runners.comment: Never counted on recent debut in decent Roscommon maiden (1m4f); reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; Australia gelding never counted on recent debut in decent Roscommon maiden (1m4f).

racecards.verdict: A maiden in which the Ballydoyle newcomer EDWARD HAMILTON is unlikely to need to be much out of the ordinary to make a winning debut
